en.tldr: First systematic study of token consumption patterns in agentic coding tasks, revealing 1000x more tokens than code reasoning, input token dominance, high variability, weak cost-accuracy correlation, and poor self-prediction by frontier models.
en.motivation: Widespread deployment of AI agents in complex workflows drives rapid token consumption growth, yet systematic understanding of consumption patterns, efficiency, and predictability is lacking.
en.method: Analyze trajectories of eight frontier LLMs on SWE-bench Verified and evaluate models' ability to predict their own token costs before task execution.
en.result: Agentic tasks consume 1000x more tokens than code reasoning; same-task token variation up to 30x; higher token usage does not yield higher accuracy; large efficiency differences across models; human-rated difficulty weakly aligns with actual cost; frontier models fail to predict token usage (correlation up to 0.39) and systematically underestimate costs.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand token economics of AI agents, select efficient models, and be aware of unreliable cost predictions.

idea_spark.idea: Develop a lightweight cost estimator based on task features (e.g., code lines, dependency count) to quickly estimate token consumption and aid user decisions.
idea_spark.risk: Estimator may suffer from limited accuracy due to task stochasticity and requires substantial labeled data for training.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverage the weak alignment between task difficulty and cost to explore cost-aware task decomposition strategies for optimized resource allocation.

papers.title: Breaking Watermarks in the Frequency Domain: A Modulated Diffusion Attack Framework
papers.summary: Digital image watermarking has advanced rapidly for copyright protection of generative AI, yet the comparatively limited progress in watermark attack techniques has broken the attack-defense balance and hindered further advances in the field. In this paper, we propose FMDiffWA, a frequency-domain modulated diffusion framework for watermark attacks. Specifically, we introduce a frequency-domain watermark modulation (FWM) module and incorporate it into the sampling stages both the forward and reverse diffusion processes. This mechanism enables selective modulation of watermark-related frequency components, thereby allowing FMDiffWA to effectively neutralize the invisible watermark signals while preserving the perceptual quality of the attacked watermarked images. To achieve a better trade-off between attack efficacy and visual fidelity, we reformulate the training strategy of conventional diffusion models by augmenting the canonical noise estimation objective with an auxiliary refinement constraint. Comprehensive experiments demonstrate that FMDiffWA achieves superior visual fidelity compared to existing watermark attacks, while exhibiting strong generalization across diverse watermarking schemes.

ai.tldr: 提出一种频域调制扩散攻击框架FMDiffWA，通过选择性调制水印相关频率分量，在保持视觉质量的同时有效中和不可见水印。
ai.motivation: 数字图像水印技术发展迅速，但水印攻击技术进展缓慢，打破了攻防平衡，阻碍了领域进步。
ai.method: 设计频域水印调制模块，集成到扩散模型的前向和反向采样过程，并改进训练策略，增加辅助细化约束。
ai.result: 在多种水印方案上取得优于现有攻击的视觉保真度和强泛化能力。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何利用频域和扩散模型进行高效水印攻击，并平衡攻击效果与图像质量。

idea_spark.idea: 将频域水印调制与扩散模型结合，实现选择性水印移除。
idea_spark.risk: 可能被滥用于非法移除版权水印，需考虑伦理和法律风险。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从信号处理角度出发，利用频域选择性干扰，启发其他基于变换域的防御或攻击方法。

en.tldr: Proposes FMDiffWA, a frequency-domain modulated diffusion attack framework that selectively modulates watermark-related frequency components to neutralize invisible watermarks while preserving visual quality.
en.motivation: Rapid advances in digital image watermarking have outpaced watermark attack techniques, breaking the attack-defense balance and hindering progress.
en.method: Introduces a frequency-domain watermark modulation module integrated into both forward and reverse diffusion sampling, and augments the training objective with an auxiliary refinement constraint.
en.result: Achieves superior visual fidelity and strong generalization across diverse watermarking schemes compared to existing attacks.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to leverage frequency domain and diffusion models for effective watermark attacks while balancing attack efficacy and image quality.

idea_spark.idea: Combining frequency-domain watermark modulation with diffusion models enables selective watermark removal.
idea_spark.risk: May be misused for illegal removal of copyright watermarks, raising ethical and legal concerns.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by signal processing, using frequency-domain selective interference to motivate other transform-domain defense or attack methods.

papers.title: ASPIRE: Make Spectral Graph Collaborative Filtering Great Again via Adaptive Filter Learning
papers.summary: Graph filter design is central to spectral collaborative filtering, yet most existing methods rely on manually tuned hyperparameters rather than fully learnable filters. We show that this challenge stems from a bias in traditional recommendation objectives, which induces a spectral phenomenon termed low-frequency explosion, thereby fundamentally hindering the effective learning of graph filters. To overcome this limitation, we propose a novel adaptive spectral graph collaborative filtering framework (ASPIRE) based on a bi-level optimization objective. Guided by our theoretical analysis, we disentangle the filter learning objective, which in turn leads to excellent recommendation performance, spectral adaptivity, and training stability in practice. Extensive experiments show our learned filters match the performance of carefully engineered task-specific designs. Furthermore, ASPIRE is equally effective in LLM-powered collaborative filtering. Our findings demonstrate that graph filter learning is viable and generalizable, paving the way for more expressive graph neural networks in collaborative filtering.

ai.tldr: 提出ASPIRE框架，通过自适应滤波器学习和双层优化解决谱协同过滤中的低频爆炸问题，实现可学习的图滤波器。
ai.motivation: 现有谱协同过滤方法依赖手动调参的滤波器，无法有效学习，原因是传统推荐目标导致低频爆炸现象。
ai.method: 基于双层优化目标，解耦滤波器学习目标，实现自适应谱图协同过滤。
ai.result: 学习到的滤波器匹配精心设计的任务特定方案，在LLM增强的协同过滤中同样有效。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过可学习滤波器提升谱协同过滤的性能和泛化能力。

idea_spark.idea: 将双层优化与谱图理论结合，设计解耦的滤波器学习目标，避免低频爆炸。
idea_spark.risk: 双层优化可能增加训练复杂度，且对超参数敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从信号处理中的自适应滤波概念获得启发，将其应用于推荐系统的图滤波器设计。

en.tldr: ASPIRE framework addresses low-frequency explosion in spectral collaborative filtering via adaptive filter learning and bi-level optimization, enabling learnable graph filters.
en.motivation: Existing spectral collaborative filtering methods rely on manually tuned filters and fail to learn effectively due to low-frequency explosion caused by traditional recommendation objectives.
en.method: A bi-level optimization objective is used to disentangle filter learning, enabling adaptive spectral graph collaborative filtering.
en.result: Learned filters match task-specific designs and are equally effective in LLM-powered collaborative filtering.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how learnable filters can improve performance and generalization in spectral collaborative filtering.

idea_spark.idea: Combine bi-level optimization with spectral graph theory to design a disentangled filter learning objective that avoids low-frequency explosion.
idea_spark.risk: Bi-level optimization may increase training complexity and be sensitive to hyperparameters.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by adaptive filtering concepts in signal processing, applied to graph filter design for recommendation systems.

papers.title: An LLM-Driven Closed-Loop Autonomous Learning Framework for Robots Facing Uncovered Tasks in Open Environments
papers.summary: Autonomous robots operating in open environments need the ability to continuously handle tasks that are not covered by predefined local methods. However, existing approaches often rely on repeated large-language-model (LLM) interaction for uncovered tasks, and even successful executions or observed successful external behaviors are not always autonomously transformed into reusable local knowledge. In this paper, we propose an LLM-driven closed-loop autonomous learning framework for robots facing uncovered tasks in open environments. The proposed framework first retrieves the local method library to determine whether a reusable solution already exists for the current task or observed event. If no suitable method is found, it triggers an autonomous learning process in which the LLM serves as a high-level reasoning component for task analysis, candidate model selection, data collection planning, and execution or observation strategy organization. The robot then learns from both self-execution and active observation, performs quasi-real-time training and adjustment, and consolidates the validated result into the local method library for future reuse. Through this recurring closed-loop process, the robot gradually converts both execution-derived and observation-derived experience into reusable local capability while reducing future dependence on repeated external LLM interaction. Results show that the proposed framework reduces execution time and LLM dependence in both repeated-task self-execution and observation-driven settings, for example reducing the average total execution time from 7.7772s to 6.7779s and the average number of LLM calls per task from 1.0 to 0.2 in the repeated-task self-execution experiments.

ai.tldr: 提出一种LLM驱动的闭环自主学习框架，使机器人在开放环境中能自主处理未覆盖任务，将执行和观察经验转化为可复用的本地知识，减少对外部LLM的重复依赖。
ai.motivation: 现有方法处理未覆盖任务时频繁依赖LLM交互，且成功经验不能自动转化为可复用的本地知识，导致效率低下。
ai.method: 框架先检索本地方法库，若无匹配则触发LLM进行任务分析、模型选择、数据收集和执行策略规划；机器人通过自执行和主动观察学习，实时训练调整，验证后存入本地库。
ai.result: 重复任务自执行实验中，平均总执行时间从7.7772秒降至6.7779秒，每次任务平均LLM调用次数从1.0降至0.2。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员设计更高效的机器人自主学习系统，减少对LLM的频繁调用，提升任务执行效率。

idea_spark.idea: 结合主动观察与自执行，将外部成功行为也纳入学习来源，扩展了知识获取途径。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖LLM的高层推理质量，若LLM分析错误可能导致学习失败；实时训练可能受计算资源限制。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将LLM作为元控制器，与本地方法库结合，实现持续自主进化，减少对外部接口的依赖。

idea_spark.risk: 依赖LLM的高层推理质量，若LLM分析错误可能导致学习失败；实时训练可能受计算资源限制。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将LLM作为元控制器，与本地方法库结合，实现持续自主进化，减少对外部接口的依赖。

en.tldr: Proposes an LLM-driven closed-loop autonomous learning framework that enables robots to handle uncovered tasks in open environments by converting execution and observation experience into reusable local knowledge, reducing dependence on repeated LLM interaction.
en.motivation: Existing methods rely heavily on repeated LLM interaction for uncovered tasks and fail to autonomously transform successful experiences into reusable local knowledge, leading to inefficiency.
en.method: The framework first checks a local method library; if no solution exists, it triggers LLM for task analysis, model selection, data collection planning, and strategy organization. The robot learns via self-execution and active observation, performs quasi-real-time training, and consolidates validated results into the library.
en.result: In repeated-task self-execution experiments, average total execution time decreased from 7.7772s to 6.7779s, and average LLM calls per task dropped from 1.0 to 0.2.
en.help_to_user: Helps researchers design more efficient robot autonomous learning systems by reducing frequent LLM calls and improving task execution efficiency.

idea_spark.idea: Combining active observation with self-execution incorporates external successful behaviors as learning sources, expanding knowledge acquisition pathways.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on the quality of LLM high-level reasoning; erroneous LLM analysis may lead to learning failures; real-time training may be constrained by computational resources.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires using LLM as a meta-controller integrated with a local method library for continuous autonomous evolution, reducing dependence on external interfaces.

papers.title: The No Barber Principle: Towards Formalised Selection in the Inaccessible Game
papers.summary: The inaccessible game (Lawrence, 2025, 2026) is an information-theoretic dynamical system governed by three information loss axioms, a marginal entropy conservation constraint and maximum entropy dynamics. In this paper we look at selection in the game. Our aim is to develop a selection policy for the game rules based on a minimal set of assumptions. We seek necessary consistency constraints for self-determining dynamical systems. Specifically, we suggest that rules that quantify over distinctions they cannot internally represent risk impredicative-style circularity. Our criterion is motivated by an analogy with Russell's paradox. We formulate a no-barber principle which prohibits dynamics that appeal to external adjudicators or structure lying outside the system.   To motivate our principle we examine Russell's paradox through its structural formalisation as a Lawvere diagonalisation. The marginal-entropy conservation in the game is a nontrivial entropy constraint which prohibits external structure. Through the no-barber principle we argue (i) the classical category FinProb, in which Shannon entropy is characterised, is cartesian and provides canonical diagonal (copying) maps that make Lawvere-style constructions expressible and is structurally incompatible with the no-copying instantiation of the no-barber principle studied here. (ii) the noncommutative category NCFinProb, in which von Neumann entropy is characterised, is symmetric monoidal and lacks canonical copying maps, making it a more natural candidate for the game's internal language.

ai.tldr: 本文提出“无理发师原则”，用于在不可及游戏中制定规则选择策略，避免系统依赖外部结构，并通过范畴论分析FinProb与NCFinProb的适用性。
ai.motivation: 为不可及游戏中的规则选择建立最小假设下的自洽约束，防止自指循环。
ai.method: 类比罗素悖论，形式化为Lawvere对角化，提出无理发师原则，并比较FinProb与NCFinProb范畴的结构。
ai.result: 无理发师原则禁止依赖外部裁判的动态；FinProb因具有复制映射而不符合该原则，NCFinProb因缺乏复制映射而更合适。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解自指系统与信息论约束下的规则设计原则。

idea_spark.inspiration: 从逻辑悖论中提取设计约束，用于构建自洽的动力学系统。

en.tldr: This paper proposes the 'No Barber Principle' for rule selection in the inaccessible game, avoiding reliance on external structure, and analyzes the suitability of FinProb vs NCFinProb via category theory.
en.motivation: To establish minimal consistency constraints for rule selection in the inaccessible game, preventing impredicative circularity.
en.method: Analogize Russell's paradox, formalize via Lawvere diagonalisation, propose the No Barber Principle, and compare FinProb and NCFinProb categories.
en.result: The No Barber Principle prohibits dynamics appealing to external adjudicators; FinProb has copying maps violating the principle, while NCFinProb lacks them and is more suitable.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand rule design principles under self-reference and information-theoretic constraints.

idea_spark.idea: Use the structure of Russell's paradox for rule design in information-theoretic dynamical systems.
idea_spark.risk: The principle may be too strict, excluding legitimate external dependencies.
idea_spark.inspiration: Derive design constraints from logical paradoxes to build self-consistent dynamical systems.

papers.title: CRAFT: Clustered Regression for Adaptive Filtering of Training data
papers.summary: Selecting a small, high-quality subset from a large corpus for fine-tuning is increasingly important as corpora grow to tens of millions of datapoints, making full fine-tuning expensive and often unnecessary. We propose CRAFT (Clustered Regression for Adaptive Filtering of Training data), a vectorization-agnostic selection method for training sequence-to-sequence models. CRAFT decomposes the joint source-target distribution and performs a two-stage selection: (i) match the validation source distribution through proportional budget allocation across k-means clusters, and (ii) within each source cluster, select training pairs whose target embeddings minimize a conditional expected distance derived from the validation target distribution. We prove that proportional cluster allocation bounds the continuous KL divergence between selected and validation distributions, with the residual controlled by cluster diameters. We evaluate CRAFT on English-Hindi translation by selecting training data from 33 million NLLB sentence pairs and fine-tuning mBART via LoRA. CRAFT achieves 43.34 BLEU, outperforming TSDS (41.21) by 2.13 points on the same candidate pool and encoder while completing selection over 40 times faster. With TF-IDF vectorization, the entire pipeline completes in under one minute on CPU. TAROT achieves 45.61 BLEU, but CRAFT completes selection in 26.86 seconds versus TAROT's 75.6 seconds, a 2.8 time speedup.

ai.tldr: 提出CRAFT方法，通过聚类回归自适应过滤训练数据，在英印翻译任务中实现高效高质量微调。
ai.motivation: 大规模语料库（如3300万句对）全量微调成本高且不必要，需要自动选择高质量子集。
ai.method: 两阶段选择：先通过k-means聚类匹配验证集源分布分配预算，再在簇内基于目标嵌入条件期望距离选择训练对。
ai.result: 在英印翻译上BLEU达43.34，比TSDS高2.13分，选择速度比TAROT快2.8倍（26.86秒 vs 75.6秒）。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种快速、向量化无关的训练数据筛选方法，适用于序列到序列模型微调。

idea_spark.idea: 结合聚类与回归条件期望，实现源-目标联合分布的高效匹配。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖验证集分布，若验证集偏差大可能影响选择质量；聚类数需调参。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将数据选择视为分布匹配问题，通过两阶段分解降低计算复杂度。

idea_spark.inspiration: 将数据选择视为分布匹配问题，通过两阶段分解降低计算复杂度。

en.tldr: Proposes CRAFT, a clustered regression method for adaptive training data filtering, achieving efficient and high-quality fine-tuning on English-Hindi translation.
en.motivation: Full fine-tuning on large corpora (e.g., 33M sentence pairs) is expensive and often unnecessary, necessitating automatic selection of high-quality subsets.
en.method: Two-stage selection: first match validation source distribution via k-means clustering with proportional budget allocation, then within each cluster select pairs minimizing conditional expected distance based on target embeddings.
en.result: Achieves 43.34 BLEU on English-Hindi translation, outperforming TSDS by 2.13 points, and completes selection 2.8x faster than TAROT (26.86s vs 75.6s).
en.help_to_user: Provides a fast, vectorization-agnostic data selection method for fine-tuning sequence-to-sequence models.

idea_spark.idea: Combines clustering with conditional expectation regression to efficiently match joint source-target distributions.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on validation distribution; biased validation may degrade selection quality; number of clusters requires tuning.
idea_spark.inspiration: Frames data selection as distribution matching, reducing complexity via two-stage decomposition.

papers.title: Contexts are Never Long Enough: Structured Reasoning for Scalable Question Answering over Long Document Sets
papers.summary: Real-world document question answering is challenging. Analysts must synthesize evidence across multiple documents and different parts of each document. However, any fixed LLM context window can be exceeded as document collections grow. A common workaround is to decompose documents into chunks and assemble answers from chunk-level outputs, but this introduces an aggregation bottleneck: as the number of chunks grows, systems must still combine and reason over an increasingly large body of extracted evidence. We present SLIDERS, a framework for question answering over long document collections through structured reasoning. SLIDERS extracts salient information into a relational database, enabling scalable reasoning over persistent structured state via SQL rather than concatenated text. To make this locally extracted representation globally coherent, SLIDERS introduces a data reconciliation stage that leverages provenance, extraction rationales, and metadata to detect and repair duplicated, inconsistent, and incomplete records. SLIDERS outperforms all baselines on three existing long-context benchmarks, despite all of them fitting within the context window of strong base LLMs, exceeding GPT-4.1 by 6.6 points on average. It also improves over the next best baseline by ~19 and ~32 points on two new benchmarks at 3.9M and 36M tokens, respectively.

ai.tldr: 提出SLIDERS框架，通过将文档信息提取到关系数据库并用SQL推理，解决长文档集问答中的上下文窗口限制和聚合瓶颈问题。
ai.motivation: 现有LLM上下文窗口有限，文档集增大时无法直接处理；分块聚合方法面临证据数量增长导致的推理瓶颈。
ai.method: 将文档中关键信息提取到关系数据库，利用SQL进行可扩展的结构化推理；引入数据协调阶段，通过溯源、提取理由和元数据检测并修复重复、不一致和不完整的记录。
ai.result: 在三个现有长上下文基准上超越所有基线（平均超过GPT-4.1 6.6分），在两个新基准（390万和3600万token）上分别比次优方法高约19和32分。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户处理超长文档集合的问答任务，无需担心上下文窗口限制，并能高效整合多文档证据。

idea_spark.idea: 结合LLM的提取能力与数据库的精确查询，设计自动数据协调机制，确保提取信息的全局一致性。
idea_spark.risk: 提取过程可能丢失细微语义或上下文依赖信息；数据协调规则可能无法覆盖所有异常情况。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受数据库管理系统启发，用持久化结构化状态替代临时文本拼接，实现可扩展推理。

idea_spark.idea: 结合LLM的提取能力与数据库的精确查询，设计自动数据协调机制，确保提取信息的全局一致性。
idea_spark.risk: 提取过程可能丢失细微语义或上下文依赖信息；数据协调规则可能无法覆盖所有异常情况。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受数据库管理系统启发，用持久化结构化状态替代临时文本拼接，实现可扩展推理。

en.tldr: Proposes SLIDERS, a framework that extracts document information into a relational database and reasons via SQL, overcoming context window limits and aggregation bottlenecks for long document set QA.
en.motivation: Fixed LLM context windows cannot handle growing document collections; chunk-based aggregation suffers from a bottleneck as evidence volume increases.
en.method: Extracts salient information into a relational database for scalable SQL-based reasoning; introduces a data reconciliation stage using provenance, extraction rationales, and metadata to detect and repair duplicates, inconsistencies, and incompleteness.
en.result: Outperforms all baselines on three long-context benchmarks (average +6.6 over GPT-4.1) and beats the next best by ~19 and ~32 points on two new benchmarks at 3.9M and 36M tokens.
en.help_to_user: Enables question answering over very long document collections without context window limits, efficiently aggregating evidence across multiple documents.

idea_spark.idea: Combine LLM extraction with database querying, and design automatic data reconciliation to ensure global consistency of extracted information.
idea_spark.risk: Extraction may lose subtle semantics or context-dependent information; reconciliation rules may not cover all anomalies.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by database management systems, using persistent structured state instead of temporary text concatenation for scalable reasoning.

papers.title: Cross-Stage Coherence in Hierarchical Driving VQA: Explicit Baselines and Learned Gated Context Projectors
papers.summary: Graph Visual Question Answering (GVQA) for autonomous driving organizes reasoning into ordered stages, namely Perception, Prediction, and Planning, where planning decisions should remain consistent with the model's own perception. We present a comparative study of cross-stage context passing on DriveLM-nuScenes using two complementary mechanisms. The explicit variant evaluates three prompt-based conditioning strategies on a domain-adapted 4B VLM (Mini-InternVL2-4B-DA-DriveLM) without additional training, reducing NLI contradiction by up to 42.6% and establishing a strong zero-training baseline. The implicit variant introduces gated context projectors, which extract a hidden-state vector from one stage and inject a normalized, gated projection into the next stage's input embeddings. These projectors are jointly trained with stage-specific QLoRA adapters on a general-purpose 8B VLM (InternVL3-8B-Instruct) while updating only approximately 0.5% of parameters. The implicit variant achieves a statistically significant 34% reduction in planning-stage NLI contradiction (bootstrap 95% CIs, p < 0.05) and increases cross-stage entailment by 50%, evaluated with a multilingual NLI classifier to account for mixed-language outputs. Planning language quality also improves (CIDEr +30.3%), but lexical overlap and structural consistency degrade due to the absence of driving-domain pretraining. Since the two variants use different base models, we present them as complementary case studies: explicit context passing provides a strong training-free baseline for surface consistency, while implicit gated projection delivers significant planning-stage semantic gains, suggesting domain adaptation as a plausible next ingredient for full-spectrum improvement.

ai.tldr: 提出显式和隐式两种跨阶段上下文传递机制，用于自动驾驶的分层驾驶VQA，分别降低NLI矛盾42.6%和34%。
ai.motivation: 自动驾驶中的分层VQA（感知-预测-规划）需要保持规划决策与自身感知的一致性，现有方法缺乏对跨阶段一致性的系统研究。
ai.method: 显式方法使用三种基于提示的条件策略，在领域适应的4B VLM上无需额外训练；隐式方法引入门控上下文投影器，从上一阶段提取隐藏状态并注入下一阶段输入嵌入，与阶段特定QLoRA适配器联合训练。
ai.result: 显式方法降低NLI矛盾42.6%，隐式方法降低34%并提升跨阶段蕴含50%，规划语言质量CIDEr提升30.3%，但词汇重叠和结构一致性下降。
ai.help_to_user: 为自动驾驶VQA中的跨阶段一致性提供了两种互补的基线方法，显式方法适合零训练场景，隐式方法适合追求语义增益的场景。

idea_spark.idea: 结合显式提示与隐式投影，设计混合上下文传递机制，在保持表面一致性的同时提升语义一致性。
idea_spark.risk: 隐式方法依赖领域适配，缺乏预训练可能导致词汇和结构退化，需额外领域数据或预训练。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从NLI矛盾减少和蕴含提升中看到，跨阶段一致性可通过轻量级投影器实现，启发设计更高效的上下文融合模块。

zh.motivation: 自动驾驶中的分层VQA（感知-预测-规划）需要保持规划决策与自身感知的一致性，现有方法缺乏对跨阶段一致性的系统研究。

en.tldr: Proposes explicit and implicit cross-stage context passing mechanisms for hierarchical driving VQA, reducing NLI contradiction by 42.6% and 34% respectively.
en.motivation: Hierarchical VQA in autonomous driving (Perception-Prediction-Planning) requires planning decisions to be consistent with the model's own perception, but existing methods lack systematic study of cross-stage consistency.
en.method: Explicit variant uses three prompt-based conditioning strategies on a domain-adapted 4B VLM without additional training; implicit variant introduces gated context projectors that extract hidden states from one stage and inject normalized gated projections into the next stage's input embeddings, jointly trained with stage-specific QLoRA adapters.
en.result: Explicit reduces NLI contradiction by 42.6%; implicit reduces it by 34% and increases cross-stage entailment by 50%, with planning language quality improving (CIDEr +30.3%), but lexical overlap and structural consistency degrade.
en.help_to_user: Provides two complementary baselines for cross-stage consistency in driving VQA: explicit for zero-training scenarios, implicit for semantic gain.

idea_spark.idea: Combine explicit prompting with implicit projection to design a hybrid context passing mechanism that maintains surface consistency while improving semantic consistency.
idea_spark.risk: Implicit method relies on domain adaptation; lack of pretraining may cause lexical and structural degradation, requiring additional domain data or pretraining.
idea_spark.inspiration: The reduction in NLI contradiction and increase in entailment show that cross-stage consistency can be achieved via lightweight projectors, inspiring more efficient context fusion modules.

papers.title: Beyond Chain-of-Thought: Rewrite as a Universal Interface for Generative Multimodal Embeddings
papers.summary: Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have emerged as a promising foundation for universal multimodal embeddings. Recent studies have shown that reasoning-driven generative multimodal embeddings can outperform discriminative embeddings on several embedding tasks. However, Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning tends to generate redundant thinking steps and introduce semantic ambiguity in the summarized answers in broader retrieval scenarios. To address this limitation, we propose Rewrite-driven Multimodal Embedding (RIME), a unified framework that jointly optimizes generation and embedding through a retrieval-friendly rewrite. Meanwhile, we present the Cross-Mode Alignment (CMA) to bridge the generative and discriminative embedding spaces, enabling flexible mutual retrieval to trade off efficiency and accuracy. Based on this, we also introduce Refine Reinforcement Learning (Refine-RL) that treats discriminative embeddings as stable semantic anchors to guide the rewrite optimization. Extensive experiments on MMEB-V2, MRMR and UVRB demonstrate that RIME substantially outperforms prior generative embedding models while significantly reducing the length of thinking.

ai.tldr: 提出RIME框架，用重写替代思维链作为多模态嵌入的通用接口，联合优化生成与嵌入，显著提升检索性能并缩短思考长度。
ai.motivation: 思维链在多模态嵌入中产生冗余思考步骤和语义歧义，限制检索效果。
ai.method: RIME框架包含重写驱动嵌入、跨模态对齐和精炼强化学习，以检索友好的重写统一生成与嵌入空间。
ai.result: 在MMEB-V2、MRMR和UVRB上超越现有生成式嵌入模型，同时大幅减少思考长度。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种更高效、更准确的多模态嵌入方法，适用于跨模态检索等任务。

idea_spark.idea: 利用判别式嵌入作为语义锚点，通过强化学习优化生成过程，实现生成与检索的协同。
idea_spark.risk: 重写可能丢失原始语义细节，需平衡简洁性与信息完整性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将生成模型与检索任务结合，用重写替代推理链，启发更简洁的生成式表示学习。

zh.tldr: 提出RIME框架，用重写替代思维链作为多模态嵌入的通用接口，联合优化生成与嵌入，显著提升检索性能并缩短思考长度。
zh.motivation: 思维链在多模态嵌入中产生冗余思考步骤和语义歧义，限制检索效果。
zh.method: RIME框架包含重写驱动嵌入、跨模态对齐和精炼强化学习，以检索友好的重写统一生成与嵌入空间。
zh.result: 在MMEB-V2、MRMR和UVRB上超越现有生成式嵌入模型，同时大幅减少思考长度。
zh.help_to_user: 提供一种更高效、更准确的多模态嵌入方法，适用于跨模态检索等任务。

idea_spark.idea: 利用判别式嵌入作为语义锚点，通过强化学习优化生成过程，实现生成与检索的协同。
idea_spark.risk: 重写可能丢失原始语义细节，需平衡简洁性与信息完整性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将生成模型与检索任务结合，用重写替代推理链，启发更简洁的生成式表示学习。

en.tldr: Proposes RIME, using rewrite as a universal interface for multimodal embeddings, jointly optimizing generation and embedding, significantly improving retrieval performance while reducing thinking length.
en.motivation: Chain-of-Thought introduces redundant steps and semantic ambiguity in multimodal embeddings, hindering retrieval.
en.method: RIME framework includes rewrite-driven embedding, cross-mode alignment, and refine reinforcement learning to unify generation and embedding via retrieval-friendly rewrite.
en.result: Outperforms prior generative embedding models on MMEB-V2, MRMR, and UVRB, with substantially reduced thinking length.
en.help_to_user: Provides a more efficient and accurate multimodal embedding method suitable for cross-modal retrieval tasks.

idea_spark.idea: Use discriminative embeddings as semantic anchors to guide generation via reinforcement learning, enabling synergy between generation and retrieval.
idea_spark.risk: Rewrite may lose original semantic details; balance between conciseness and information completeness is needed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining generative models with retrieval, replacing reasoning chains with rewrite, inspires more concise generative representation learning.

papers.title: CGC: Compositional Grounded Contrast for Fine-Grained Multi-Image Understanding
papers.summary: Although Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have advanced rapidly, they still face notable challenges in fine-grained multi-image understanding, often exhibiting spatial hallucination, attention leakage, and failures in object constancy. In addition, existing approaches typically rely on expensive human annotations or large-scale chain-of-thought (CoT) data generation. We propose Compositional Grounded Contrast (abbr. CGC), a low-cost full framework for boosting fine-grained multi-image understanding of MLLMs. Built on existing single-image grounding annotations, CGC constructs compositional multi-image training instances through Inter-Image Contrast and Intra-Image Contrast, which introduce semantically decoupled distractor contexts for cross-image discrimination and correlated cross-view samples for object constancy, respectively. CGC further introduces a Rule-Based Spatial Reward within the GRPO framework to improve source-image attribution, spatial alignment, and structured output validity under a Think-before-Grounding paradigm. Experiments show that CGC achieves state-of-the-art results on fine-grained multi-image benchmarks, including MIG-Bench and VLM2-Bench. The learned multi-image understanding capability also transfers to broader multimodal understanding and reasoning tasks, yielding consistent gains over the Qwen3-VL-8B base model on MathVista (+2.90), MuirBench (+2.88), MMStar (+1.93), MMMU (+1.77), and BLINK (+1.69).

ai.tldr: 提出CGC框架，通过组合式对比学习和规则空间奖励，低成本提升多模态大模型的多图像细粒度理解能力。
ai.motivation: 现有MLLM在多图像理解中存在空间幻觉、注意力泄漏和物体恒常性失败，且依赖昂贵的人工标注或大规模思维链数据。
ai.method: 基于单图像标注构建组合式多图像训练实例，包括图像间对比和图像内对比；在GRPO框架中引入基于规则的空间奖励，实现先思考后定位。
ai.result: 在MIG-Bench和VLM2-Bench上达到SOTA，并在MathVista、MuirBench等任务上持续提升Qwen3-VL-8B基线。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种低成本、可迁移的多图像理解增强方法，适用于需要跨图像比较和空间定位的应用。

idea_spark.idea: 利用单图像标注自动生成多图像对比样本，避免人工标注。
idea_spark.risk: 规则奖励可能过于简化，复杂场景下空间约束不足。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将单图像标注通过组合策略扩展为多图像训练数据，降低数据成本。

en.tldr: Proposes CGC, a low-cost framework using compositional contrastive learning and rule-based spatial reward to boost fine-grained multi-image understanding in MLLMs.
en.motivation: Existing MLLMs suffer from spatial hallucination, attention leakage, and object constancy failures in multi-image understanding, and rely on expensive human annotations or large-scale CoT data.
en.method: Constructs compositional multi-image instances from single-image grounding annotations via inter-image and intra-image contrast; introduces a rule-based spatial reward within GRPO for a think-before-grounding paradigm.
en.result: Achieves SOTA on MIG-Bench and VLM2-Bench, and consistently improves Qwen3-VL-8B on MathVista (+2.90), MuirBench (+2.88), MMStar (+1.93), MMMU (+1.77), and BLINK (+1.69).
en.help_to_user: Provides a low-cost, transferable method for enhancing multi-image understanding, useful for cross-image comparison and spatial grounding applications.

idea_spark.idea: Automatically generate multi-image contrastive samples from single-image annotations, avoiding manual labeling.
idea_spark.risk: Rule-based rewards may oversimplify spatial constraints in complex scenes.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverage single-image annotations via compositional strategies to create multi-image training data, reducing data cost.

papers.title: Uni-Encoder Meets Multi-Encoders: Representation Before Fusion for Brain Tumor Segmentation with Missing Modalities
papers.summary: Multimodal MRI offers complementary information for brain tumor segmentation, but clinical scans often lack one or more modalities, which degrades segmentation performance. In this paper, we propose UniME (Uni-Encoder Meets Multi-Encoders), a two-stage heterogeneous method for brain tumor segmentation with missing modalities that reconciles the trade-offs among fine-grained structure capture, cross-modal complementarity modeling, and exploitation of available modalities. The idea is to decouple representation learning from segmentation via a two-stage heterogeneous architecture. Stage 1 pretrains a single ViT Uni-Encoder with masked image modeling to establish a unified representation robust to missing modalities. Stage 2 adds modality-specific CNN Multi-Encoders to extract high-resolution, multi-scale, fine-grained features. We fuse these features with the global representation to produce precise segmentations. Experiments on BraTS 2023 and BraTS 2024 show that UniME outperforms previous methods under incomplete multi-modal scenarios. The code is available at https://github.com/Hooorace-S/UniME

ai.tldr: 提出UniME两阶段异构方法，先通过单ViT统一编码器学习鲁棒表示，再添加多CNN编码器提取细粒度特征，解决多模态MRI缺失下的脑肿瘤分割问题。
ai.motivation: 临床MRI常缺失模态，现有方法在细粒度结构捕获、跨模态互补和可用模态利用间存在权衡。
ai.method: 两阶段异构架构：阶段1用掩码图像建模预训练单ViT统一编码器，得到对缺失模态鲁棒的全局表示；阶段2添加模态特定CNN多编码器提取高分辨率多尺度细粒度特征，与全局表示融合后分割。
ai.result: 在BraTS 2023和BraTS 2024上，UniME在多种模态缺失场景下优于先前方法。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了一种处理缺失模态的通用框架，可应用于其他多模态医学图像分割任务。

idea_spark.idea: 尝试在阶段1引入模态无关的对比学习，进一步增强统一表示的鲁棒性。
idea_spark.risk: 两阶段训练可能增加计算开销，且CNN多编码器对高分辨率特征依赖较大，在低分辨率输入下效果可能下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将全局与局部特征分离学习再融合，启发设计更灵活的多模态架构，例如动态选择编码器数量或类型。

idea_spark.risk: 两阶段训练可能增加计算开销，且CNN多编码器对高分辨率特征依赖较大，在低分辨率输入下效果可能下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将全局与局部特征分离学习再融合，启发设计更灵活的多模态架构，例如动态选择编码器数量或类型。

en.tldr: Proposes UniME, a two-stage heterogeneous method that first pretrains a single ViT Uni-Encoder via masked image modeling for robust representation, then adds modality-specific CNN Multi-Encoders for fine-grained features, addressing brain tumor segmentation with missing modalities.
en.motivation: Clinical MRI often misses modalities; existing methods struggle to balance fine-grained structure capture, cross-modal complementarity, and exploitation of available modalities.
en.method: Two-stage heterogeneous architecture: Stage 1 pretrains a single ViT Uni-Encoder with masked image modeling to learn a unified representation robust to missing modalities; Stage 2 adds modality-specific CNN Multi-Encoders to extract high-resolution multi-scale fine-grained features, which are fused with the global representation for segmentation.
en.result: On BraTS 2023 and BraTS 2024, UniME outperforms previous methods under various missing modality scenarios.
en.help_to_user: Provides a general framework for handling missing modalities, applicable to other multi-modal medical image segmentation tasks.

idea_spark.idea: Try introducing modality-agnostic contrastive learning in Stage 1 to further enhance the robustness of the unified representation.
idea_spark.risk: Two-stage training may increase computational cost, and CNN Multi-Encoders rely heavily on high-resolution features, potentially degrading performance with low-resolution input.
idea_spark.inspiration: Separating global and local feature learning before fusion inspires designing more flexible multi-modal architectures, e.g., dynamically selecting the number or type of encoders.

papers.title: Towards Safe Mobility: A Unified Transportation Foundation Model enabled by Open-Ended Vision-Language Dataset
papers.summary: Urban transportation systems face growing safety challenges that require scalable intelligence for emerging smart mobility infrastructures. While recent advances in foundation models and large-scale multimodal datasets have strengthened perception and reasoning in intelligent transportation systems (ITS), existing research remains largely centered on microscopic autonomous driving (AD), with limited attention to city-scale traffic analysis. In particular, open-ended safety-oriented visual question answering (VQA) and corresponding foundation models for reasoning over heterogeneous roadside camera observations remain underexplored. To address this gap, we introduce the Land Transportation Dataset (LTD), a large-scale open-source vision-language dataset for open-ended reasoning in urban traffic environments. LTD contains 11.6K high-quality VQA pairs collected from heterogeneous roadside cameras, spanning diverse road geometries, traffic participants, illumination conditions, and adverse weather. The dataset integrates three complementary tasks: fine-grained multi-object grounding, multi-image camera selection, and multi-image risk analysis, requiring joint reasoning over minimally correlated views to infer hazardous objects, contributing factors, and risky road directions. To ensure annotation fidelity, we combine multi-model vision-language generation with cross-validation and human-in-the-loop refinement. Building upon LTD, we further propose UniVLT, a transportation foundation model trained via curriculum-based knowledge transfer to unify microscopic AD reasoning and macroscopic traffic analysis within a single architecture. Extensive experiments on LTD and multiple AD benchmarks demonstrate that UniVLT achieves SOTA performance on open-ended reasoning tasks across diverse domains, while exposing limitations of existing foundation models in complex multi-view traffic scenarios.

ai.tldr: 提出面向城市交通安全的开放端视觉语言数据集LTD和统一交通基础模型UniVLT，实现微观自动驾驶与宏观交通分析的统一推理。
ai.motivation: 现有研究集中于微观自动驾驶，缺乏面向城市尺度交通安全的开放端视觉问答数据集和基础模型。
ai.method: 构建包含11.6K高质量VQA对的LTD数据集，覆盖多视角、多天气条件，采用多模型生成+交叉验证+人工修正保证质量；提出UniVLT模型，通过课程式知识迁移统一微观与宏观推理。
ai.result: UniVLT在LTD和多个自动驾驶基准上达到开放端推理任务的最优性能，揭示现有基础模型在多视角复杂场景中的局限。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何利用多视角路侧摄像头数据进行城市交通安全分析，并提供可复用的数据集和模型。

idea_spark.idea: 将多视角路侧摄像头数据与开放端VQA结合，构建城市级交通安全推理系统。
idea_spark.risk: 数据集标注依赖人工修正，扩展至更多城市时成本较高；模型对极端罕见场景的泛化能力待验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从多视角、多任务联合推理角度出发，可启发构建更通用的城市感知与决策基础模型。

zh.tldr: 提出面向城市交通安全的开放端视觉语言数据集LTD和统一交通基础模型UniVLT，实现微观自动驾驶与宏观交通分析的统一推理。

idea_spark.risk: 数据集标注依赖人工修正，扩展至更多城市时成本较高；模型对极端罕见场景的泛化能力待验证。

en.tldr: Proposes LTD, a large-scale open-ended vision-language dataset for urban traffic safety, and UniVLT, a unified transportation foundation model that integrates microscopic AD reasoning with macroscopic traffic analysis via curriculum-based knowledge transfer.
en.motivation: Existing research focuses on microscopic autonomous driving, lacking open-ended VQA datasets and foundation models for city-scale traffic safety analysis using roadside cameras.
en.method: Constructs LTD with 11.6K high-quality VQA pairs from heterogeneous roadside cameras under diverse conditions, using multi-model generation, cross-validation, and human-in-the-loop refinement; proposes UniVLT trained with curriculum-based knowledge transfer to unify micro and macro reasoning.
en.result: UniVLT achieves SOTA on open-ended reasoning tasks on LTD and multiple AD benchmarks, revealing limitations of existing foundation models in complex multi-view traffic scenarios.
en.help_to_user: Helps users leverage multi-view roadside camera data for urban traffic safety analysis, providing a reusable dataset and model.

idea_spark.idea: Combine multi-view roadside camera data with open-ended VQA to build a city-level traffic safety reasoning system.
idea_spark.risk: Dataset annotation relies on human refinement, costly to scale to more cities; model generalization to extreme rare scenarios needs validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: The multi-view, multi-task joint reasoning approach can inspire more general urban perception and decision foundation models.

papers.title: Revisiting Geometric Obfuscation with Dual Convergent Lines for Privacy-Preserving Image Queries in Visual Localization
papers.summary: Privacy-Preserving Image Queries (PPIQ) are an emerging mechanism for cloud-based visual localization, enabling pose estimation from obfuscated features instead of private images or raw keypoints. However, the main approaches for PPIQ, primarily geometry-based and segmentation-based obfuscation, both suffer from vulnerabilities to recent privacy attacks. In particular, a fundamental limitation of geometry-based obfuscation is that the spatial distribution of obfuscated neighboring lines still effectively surrounds the original keypoint location, providing exploitable cues for recovering the original points. We revisit this geometric paradigm and introduce Dual Convergent Lines (DCL), a novel keypoint obfuscation method demonstrating strong resilience against such attack. DCL places two fixed anchors on a central partition line and lifts each keypoint to a line originating from one of them, with the active anchor determined by the keypoint's location. This arrangement invalidates the geometry-recovery attack by making its optimization ill-posed: Neighboring lines either misleadingly converge to one anchor, yielding a trivial solution, or become near-parallel at the partition boundary, yielding an unstable high-variance solution. Both outcomes thwart point recovery. DCL is also compatible with an existing line-based solver, enabling deployment in traditional localization pipelines. Experiments on both indoor and large-scale outdoor datasets demonstrate DCL's robustness against privacy attacks, efficiency, and scalability, while achieving practical localization performance.

ai.tldr: 提出双收敛线（DCL）方法，通过使相邻线误导性汇聚或近乎平行，使几何恢复攻击失效，实现隐私保护的图像查询。
ai.motivation: 现有基于几何和分割的模糊化方法易受隐私攻击，几何模糊化中相邻线的空间分布仍包围原始关键点，提供可恢复线索。
ai.method: 在中央分割线上放置两个固定锚点，每个关键点被提升为从其中一个锚点出发的线，锚点选择由关键点位置决定。
ai.result: DCL使攻击优化病态：相邻线要么误导性汇聚到同一锚点（平凡解），要么在分割边界近乎平行（高方差解），均阻止点恢复。在室内外数据集上验证了鲁棒性、效率和可扩展性。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解一种新的几何模糊化方法，用于保护视觉定位中图像查询的隐私。

idea_spark.idea: 利用锚点使攻击优化病态，可启发设计其他使攻击问题不适定的隐私保护机制。
idea_spark.risk: 若攻击者获知锚点位置或分割线，可能设计针对性攻击；实际部署需考虑锚点保密性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 通过引入固定锚点使攻击优化病态，可启发其他隐私保护场景中利用几何约束使攻击失效的思路。

zh.tldr: 提出双收敛线（DCL）方法，通过使相邻线误导性汇聚或近乎平行，使几何恢复攻击失效，实现隐私保护的图像查询。

idea_spark.risk: 若攻击者获知锚点位置或分割线，可能设计针对性攻击；实际部署需考虑锚点保密性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 通过引入固定锚点使攻击优化病态，可启发其他隐私保护场景中利用几何约束使攻击失效的思路。

en.tldr: Proposes Dual Convergent Lines (DCL) that invalidates geometry-recovery attacks by making neighboring lines either misleadingly converge to one anchor or become near-parallel, achieving privacy-preserving image queries.
en.motivation: Existing geometry-based and segmentation-based obfuscation methods are vulnerable to privacy attacks; the spatial distribution of obfuscated neighboring lines still surrounds the original keypoint, providing exploitable cues.
en.method: Two fixed anchors are placed on a central partition line; each keypoint is lifted to a line originating from one anchor, with the active anchor determined by the keypoint's location.
en.result: DCL makes the attack optimization ill-posed: neighboring lines either misleadingly converge to one anchor (trivial solution) or become near-parallel at the partition boundary (high-variance solution), both thwarting point recovery. Experiments on indoor and outdoor datasets demonstrate robustness, efficiency, and scalability.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand a novel geometric obfuscation method for privacy-preserving image queries in visual localization.

idea_spark.idea: Using anchors to make attack optimization ill-posed can inspire other privacy-preserving mechanisms that render attack problems ill-conditioned.
idea_spark.risk: If attackers learn anchor positions or the partition line, they may design targeted attacks; practical deployment requires anchor secrecy.
idea_spark.inspiration: Introducing fixed anchors to make attack optimization ill-posed can inspire other privacy scenarios where geometric constraints are used to thwart attacks.

papers.title: OccDirector: Language-Guided Behavior and Interaction Generation in 4D Occupancy Space
papers.summary: Generative world models increasingly rely on 4D occupancy for realistic autonomous driving simulation. However, existing generation frameworks depend on rigid geometric conditions (e.g., explicit trajectories) or simplistic attribute-level text, failing to orchestrate complex, sequential multi-agent interactions. To address this semantic-spatiotemporal gap, we propose OccDirector, a pioneering framework that generates 4D occupancy dynamics conditioned solely on natural language. Operating as a ``scenario director'', OccDirector maps natural language scripts into physically plausible voxel dynamics without requiring geometric priors. Technically, it employs a VLM-driven Spatio-Temporal MMDiT equipped with a history-prefix anchoring strategy to ensure long-horizon interaction consistency. Furthermore, we introduce OccInteract-85k, a novel dataset uniquely annotated with multi-level language instructions: ranging from static layouts to intricate multi-agent behaviors, alongside a novel VLM-based evaluation benchmark. Extensive experiments demonstrate that OccDirector achieves state-of-the-art generation quality and unprecedented instruction-following capabilities, successfully shifting the paradigm from appearance synthesis to language-driven behavior orchestration.

ai.tldr: 提出OccDirector框架，仅用自然语言即可生成4D占据空间的动态场景，实现多智能体交互的语义-时空控制。
ai.motivation: 现有生成框架依赖刚性几何条件或简单属性文本，无法编排复杂的多智能体顺序交互。
ai.method: 采用VLM驱动的时空MMDiT，结合历史前缀锚定策略，将自然语言脚本映射为物理合理的体素动态。
ai.result: 在生成质量和指令跟随能力上达到最优，成功从外观合成转向语言驱动的行为编排。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员仅通过自然语言描述即可生成自动驾驶仿真中的复杂交互场景。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将历史前缀锚定策略用于长视频生成中的时序一致性保持。
idea_spark.risk: 语言指令的歧义性可能导致生成结果不符合预期，需要更精细的语义解析。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将VLM作为场景导演，用自然语言直接控制底层物理动态，启发新的交互式仿真范式。

zh.tldr: 提出OccDirector框架，仅用自然语言即可生成4D占据空间的动态场景，实现多智能体交互的语义-时空控制。
zh.motivation: 现有生成框架依赖刚性几何条件或简单属性文本，无法编排复杂的多智能体顺序交互。
zh.method: 采用VLM驱动的时空MMDiT，结合历史前缀锚定策略，将自然语言脚本映射为物理合理的体素动态。
zh.result: 在生成质量和指令跟随能力上达到最优，成功从外观合成转向语言驱动的行为编排。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员仅通过自然语言描述即可生成自动驾驶仿真中的复杂交互场景。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将历史前缀锚定策略用于长视频生成中的时序一致性保持。
idea_spark.risk: 语言指令的歧义性可能导致生成结果不符合预期，需要更精细的语义解析。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将VLM作为场景导演，用自然语言直接控制底层物理动态，启发新的交互式仿真范式。

en.tldr: OccDirector generates 4D occupancy dynamics from natural language, enabling language-guided multi-agent interaction in autonomous driving simulation.
en.motivation: Existing frameworks rely on rigid geometric conditions or simple attribute-level text, failing to orchestrate complex sequential multi-agent interactions.
en.method: A VLM-driven Spatio-Temporal MMDiT with history-prefix anchoring maps natural language scripts to physically plausible voxel dynamics.
en.result: Achieves state-of-the-art generation quality and instruction-following, shifting from appearance synthesis to language-driven behavior orchestration.
en.help_to_user: Enables researchers to generate complex interactive driving scenarios using only natural language descriptions.

idea_spark.idea: Explore using history-prefix anchoring for temporal consistency in long video generation.
idea_spark.risk: Ambiguity in language instructions may lead to unexpected generation results, requiring finer semantic parsing.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using VLM as a scene director to directly control underlying physical dynamics via natural language inspires new interactive simulation paradigms.

papers.title: DocPrune:Efficient Document Question Answering via Background, Question, and Comprehension-aware Token Pruning
papers.summary: Recent advances in vision-language models have demonstrated remarkable performance across diverse multi-modal tasks, including document question answering that leverages structured visual cues from text, tables, and figures. However, unlike natural images, document images contain large backgrounds and only sparse supporting evidence, leading to the inefficient consumption of substantial computational resources, especially for long documents. We observe that existing token-reduction methods for natural images and videos fall short in utilizing the structural sparsity unique to documents. To address this, we propose DocPrune, a training-free and progressive document token pruning framework designed for efficient long-document understanding. The proposed method preserves only the essential tokens for the task while removing unnecessary ones, such as background or question-irrelevant tokens. Moreover, it automatically selects the appropriate layers to initiate token pruning based on the model's level of comprehension. Our experiments on the M3DocRAG show that DocPrune improves throughput by 3.0x and 3.3x in the encoder and decoder, respectively, while boosting the F1 score by +1.0, achieving both higher accuracy and efficiency without any additional training.

ai.tldr: 提出DocPrune，一种无需训练、渐进式的文档令牌剪枝框架，通过移除背景和无关令牌，在长文档问答中提升3倍吞吐量并提高F1分数。
ai.motivation: 文档图像包含大量背景和稀疏支持证据，现有令牌缩减方法未能利用文档的结构稀疏性，导致计算资源浪费。
ai.method: 基于背景、问题和理解感知的渐进式令牌剪枝，自动选择合适层开始剪枝，保留任务关键令牌。
ai.result: 在M3DocRAG上，编码器吞吐量提升3.0倍，解码器提升3.3倍，F1分数提升1.0，无需额外训练。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户高效处理长文档问答任务，减少计算开销同时保持或提升准确性。

idea_spark.idea: 结合文档布局信息（如表格、标题）设计更细粒度的令牌重要性评分，或引入自适应剪枝阈值。
idea_spark.risk: 过度剪枝可能丢失关键证据，需平衡效率与召回率；对复杂布局文档的泛化性需验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从文档的结构稀疏性出发，启发其他领域（如代码、网页）利用固有结构进行高效推理。

zh.motivation: 文档图像包含大量背景和稀疏支持证据，现有令牌缩减方法未能利用文档的结构稀疏性，导致计算资源浪费。
zh.method: 基于背景、问题和理解感知的渐进式令牌剪枝，自动选择合适层开始剪枝，保留任务关键令牌。
zh.result: 在M3DocRAG上，编码器吞吐量提升3.0倍，解码器提升3.3倍，F1分数提升1.0，无需额外训练。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户高效处理长文档问答任务，减少计算开销同时保持或提升准确性。

idea_spark.risk: 过度剪枝可能丢失关键证据，需平衡效率与召回率；对复杂布局文档的泛化性需验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从文档的结构稀疏性出发，启发其他领域（如代码、网页）利用固有结构进行高效推理。

en.tldr: Proposes DocPrune, a training-free progressive token pruning framework for document QA, removing background and irrelevant tokens to achieve 3x throughput improvement and +1.0 F1 gain on M3DocRAG.
en.motivation: Document images have large backgrounds and sparse evidence; existing token reduction methods fail to exploit document structural sparsity, causing computational waste.
en.method: Progressive token pruning based on background, question, and comprehension awareness, automatically selecting layers to start pruning and retaining task-critical tokens.
en.result: On M3DocRAG, encoder throughput improved 3.0x, decoder 3.3x, and F1 score increased by 1.0, all without additional training.
en.help_to_user: Helps users efficiently handle long-document QA tasks, reducing computational cost while maintaining or improving accuracy.

idea_spark.idea: Design finer-grained token importance scores using document layout (e.g., tables, headings) or introduce adaptive pruning thresholds.
idea_spark.risk: Over-pruning may lose critical evidence; balance between efficiency and recall; generalization to complex layouts needs validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leveraging structural sparsity in documents inspires efficient inference in other domains like code or web pages with inherent structure.

papers.title: Outcome Rewards Do Not Guarantee Verifiable or Causally Important Reasoning
papers.summary: Reinforcement Learning from Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) on chain-of-thought reasoning has become a standard part of language model post-training recipes. A common assumption is that the reasoning chains trained through RLVR reliably represent how a model gets to its answer. In this paper, we develop two metrics for critically examining this assumption: Causal Importance of Reasoning (CIR), which measures the cumulative effect of reasoning tokens on the final answer, and Sufficiency of Reasoning (SR), which measures whether a verifier can arrive at an unambiguous answer based on the reasoning alone. Through experiments with the Qwen2.5 model series and ReasoningGym tasks, we find that: (1) while RLVR does improve task accuracy, it does not reliably improve CIR or SR, calling the role of reasoning in model performance into question; (2) a small amount of SFT before RLVR can be a remedy for low CIR and SR; and (3) CIR and SR can be improved even without SFT by applying auxiliary CIR/SR rewards on top of the outcome-based reward. This joint reward matches the accuracy of RLVR while also leading to causally important and sufficient reasoning. These results show that RLVR does not always lead models to rely on reasoning in the way that is commonly thought, but this issue can be remedied with simple modifications to the post-training procedure.

ai.tldr: 本文发现基于可验证奖励的强化学习（RLVR）虽然能提升任务准确率，但并不能保证推理链的因果重要性和充分性，而通过简单修改训练流程（如加入监督微调或辅助奖励）可以改善这一问题。
ai.motivation: 常见假设认为RLVR训练出的推理链可靠地反映了模型如何得到答案，但这一假设缺乏严格验证。
ai.method: 提出两个度量指标：推理因果重要性（CIR）和推理充分性（SR），并在Qwen2.5模型和ReasoningGym任务上进行实验分析。
ai.result: RLVR提升准确率但未可靠提升CIR或SR；少量SFT可改善；联合奖励（结果奖励+CIR/SR辅助奖励）可同时提升准确率和推理质量。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解RLVR在推理任务中的实际效果，并提供改进训练流程的具体方向。

idea_spark.idea: 设计基于推理因果重要性和充分性的辅助奖励，替代或补充传统结果奖励，以引导模型生成更可靠的推理链。
idea_spark.risk: 辅助奖励可能增加训练复杂度，且CIR/SR的计算本身需要额外验证器，可能引入偏差。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从因果推断和可解释性角度重新审视强化学习在语言模型推理中的应用，启发更细粒度的奖励设计。

en.tldr: This paper finds that RLVR improves task accuracy but does not guarantee causal importance or sufficiency of reasoning chains, and simple modifications (e.g., SFT or auxiliary rewards) can remedy this.
en.motivation: The common assumption that RLVR-trained reasoning chains reliably reflect how a model reaches its answer lacks rigorous validation.
en.method: Proposes two metrics: Causal Importance of Reasoning (CIR) and Sufficiency of Reasoning (SR), and conducts experiments with Qwen2.5 models and ReasoningGym tasks.
en.result: RLVR improves accuracy but not reliably CIR or SR; a small amount of SFT helps; joint reward (outcome + auxiliary CIR/SR) improves both accuracy and reasoning quality.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the actual effectiveness of RLVR in reasoning tasks and provides concrete directions for improving training procedures.

idea_spark.idea: Design auxiliary rewards based on causal importance and sufficiency of reasoning to complement or replace traditional outcome rewards, guiding models to produce more reliable reasoning chains.
idea_spark.risk: Auxiliary rewards may increase training complexity, and computing CIR/SR requires additional verifiers that could introduce bias.
idea_spark.inspiration: Re-examine reinforcement learning in language model reasoning from causal inference and interpretability perspectives, inspiring finer-grained reward design.

papers.title: Distilling Vision Transformers for Distortion-Robust Representation Learning
papers.summary: Self-supervised learning has achieved remarkable success in learning visual representations from clean data, yet remains challenging when clean observations are sparse or not available at all. In this paper, we demonstrate that pretrained vision models can be leveraged to learn distortion-robust representations, which can then be effectively applied to downstream tasks operating on distorted observations. In particular, we propose an asymmetric knowledge distillation framework in which both teacher and student are initialized from the same pretrained Vision Transformer but receive different views of each image: the teacher processes clean images, while the student sees their distorted versions. We introduce multi-level distillation that aligns global embeddings, patch-level features, and attention maps and show that the student is able to approximate clean-image representations despite never directly accessing clean data. We evaluate our approach on image classification tasks across several datasets and under various distortions, consistently outperforming existing alternatives for the same amount of human supervision.

ai.tldr: 提出非对称知识蒸馏框架，让视觉Transformer学生模型仅从失真图像中学习，逼近教师模型在干净图像上的表示。
ai.motivation: 自监督学习在干净数据上表现优异，但实际中干净观测往往稀疏或不可得。
ai.method: 教师和学生初始化自同一预训练ViT，教师处理干净图像，学生处理失真版本；通过全局嵌入、补丁级特征和注意力图的多级蒸馏对齐。
ai.result: 在多个数据集和多种失真下，图像分类任务一致优于同等监督量的现有方法。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种无需干净数据即可获得鲁棒视觉表示的训练范式。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将多级蒸馏扩展到视频或3D数据，处理时域或空间失真。
idea_spark.risk: 教师模型对干净数据的依赖可能限制在完全无干净数据的场景。
idea_spark.inspiration: 不对称蒸馏与多级对齐的结合启发设计更通用的鲁棒表示学习框架。

zh.tldr: 提出非对称知识蒸馏框架，让视觉Transformer学生模型仅从失真图像中学习，逼近教师模型在干净图像上的表示。

en.tldr: Proposes an asymmetric knowledge distillation framework where a Vision Transformer student learns from distorted images to approximate the teacher's clean-image representations.
en.motivation: Self-supervised learning excels on clean data but struggles when clean observations are scarce or unavailable.
en.method: Teacher and student are initialized from the same pretrained ViT; teacher sees clean images, student sees distorted versions; multi-level distillation aligns global embeddings, patch-level features, and attention maps.
en.result: Consistently outperforms existing alternatives with the same amount of human supervision on image classification across multiple datasets and distortions.
en.help_to_user: Provides a training paradigm to obtain robust visual representations without requiring clean data.

idea_spark.idea: Explore extending multi-level distillation to video or 3D data to handle temporal or spatial distortions.
idea_spark.risk: Dependence on a teacher model with clean data may limit applicability in fully clean-free scenarios.
idea_spark.inspiration: The combination of asymmetric distillation and multi-level alignment inspires more general robust representation learning frameworks.

papers.title: Learning Evidence Highlighting for Frozen LLMs
papers.summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) can reason well, yet often miss decisive evidence when it is buried in long, noisy contexts. We introduce HiLight, an Evidence Emphasis framework that decouples evidence selection from reasoning for frozen LLM solvers. HiLight avoids compressing or rewriting the input, which can discard or distort evidence, by training a lightweight Emphasis Actor to insert minimal highlight tags around pivotal spans in the unaltered context. A frozen Solver then performs downstream reasoning on the emphasized input. We cast highlighting as a weakly supervised decision-making problem and optimize the Actor with reinforcement learning using only the Solver's task reward, requiring no evidence labels and no access to or modification of the Solver. Across sequential recommendation and long-context question answering, HiLight consistently improves performance over strong prompt-based and automated prompt-optimization baselines. The learned emphasis policy transfers zero-shot to both smaller and larger unseen Solver families, including an API-based Solver, suggesting that the Actor captures genuine, reusable evidence structure rather than overfitting to a single backbone.

ai.tldr: 提出HiLight框架，通过强化学习训练轻量级强调Actor，在冻结LLM的输入中插入高亮标记，提升长上下文中的证据识别能力。
ai.motivation: LLM在长且嘈杂的上下文中容易遗漏关键证据，现有方法（压缩或重写输入）可能丢失或扭曲证据。
ai.method: 将证据高亮视为弱监督决策问题，使用强化学习优化Actor，仅依赖求解器的任务奖励，无需证据标签或修改求解器。
ai.result: 在序列推荐和长上下文问答任务中，HiLight持续优于强基线，且学到的强调策略可零样本迁移到不同规模的求解器。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何在不修改LLM的情况下，通过轻量级标记提升模型对关键信息的关注。

idea_spark.idea: 将证据选择与推理解耦，用强化学习训练独立于求解器的强调模块。
idea_spark.risk: 强调标记可能引入偏差，或对某些任务类型（如需要全局信息的任务）效果有限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 可探索将类似强调机制应用于其他模态（如视觉或表格数据）的上下文理解。

zh.tldr: 提出HiLight框架，通过强化学习训练轻量级强调Actor，在冻结LLM的输入中插入高亮标记，提升长上下文中的证据识别能力。

idea_spark.idea: 将证据选择与推理解耦，用强化学习训练独立于求解器的强调模块。
idea_spark.risk: 强调标记可能引入偏差，或对某些任务类型（如需要全局信息的任务）效果有限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 可探索将类似强调机制应用于其他模态（如视觉或表格数据）的上下文理解。

en.tldr: Proposes HiLight, an evidence emphasis framework that trains a lightweight Emphasis Actor via reinforcement learning to insert highlight tags into frozen LLM inputs, improving evidence detection in long contexts.
en.motivation: LLMs often miss decisive evidence buried in long, noisy contexts; existing methods (compressing or rewriting input) may discard or distort evidence.
en.method: Formulates highlighting as a weakly supervised decision-making problem, optimizing the Actor with reinforcement learning using only the Solver's task reward, without evidence labels or Solver modification.
en.result: HiLight consistently outperforms strong baselines on sequential recommendation and long-context QA, and the learned emphasis policy transfers zero-shot to unseen Solver families.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to improve LLM focus on key evidence via lightweight tags without modifying the model.

idea_spark.idea: Decouple evidence selection from reasoning by training a separate emphasis module with reinforcement learning.
idea_spark.risk: Highlight tags may introduce bias or be less effective for tasks requiring global context.
idea_spark.inspiration: Explore applying similar emphasis mechanisms to other modalities (e.g., vision or tabular data) for context understanding.

papers.title: GICC: A High-Performance Runtime for GPU-Initiated Communication and Coordination in Modern HPC Systems
papers.summary: Distributed GPU applications increasingly rely on kernel-level, cross-node coordination to reduce launch overheads and improve compute-communication overlap, but such support is lacking. On OFI-based interconnects such as HPE Slingshot, which powers six of the top ten systems in the November 2025 Top500, including the top three, GPU kernels cannot autonomously drive distributed coordination: existing runtimes rely on host-driven progress and lack a bounded mechanism for recycling pre-staged NIC work across repeated GPU-triggered operations. On InfiniBand, GPU-initiated communication is possible, but current implementations incur unnecessary synchronization and locking overheads.   This paper presents GICC, a framework that enables GPU kernels to directly trigger NIC-level operations without host involvement on the fast path. In stencils, GPU threads initiate halo exchanges as soon as boundary regions are computed, enabling fine-grained overlap between interior computation and boundary transfer. GICC decouples coordination semantics from data movement and introduces asynchronous resource reclamation: the NIC signals completion to both GPU and host memory, letting a lightweight host thread recycle NIC resources concurrently with GPU execution without injecting latency into the coordination path. This sustains GPU-driven coordination under finite NIC state, absent from existing OFI-based runtimes.   We implement GICC on NVIDIA and AMD GPUs over InfiniBand and Slingshot. On Slingshot, GICC reduces per-coordination latency by up to 229x and improves weak scaling efficiency by up to 25%. On InfiniBand, it achieves up to 1.95x lower put latency than NVSHMEM by eliminating unnecessary locking and synchronization. On an industrial stencil proxy on 64 AMD MI250X GCDs, GPU-aware MPI incurs over 52% higher communication time than GICC, which achieves 42% parallel efficiency versus MPI's 35.4%.

ai.tldr: 提出GICC框架，使GPU内核能直接触发NIC操作，无需主机参与，实现高效跨节点协调。
ai.motivation: 现有运行时依赖主机驱动，缺乏GPU自主协调能力，且存在同步和锁开销。
ai.method: 解耦协调语义与数据移动，引入异步资源回收，NIC同时通知GPU和主机，轻量级主机线程并发回收NIC资源。
ai.result: 在Slingshot上延迟降低229倍，弱扩展效率提升25%；InfiniBand上put延迟比NVSHMEM低1.95倍；工业stencil代理上通信时间比MPI低52%。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过GPU自主通信减少主机干预，提升HPC应用性能。

idea_spark.idea: 将NIC资源回收与GPU执行完全并行化，消除协调路径上的延迟。
idea_spark.risk: 对NIC硬件和驱动有依赖，可能限制跨平台通用性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从GPU内核直接驱动网络操作，类似GPU直接内存访问的思路，但扩展到协调语义。

zh.tldr: 提出GICC框架，使GPU内核能直接触发NIC操作，无需主机参与，实现高效跨节点协调。
zh.motivation: 现有运行时依赖主机驱动，缺乏GPU自主协调能力，且存在同步和锁开销。
zh.method: 解耦协调语义与数据移动，引入异步资源回收，NIC同时通知GPU和主机，轻量级主机线程并发回收NIC资源。
zh.result: 在Slingshot上延迟降低229倍，弱扩展效率提升25%；InfiniBand上put延迟比NVSHMEM低1.95倍；工业stencil代理上通信时间比MPI低52%。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过GPU自主通信减少主机干预，提升HPC应用性能。

idea_spark.idea: 将NIC资源回收与GPU执行完全并行化，消除协调路径上的延迟。
idea_spark.risk: 对NIC硬件和驱动有依赖，可能限制跨平台通用性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从GPU内核直接驱动网络操作，类似GPU直接内存访问的思路，但扩展到协调语义。

en.tldr: GICC enables GPU kernels to directly trigger NIC operations without host involvement, reducing coordination latency and improving overlap.
en.motivation: Existing runtimes rely on host-driven progress and lack bounded resource recycling for GPU-triggered operations on OFI interconnects; InfiniBand implementations have unnecessary synchronization and locking.
en.method: Decouples coordination semantics from data movement, introduces asynchronous resource reclamation where NIC signals both GPU and host, allowing a lightweight host thread to recycle NIC resources concurrently with GPU execution.
en.result: On Slingshot, up to 229x lower per-coordination latency and 25% better weak scaling efficiency; on InfiniBand, 1.95x lower put latency than NVSHMEM; on industrial stencil proxy, 42% parallel efficiency vs MPI's 35.4%.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how GPU-initiated communication reduces host involvement and improves HPC application performance.

idea_spark.idea: Parallelize NIC resource recycling with GPU execution to eliminate latency from the coordination path.
idea_spark.risk: Dependence on specific NIC hardware and drivers may limit cross-platform generality.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by GPU direct memory access, extended to coordination semantics for kernel-level network operations.

papers.title: Incentivizing Neuro-symbolic Language-based Reasoning in VLMs via Reinforcement Learning
papers.summary: There are 7,407 languages in the world. But, what about the languages that are not there in the world? Are humans so narrow minded that we don't care about the languages aliens communicate in? Aliens are humans too! In the 2016 movie Arrival, Amy Adams plays a linguist, Dr. Louise Banks who, by learning to think in an alien language (Heptapod) formed of non-sequential sentences, gains the ability to transcend time and look into the future. In this work, I aim to explore the representation and reasoning of vision-language concepts in a neuro-symbolic language, and study improvement in analytical reasoning abilities and efficiency of "thinking systems". With Qwen3-VL-2B-Instruct as base model and 4 $\times$ Nvidia H200 GPU nodes, I achieve an accuracy improvement of 3.33\% on a vision-language evaluation dataset consisting of math, science, and general knowledge questions, while reducing the reasoning tokens by 75\% over SymPy. I've documented the compute challenges faced, scaling possibilities, and the future work to improve thinking in a neuro-symbolic language in vision-language models. The training and inference setup can be found here: https://github.com/i-like-bfs-and-dfs/wolfram-reasoning.

ai.tldr: 通过强化学习激励视觉语言模型使用神经符号语言进行推理，在减少75%推理token的同时提升3.33%的准确率。
ai.motivation: 探索神经符号语言在视觉语言模型中的表示与推理能力，提升分析推理效率和思维系统的性能。
ai.method: 以Qwen3-VL-2B-Instruct为基础模型，采用强化学习训练模型使用神经符号语言进行推理。
ai.result: 在包含数学、科学和常识的视觉语言评估数据集上准确率提升3.33%，推理token较SymPy减少75%。
ai.help_to_user: 展示了如何通过强化学习让视觉语言模型学会更高效的符号化推理，减少计算开销。

idea_spark.idea: 将神经符号语言作为中间表示，结合强化学习优化推理路径，可能适用于多模态智能体。
idea_spark.risk: 神经符号语言的泛化性可能受限，外星语言设定缺乏实际数据支撑。

en.motivation: Explore representation and reasoning of vision-language concepts in a neuro-symbolic language to improve analytical reasoning and efficiency of thinking systems.
en.method: Fine-tune Qwen3-VL-2B-Instruct with reinforcement learning to reason in a neuro-symbolic language.

idea_spark.risk: Generalization of neuro-symbolic language may be limited; the alien language premise lacks real-world data support.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by non-linear thinking in the alien language from the movie Arrival, exploring non-sequential reasoning in AI.

papers.title: An Undecidability Proof for the Plan Existence Problem
papers.summary: The plan existence problem asks, given a goal in the form of a formula in modal logic, an initial epistemic state (a pointed Kripke model), and a set of epistemic actions, whether there exists a sequence of actions that can be applied to reach the goal. We prove that even in the case where the preconditions of the epistemic actions have modal depth at most 1, and there are no postconditions, the plan existence problem is undecidable. The (un)decidability of this problem was previously unknown.

ai.tldr: 证明即使认知动作的前提条件模态深度不超过1且无后置条件，规划存在问题也是不可判定的。
ai.motivation: 此前规划存在问题的可判定性未知，本文旨在填补这一空白。
ai.method: 通过归约到已知不可判定问题（如图灵机停机问题）来证明不可判定性。
ai.result: 在限制条件下规划存在问题仍不可判定。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解认知规划问题的理论边界，避免在不可判定情形下浪费计算资源。

idea_spark.idea: 探索更弱限制（如限制动作数量或模型大小）下的可判定子类。
idea_spark.risk: 过度简化可能导致问题平凡化，失去实际意义。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受经典规划不可判定性结果启发，将认知维度纳入分析。

en.tldr: Proves that the plan existence problem remains undecidable even when epistemic action preconditions have modal depth at most 1 and there are no postconditions.
en.motivation: The (un)decidability of this problem was previously unknown; this work fills the gap.
en.method: Reduction from a known undecidable problem (e.g., Turing machine halting problem).
en.result: The plan existence problem is undecidable under the given restrictions.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand the theoretical limits of epistemic planning, avoiding wasted computation on undecidable cases.

idea_spark.idea: Explore decidable subclasses under weaker restrictions (e.g., bounded action sequences or model size).
idea_spark.risk: Over-simplification may trivialize the problem, losing practical relevance.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by classical undecidability results in planning, now extended to the epistemic dimension.

keywords_raw: preconditions with modal depth at most 1

papers.title: Long-tail Internet photo reconstruction
papers.summary: Internet photo collections exhibit an extremely long-tailed distribution: a few famous landmarks are densely photographed and easily reconstructed in 3D, while most real-world sites are represented with sparse, noisy, uneven imagery beyond the capabilities of both classical and learned 3D methods. We believe that tackling this long-tail regime represents one of the next frontiers for 3D foundation models. Although reliable ground-truth 3D supervision from sparse scenes is challenging to acquire, we observe that it can be effectively simulated by sampling sparse subsets from well-reconstructed Internet landmarks. To this end, we introduce MegaDepth-X, a large dataset of 3D reconstructions with clean, dense depth, together with a strategy for sampling sets of training images that mimic camera distributions in long-tail scenes. Finetuning 3D foundation models with these components yields robust reconstructions under extreme sparsity, and also enables more reliable reconstruction in symmetric and repetitive scenes, while preserving generalization to standard, dense 3D benchmark datasets.

ai.tldr: 针对互联网照片的长尾分布问题，提出通过模拟稀疏场景训练3D基础模型，并构建MegaDepth-X数据集，实现稀疏、对称、重复场景的鲁棒重建。
ai.motivation: 互联网照片集呈现极端长尾分布：少数地标有密集照片，多数真实场景只有稀疏、有噪声、不均匀的图像，现有经典和学习的3D方法难以处理。
ai.method: 从重建良好的地标中采样稀疏子集模拟稀疏场景，构建包含干净稠密深度的MegaDepth-X数据集，并设计训练图像采样策略以匹配长尾场景的相机分布。
ai.result: 微调3D基础模型后，在极端稀疏条件下重建鲁棒，对称和重复场景重建更可靠，且在标准密集3D基准上保持泛化能力。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过数据模拟和微调提升3D基础模型在长尾稀疏场景下的重建性能。

idea_spark.idea: 利用生成模型或扩散模型合成更逼真的稀疏相机分布，进一步扩展训练数据的多样性。
idea_spark.risk: 模拟数据可能与真实长尾场景存在分布偏移，导致模型在极端未见场景中泛化不足。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从长尾分布角度重新思考3D重建的数据瓶颈，启发设计更高效的数据采集或增强策略。

en.tldr: Addressing the long-tail distribution of Internet photos, this work proposes simulating sparse scenes to fine-tune 3D foundation models, and introduces the MegaDepth-X dataset for robust reconstruction under extreme sparsity, symmetry, and repetition.
en.motivation: Internet photo collections exhibit an extremely long-tailed distribution: a few landmarks are densely photographed, while most real-world sites have sparse, noisy, uneven imagery beyond the capabilities of existing 3D methods.
en.method: Sparse subsets are sampled from well-reconstructed landmarks to simulate sparse scenes. The MegaDepth-X dataset with clean dense depth is constructed, along with a training image sampling strategy that mimics camera distributions in long-tail scenes.
en.result: Fine-tuning 3D foundation models yields robust reconstructions under extreme sparsity, more reliable reconstruction in symmetric and repetitive scenes, while preserving generalization to standard dense 3D benchmarks.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how data simulation and fine-tuning can improve 3D foundation model performance in long-tail sparse scenarios.

idea_spark.idea: Use generative or diffusion models to synthesize more realistic sparse camera distributions to further diversify training data.
idea_spark.risk: Simulated data may have distribution shift from real long-tail scenes, leading to insufficient generalization in extreme unseen scenarios.
idea_spark.inspiration: Reconsidering the data bottleneck in 3D reconstruction from a long-tail perspective inspires more efficient data acquisition or augmentation strategies.

papers.title: Selective Contrastive Learning For Gloss Free Sign Language Translation
papers.summary: Sign language translation (SLT) converts continuous sign videos into spoken-language text, yet it remains challenging due to the intrinsic modality mismatch between visual signs and written text, particularly in gloss-free settings. Recent SLT systems increasingly adopt CLIP-like Vision-Language pretraining (VLP) for cross-modal alignment, but the random in-batch contrast provides few, batch-dependent negatives and may mislabel semantically similar (or even identical) pairs as negatives, introducing noisy and potentially inconsistent alignment supervision. In this work, we first conduct a preliminary trajectory-based analysis that tracks negative video-text similarity over training. The results show that only a small subset of negatives exhibits the desired behavior of being consistently pushed away, while the remaining negatives display heterogeneous and often non-decreasing similarity dynamics, suggesting that random in-batch negatives are frequently uninformative for effective alignment. Inspired by this, we propose Selective Contrastive Learning for SLT (SCL-SLT) with a Pair Selection (PS) strategy. PS scores candidate negatives using similarity dynamics from reference checkpoints and constructs mini-batches via a curriculum that progressively emphasizes more challenging negatives, thereby strengthening contrastive supervision while reducing the influence of noisy or semantically invalid negatives.

ai.tldr: 提出选择性对比学习（SCL-SLT），通过轨迹分析筛选有信息量的负样本，减少噪声监督，提升无词汇手语翻译性能。
ai.motivation: 现有CLIP类视觉-语言预训练在手语翻译中随机批次对比产生大量无信息或语义错误的负样本，导致对齐监督不一致。
ai.method: 先进行负样本相似度轨迹分析，发现仅少数负样本持续被推开；据此设计对选择策略，利用参考检查点的相似度动态评分候选负样本，并通过课程学习逐步强调困难负样本。
ai.result: 强化对比监督，减少噪声或语义无效负样本的影响，提升翻译质量。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何通过选择性对比学习改进跨模态对齐，适用于手语翻译及其他视觉-语言任务。

idea_spark.idea: 利用训练过程中负样本相似度的动态变化作为筛选依据，而非静态特征，可能更适应模型演化。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖参考检查点的选择可能引入额外计算开销，且课程进度需仔细调参。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从负样本行为轨迹中挖掘信息，启发设计自适应负样本采样方法。

idea_spark.idea: 利用训练过程中负样本相似度的动态变化作为筛选依据，而非静态特征，可能更适应模型演化。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖参考检查点的选择可能引入额外计算开销，且课程进度需仔细调参。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从负样本行为轨迹中挖掘信息，启发设计自适应负样本采样方法。

en.tldr: Proposes Selective Contrastive Learning (SCL-SLT) with a Pair Selection strategy that filters informative negatives via trajectory analysis, reducing noisy supervision for gloss-free sign language translation.
en.motivation: Existing CLIP-like VLP in SLT uses random in-batch contrast, yielding many uninformative or semantically invalid negatives, leading to inconsistent alignment supervision.
en.method: First analyzes negative video-text similarity dynamics during training, finding only a few negatives are consistently pushed away; then designs a Pair Selection strategy that scores candidate negatives using similarity dynamics from reference checkpoints and builds mini-batches via curriculum learning to progressively emphasize harder negatives.
en.result: Strengthens contrastive supervision while reducing the influence of noisy or semantically invalid negatives, improving translation quality.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand how selective contrastive learning improves cross-modal alignment, applicable to sign language translation and other vision-language tasks.

idea_spark.idea: Using the dynamics of negative similarity during training as a selection criterion, rather than static features, may better adapt to model evolution.
idea_spark.risk: Dependence on reference checkpoints may introduce extra computational cost, and curriculum pacing requires careful tuning.
idea_spark.inspiration: Mining information from negative behavior trajectories inspires adaptive negative sampling methods.

papers.title: Transferable Physical-World Adversarial Patches Against Pedestrian Detection Models
papers.summary: Physical adversarial patch attacks critically threaten pedestrian detection, causing surveillance and autonomous driving systems to miss pedestrians and creating severe safety risks. Despite their effectiveness in controlled settings, existing physical attacks face two major limitations in practice: they lack systematic disruption of the multi-stage decision pipeline, enabling residual modules to offset perturbations, and they fail to model complex physical variations, leading to poor robustness. To overcome these limitations, we propose a novel pedestrian adversarial patch generation method that combines multi-stage collaborative attacks with robustness enhancement under physical diversity, called TriPatch. Specifically, we design a triplet loss consisting of detection confidence suppression, bounding-box offset amplification, and non-maximum suppression (NMS) disruption, which jointly act across different stages of the detection pipeline. In addition, we introduce an appearance consistency loss to constrain the color distribution of the patch, thereby improving its adaptability under diverse imaging conditions, and incorporate data augmentation to further enhance robustness against complex physical perturbations. Extensive experiments demonstrate that TriPatch achieves a higher attack success rate across multiple detector models compared to existing approaches.

ai.tldr: 提出一种名为TriPatch的物理对抗补丁方法，通过多阶段协同攻击和鲁棒性增强，有效欺骗行人检测模型。
ai.motivation: 现有物理攻击未能系统破坏检测模型的多阶段决策流程，且对复杂物理变化鲁棒性差。
ai.method: 设计三元组损失（抑制检测置信度、放大边界框偏移、破坏非极大值抑制），并引入外观一致性损失和数据增强。
ai.result: 在多个检测器模型上，TriPatch的攻击成功率高于现有方法。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何生成鲁棒的物理对抗样本，用于评估行人检测系统的安全性。

idea_spark.idea: 将多阶段协同攻击与外观一致性约束结合，提升物理对抗补丁的实用性和鲁棒性。
idea_spark.risk: 可能被恶意用于攻击自动驾驶系统，造成安全隐患。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发设计更通用的对抗防御策略，关注检测模型的多阶段决策过程。
