idea_spark.inspiration: 启发设计更通用的对抗防御策略，关注检测模型的多阶段决策过程。

en.tldr: Proposes TriPatch, a physical adversarial patch method that combines multi-stage collaborative attack and robustness enhancement to effectively fool pedestrian detection models.
en.motivation: Existing physical attacks fail to systematically disrupt the multi-stage decision pipeline of detection models and lack robustness to complex physical variations.
en.method: Designs a triplet loss (suppressing detection confidence, amplifying bounding-box offset, disrupting NMS) and introduces appearance consistency loss and data augmentation.
en.result: TriPatch achieves higher attack success rates across multiple detector models compared to existing approaches.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand how to generate robust physical adversarial examples for evaluating pedestrian detection system security.

idea_spark.idea: Combining multi-stage collaborative attack with appearance consistency constraints enhances the practicality and robustness of physical adversarial patches.
idea_spark.risk: Could be maliciously used to attack autonomous driving systems, causing safety hazards.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires designing more general adversarial defense strategies that focus on the multi-stage decision process of detection models.

papers.title: TTS-PRISM: A Perceptual Reasoning and Interpretable Speech Model for Fine-Grained Diagnosis
papers.summary: While generative text-to-speech (TTS) models approach human-level quality, monolithic metrics fail to diagnose fine-grained acoustic artifacts or explain perceptual collapse. To address this, we propose TTS-PRISM, a multi-dimensional diagnostic framework for Mandarin. First, we establish a 12-dimensional schema spanning stability to advanced expressiveness. Second, we design a targeted synthesis pipeline with adversarial perturbations and expert anchors to build a high-quality diagnostic dataset. Third, schema-driven instruction tuning embeds explicit scoring criteria and reasoning into an efficient end-to-end model. Experiments on a 1,600-sample Gold Test Set show TTS-PRISM outperforms generalist models in human alignment. Profiling six TTS paradigms establishes intuitive diagnostic flags that reveal fine-grained capability differences. TTS-PRISM is open-source, with code and checkpoints at https://github.com/xiaomi-research/tts-prism.

ai.tldr: 提出TTS-PRISM，一个针对普通话语音合成细粒度诊断的多维框架，通过12维评估体系、对抗扰动合成管道和指令微调，在1600样本测试集上优于通用模型。
ai.motivation: 现有生成式TTS模型虽接近人类水平，但单一指标无法诊断细粒度声学伪影或解释感知崩溃。
ai.method: 建立12维评估体系，设计对抗扰动与专家锚点的合成管道构建诊断数据集，通过模式驱动指令微调嵌入评分标准与推理到端到端模型。
ai.result: 在1600样本黄金测试集上，TTS-PRISM在人类对齐上优于通用模型，并揭示六种TTS范式的细粒度能力差异。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户诊断TTS输出中的具体声学问题，提供可解释的细粒度评分。

idea_spark.idea: 将对抗扰动与专家锚点结合，生成有针对性的诊断数据集，提升模型对特定伪影的敏感性。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖专家定义的评估维度可能遗漏未知伪影，且对抗扰动可能引入人工痕迹。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴计算机视觉中的对抗性诊断方法，将其应用于语音生成模型的细粒度评估。

en.tldr: Proposes TTS-PRISM, a multi-dimensional diagnostic framework for Mandarin TTS fine-grained diagnosis, using a 12-dimension schema, adversarial perturbation pipeline, and instruction tuning, outperforming generalist models on a 1600-sample test set.
en.motivation: Generative TTS models approach human quality, but monolithic metrics fail to diagnose fine-grained acoustic artifacts or explain perceptual collapse.
en.method: Establishes a 12-dimension schema, designs a synthesis pipeline with adversarial perturbations and expert anchors for diagnostic dataset, and uses schema-driven instruction tuning to embed scoring criteria and reasoning into an end-to-end model.
en.result: TTS-PRISM outperforms generalist models in human alignment on a 1600-sample Gold Test Set and reveals fine-grained capability differences across six TTS paradigms.
en.help_to_user: Helps users diagnose specific acoustic issues in TTS outputs with interpretable fine-grained scores.

idea_spark.idea: Combine adversarial perturbations with expert anchors to generate targeted diagnostic datasets, enhancing model sensitivity to specific artifacts.
idea_spark.risk: Reliance on expert-defined dimensions may miss unknown artifacts, and adversarial perturbations could introduce artificial traces.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draws from adversarial diagnosis in computer vision, applying it to fine-grained evaluation of speech generation models.

papers.title: Rethinking Math Reasoning Evaluation: A Robust LLM-as-a-Judge Framework Beyond Symbolic Rigidity
papers.summary: Recent advancements in large language models have led to significant improvements across various tasks, including mathematical reasoning, which is used to assess models' intelligence in logical reasoning and problem-solving. Models are evaluated on mathematical reasoning benchmarks by verifying the correctness of the final answer against a ground truth answer. A common approach for this verification is based on symbolic mathematics comparison, which fails to generalize across diverse mathematical representations and solution formats. In this work, we offer a robust and flexible alternative to rule-based symbolic mathematics comparison. We propose an LLM-based evaluation framework for evaluating model-generated answers, enabling accurate evaluation across diverse mathematical representations and answer formats. We present failure cases of symbolic evaluation in two popular frameworks, Lighteval and SimpleRL, and compare them to our approach, demonstrating clear improvements over commonly used methods. Our framework enables more reliable evaluation and benchmarking, leading to more accurate performance monitoring, which is important for advancing mathematical problem-solving and intelligent systems.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于LLM的数学推理评估框架，克服符号数学比较的局限性，提升评估准确性和鲁棒性。
ai.motivation: 现有符号数学比较方法无法泛化到多样化的数学表示和答案格式，导致评估不准确。
ai.method: 使用LLM作为评判者，直接评估模型生成的答案，替代规则驱动的符号比较。
ai.result: 在Lighteval和SimpleRL框架中展示符号评估的失败案例，并证明所提方法优于常用方法。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员更可靠地评估数学推理能力，避免符号比较的误判。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将LLM评判与少量示例或思维链提示结合，进一步提升评估一致性。
idea_spark.risk: LLM评判可能引入自身偏见或对复杂答案的误判，需设计校准机制。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受符号比较失败案例启发，转向更灵活的语义理解评估方式。

idea_spark.inspiration: 受符号比较失败案例启发，转向更灵活的语义理解评估方式。

en.tldr: Proposes an LLM-based evaluation framework for math reasoning that overcomes the limitations of symbolic comparison, improving accuracy and robustness.
en.motivation: Existing symbolic math comparison fails to generalize across diverse mathematical representations and answer formats, leading to inaccurate evaluation.
en.method: Uses an LLM as a judge to directly evaluate model-generated answers, replacing rule-based symbolic comparison.
en.result: Demonstrates failure cases of symbolic evaluation in Lighteval and SimpleRL, and shows clear improvements over common methods.
en.help_to_user: Helps researchers evaluate math reasoning more reliably, avoiding misjudgments from symbolic comparison.

idea_spark.idea: Explore combining LLM judging with few-shot examples or chain-of-thought prompting to further improve consistency.
idea_spark.risk: LLM judging may introduce its own biases or misjudge complex answers, requiring calibration mechanisms.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by failure cases of symbolic evaluation, leading to a more flexible semantic understanding approach.

papers.title: Context-Fidelity Boosting: Enhancing Faithful Generation through Watermark-Inspired Decoding
papers.summary: Large language models (LLMs) often produce content that contradicts or overlooks information provided in the input context, a phenomenon known as faithfulness hallucination. In this paper, we propose Context-Fidelity Boosting (CFB), a lightweight and general decoding-time framework that reduces such hallucinations by increasing the generation probability of source-supported tokens. Motivated by logit-shaping principles from watermarking techniques, CFB applies additive token-level logit adjustments based on a token's degree of support from the input context. Specifically, we develop three boosting strategies: static boosting, which applies a fixed bias to source-supported tokens; context-aware boosting, which scales this bias using the divergence between next-token distributions with and without context; and token-aware boosting, which further redistributes the adaptive bias according to local relevance estimated from source-position attention and source-scoped semantic similarity. CFB requires no retraining or architectural changes, making it compatible with a wide range of LLMs. Experiments on summarization and question answering tasks across multiple open-source LLMs show that CFB consistently improves faithfulness metrics with minimal generation overhead. Our implementation is fully open-sourced.

ai.tldr: 提出上下文保真增强（CFB），一种轻量级解码框架，通过水印启发的logit调整减少大语言模型中的忠实性幻觉。
ai.motivation: 大语言模型常生成与输入上下文矛盾或忽略上下文信息的文本，即忠实性幻觉。现有方法需重训练或修改架构，成本高。
ai.method: 基于水印技术的logit调整思想，根据token受上下文支持程度施加加法logit偏置。设计三种增强策略：静态增强（固定偏置）、上下文感知增强（利用有无上下文时分布差异缩放偏置）、token感知增强（根据源位置注意力和语义相似度重新分配自适应偏置）。
ai.result: 在摘要和问答任务上，CFB一致提升忠实性指标，且生成开销极小，兼容多种开源大语言模型。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户减少模型生成中的事实错误，提升输出与给定上下文的一致性，无需额外训练。

idea_spark.idea: 将上下文支持度与token级logit调整结合，实现无需训练的忠实性增强。
idea_spark.risk: 偏置强度需谨慎调参，过强可能限制生成多样性或导致过拟合上下文。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从水印技术中借鉴logit塑形原理，为解决幻觉问题提供了新视角。

idea_spark.risk: 偏置强度需谨慎调参，过强可能限制生成多样性或导致过拟合上下文。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从水印技术中借鉴logit塑形原理，为解决幻觉问题提供了新视角。

en.tldr: Proposes Context-Fidelity Boosting (CFB), a lightweight decoding framework that reduces faithfulness hallucinations in LLMs via watermark-inspired logit adjustments.
en.motivation: LLMs often produce content contradicting or ignoring input context, known as faithfulness hallucination. Existing methods require retraining or architectural changes.
en.method: Inspired by logit-shaping in watermarking, CFB applies additive token-level logit biases based on context support. Three strategies: static boosting (fixed bias), context-aware boosting (scales bias by distribution divergence with/without context), and token-aware boosting (redistributes adaptive bias via source-position attention and semantic similarity).
en.result: On summarization and QA tasks, CFB consistently improves faithfulness metrics with minimal overhead, compatible with multiple open-source LLMs.
en.help_to_user: Helps reduce factual errors in model outputs, improving consistency with given context without retraining.

idea_spark.idea: Combining context support with token-level logit adjustments enables training-free faithfulness enhancement.
idea_spark.risk: Bias strength requires careful tuning; excessive bias may limit generation diversity or cause overfitting to context.
idea_spark.inspiration: Borrowing logit-shaping from watermarking offers a novel perspective for addressing hallucination problems.

papers.title: GenMatter: Perceiving Physical Objects with Generative Matter Models
papers.summary: Human visual perception offers valuable insights for understanding computational principles of motion-based scene interpretation. Humans robustly detect and segment moving entities that constitute independently moveable chunks of matter, whether observing sparse moving dots, textured surfaces, or naturalistic scenes. In contrast, existing computer vision systems lack a unified approach that works across these diverse settings. Inspired by principles of human perception, we propose a generative model that hierarchically groups low-level motion cues and high-level appearance features into particles (small Gaussians representing local matter), and groups particles into clusters capturing coherently and independently moveable physical entities. We develop a hardware-accelerated inference algorithm based on parallelized block Gibbs sampling to recover stable particle motion and groupings. Our model operates on different kinds of inputs (random dots, stylized textures, or naturalistic RGB video), enabling it to work across settings where biological vision succeeds but existing computer vision approaches do not. We validate this unified framework across three domains: on 2D random dot kinematograms, our approach captures human object perception including graded uncertainty across ambiguous conditions; on a Gestalt-inspired dataset of camouflaged rotating objects, our approach recovers correct 3D structure from motion and thereby accurate 2D object segmentation; and on naturalistic RGB videos, our model tracks the moving 3D matter that makes up deforming objects, enabling robust object-level scene understanding. This work thus establishes a general framework for motion-based perception grounded in principles of human vision.

ai.tldr: 提出一种受人类视觉启发的生成式物质模型，通过分层分组低层运动线索和高层外观特征为粒子与簇，统一处理随机点、纹理和自然视频中的运动感知与物体分割。
ai.motivation: 现有计算机视觉系统缺乏统一方法处理不同场景下的运动感知，而人类视觉能稳健检测和分割独立运动的实体。
ai.method: 构建分层生成模型：将运动线索和外观特征分组为高斯粒子，再将粒子聚类为独立运动实体；采用并行块吉布斯采样加速推理。
ai.result: 在随机点运动图、伪装旋转物体和自然RGB视频上均取得良好效果，捕捉人类感知的不确定性、恢复3D结构并实现鲁棒物体分割。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种基于人类视觉原理的统一运动感知框架，可用于视频中的物体分割、3D结构恢复和场景理解。

idea_spark.idea: 将人类视觉的“物质”概念引入生成模型，通过概率推理实现跨模态（点、纹理、视频）的统一运动感知。
idea_spark.risk: 模型依赖运动线索，对静态场景或极低帧率视频可能失效；并行吉布斯采样的计算开销在实时应用中需优化。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从人类视觉的“可独立移动物质块”感知出发，启发设计更通用的物理世界理解模型。

zh.tldr: 提出一种受人类视觉启发的生成式物质模型，通过分层分组低层运动线索和高层外观特征为粒子与簇，统一处理随机点、纹理和自然视频中的运动感知与物体分割。

idea_spark.idea: 将人类视觉的“物质”概念引入生成模型，通过概率推理实现跨模态（点、纹理、视频）的统一运动感知。

idea_spark.inspiration: 从人类视觉的“可独立移动物质块”感知出发，启发设计更通用的物理世界理解模型。

en.tldr: Proposes a generative matter model inspired by human vision that hierarchically groups motion cues and appearance features into particles and clusters, unifying motion-based perception across random dots, textures, and natural videos.
en.motivation: Existing computer vision lacks a unified approach for motion-based scene interpretation across diverse settings, whereas human vision robustly detects and segments independently moving entities.
en.method: Builds a hierarchical generative model: groups low-level motion cues and high-level appearance features into Gaussian particles, then clusters particles into coherent moving entities; uses parallelized block Gibbs sampling for efficient inference.
en.result: Demonstrates effectiveness on random dot kinematograms (capturing human-like uncertainty), camouflaged rotating objects (recovering 3D structure and 2D segmentation), and natural RGB videos (tracking deforming objects).
en.help_to_user: Provides a unified motion perception framework grounded in human vision principles, applicable to object segmentation, 3D structure recovery, and scene understanding from video.

idea_spark.idea: Introduces the concept of 'matter' from human vision into generative models, enabling cross-modal (dots, textures, video) unified motion perception via probabilistic inference.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on motion cues, may fail for static scenes or very low frame-rate videos; computational cost of parallel Gibbs sampling needs optimization for real-time use.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the human visual ability to perceive 'independently movable chunks of matter', suggesting a general framework for physical world understanding.

papers.title: Forecasting Solar Energy Using a Single Image
papers.summary: Solar panels are increasingly deployed in cities on rooftops, walls, and urban infrastructure. Although the panel costs have fallen in recent years, the soft costs of installing them have not. These soft costs include assessing the illumination (irradiance) of a panel, which is typically performed using a 3D model that fails to capture small nearby structures that impact the irradiance. Our approach uses a single image taken at the panel's location to forecast its irradiance at any time in the future. We use visual cues in the image to find the camera's orientation and the portion of the sky visible to the panel in order to forecast the irradiance due to the sun and the sky. In addition, we show that the irradiance due to reflections from nearby buildings varies smoothly over time and can be forecasted from the image. This approach enables assessing the solar energy potential of any surface and forecasting the temporal variation of a panel's irradiance. We validate our approach using real irradiance measurements in urban canyons. We show that our approach often yields more accurate irradiance forecasts compared to conventional irradiance-based transposition methods and 3D model-based simulations. We also show that a single spherical image can be used to find the best fixed orientation of a panel. Finally, we present Solaris, a device to capture the image seen by a panel in a variety of urban settings.

ai.tldr: 通过单张图像预测太阳能电池板未来任意时刻的辐照度，无需3D模型。
ai.motivation: 太阳能板安装的软成本（如辐照度评估）居高不下，传统3D模型方法无法捕捉附近小结构的影响。
ai.method: 利用单张图像中的视觉线索确定相机朝向和可见天空区域，预测太阳和天空的辐照度；同时利用反射辐照度的时间平滑性进行预测。
ai.result: 在城市峡谷实测中，该方法比传统辐照度转换方法和3D模型模拟更准确，并可用单张球面图像确定面板最佳固定朝向。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户快速评估任意表面的太阳能潜力，无需复杂建模。

idea_spark.idea: 结合深度学习自动从图像中提取天空可见区域和建筑反射特征，进一步提升预测精度。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖图像质量，极端天气或遮挡严重时可能失效。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用日常手机照片即可进行太阳能评估，降低专业设备门槛。

zh.tldr: 通过单张图像预测太阳能电池板未来任意时刻的辐照度，无需3D模型。
zh.motivation: 太阳能板安装的软成本（如辐照度评估）居高不下，传统3D模型方法无法捕捉附近小结构的影响。
zh.method: 利用单张图像中的视觉线索确定相机朝向和可见天空区域，预测太阳和天空的辐照度；同时利用反射辐照度的时间平滑性进行预测。
zh.result: 在城市峡谷实测中，该方法比传统辐照度转换方法和3D模型模拟更准确，并可用单张球面图像确定面板最佳固定朝向。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户快速评估任意表面的太阳能潜力，无需复杂建模。

idea_spark.idea: 结合深度学习自动从图像中提取天空可见区域和建筑反射特征，进一步提升预测精度。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖图像质量，极端天气或遮挡严重时可能失效。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用日常手机照片即可进行太阳能评估，降低专业设备门槛。

en.tldr: Forecast solar panel irradiance at any future time using a single image, without 3D models.
en.motivation: Soft costs of solar installation (e.g., irradiance assessment) remain high; traditional 3D models miss small nearby structures affecting irradiance.
en.method: Use visual cues from a single image to determine camera orientation and visible sky portion, predict sun and sky irradiance; leverage temporal smoothness of reflected irradiance from buildings.
en.result: In urban canyon tests, the method outperforms conventional transposition methods and 3D model simulations; a single spherical image can determine optimal fixed panel orientation.
en.help_to_user: Enables quick solar potential assessment of any surface without complex modeling.

idea_spark.idea: Integrate deep learning to automatically extract sky visibility and building reflection features for higher accuracy.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on image quality; may fail under extreme weather or heavy occlusion.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverage everyday phone photos for solar assessment, lowering the barrier of specialized equipment.

papers.title: SHAPE: Unifying Safety, Helpfulness and Pedagogy for Educational LLMs
papers.summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) have been widely explored in educational scenarios. We identify a critical vulnerability in current educational LLMs, pedagogical jailbreaks, where students use answer-inducing prompts to elicit solutions rather than scaffolded instructions. To enable systematic study, we unify and formalize safe, helpful, and pedagogical behaviors with a knowledge-mastery graph and introduce SHAPE, a benchmark of 9,087 student-question pairs for evaluating tutoring behavior under adversarial pressure. We propose a graph-augmented tutoring pipeline that infers prerequisite concepts from queries, identifies mastery gaps, and routes generation between instructing and problem-solving via explicit gating. Experiments across multiple LLMs show that our method yields significantly improved safety under two pedagogical jailbreak settings, while maintaining near-ceiling helpfulness under the same evaluation protocol. Our code and data are available at https://github.com/MAPS-research/SHaPE

ai.tldr: 提出SHAPE基准和基于知识图谱的管道，统一教育大语言模型的安全、有用和教学行为，防御教学越狱攻击。
ai.motivation: 当前教育大语言模型易受教学越狱攻击，学生通过诱导提示直接获取答案而非引导式教学。
ai.method: 构建知识掌握图统一安全、有用和教学行为；设计图增强管道，推断查询的先决概念、识别掌握缺口，并通过显式门控在指导与解题间路由生成。
ai.result: 在两种教学越狱设置下显著提升安全性，同时保持接近天花板的有用性。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助教育AI开发者构建更安全、更有用的教学系统，防止学生滥用模型获取答案。

idea_spark.idea: 将教学越狱防御与知识掌握图结合，实现动态路由生成。
idea_spark.risk: 门控策略可能过于保守，限制合理的问题求解需求。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴知识图谱推理与安全对齐的交叉思路，用于构建可信教育AI。

en.tldr: Proposes SHAPE benchmark and a graph-augmented pipeline to unify safe, helpful, and pedagogical behaviors in educational LLMs, defending against pedagogical jailbreaks.
en.motivation: Current educational LLMs are vulnerable to pedagogical jailbreaks where students elicit direct answers instead of scaffolded instruction.
en.method: Constructs a knowledge-mastery graph to unify safety, helpfulness, and pedagogy; designs a graph-augmented pipeline that infers prerequisite concepts, identifies mastery gaps, and routes generation between instructing and problem-solving via explicit gating.
en.result: Significantly improves safety under two pedagogical jailbreak settings while maintaining near-ceiling helpfulness.
en.help_to_user: Helps educational AI developers build safer and more useful tutoring systems, preventing students from misusing models to obtain answers.

idea_spark.idea: Combine pedagogical jailbreak defense with a knowledge mastery graph for dynamic generation routing.
idea_spark.risk: The gating strategy may be overly conservative, limiting legitimate problem-solving requests.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draws on the intersection of knowledge graph reasoning and safety alignment to build trustworthy educational AI.

papers.title: Objective Shaping with Hard Negatives: Windowed Partial AUC Optimization for RL-based LLM Recommenders
papers.summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) effectively optimizes Large Language Model (LLM)-based recommenders by contrasting positive and negative items. Empirically, training with beam-search negatives consistently outperforms random negatives, yet the mechanism is not well understood. We address this gap by analyzing the induced optimization objective and show that: (i) Under binary reward feedback, optimizing LLM recommenders with Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) is theoretically equivalent to maximizing the Area Under the ROC Curve (AUC), which is often misaligned with Top-$K$ recommendation; and (ii) Replacing random negatives with beam-search negatives reshapes the objective toward partial AUC, improving alignment with Top-$K$ metrics. Motivated by this perspective, we introduce Windowed Partial AUC (WPAUC), which constrains the false positive rate (FPR) to a window [$α,α+d$] to more directly align with Top-$K$ metrics. We further propose an efficient Threshold-Adjusted Windowed reweighting (TAWin) RL method for its optimization, enabling explicit control over the targeted Top-$K$ performance. Experiments on four real-world datasets validate the theory and deliver consistent state-of-the-art performance.

ai.tldr: 本文揭示了在RL优化LLM推荐器时，使用beam-search负样本等价于优化部分AUC，从而更好地对齐Top-K指标，并提出了WPAUC和TAWin方法。
ai.motivation: 现有RL训练LLM推荐器时，beam-search负样本效果优于随机负样本，但机制不明；且标准AUC优化与Top-K推荐存在偏差。
ai.method: 理论证明GRPO优化等价于AUC最大化，beam-search负样本将目标重塑为部分AUC；提出窗口化部分AUC（WPAUC）和阈值调整加权RL方法（TAWin）以直接优化Top-K性能。
ai.result: 在四个真实数据集上验证了理论，并取得了一致的最优性能。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解RL推荐器中负样本选择对优化目标的影响，并提供一种直接优化Top-K指标的高效方法。

idea_spark.idea: 将部分AUC的窗口概念与RL中的策略梯度结合，实现更精准的排序优化。
idea_spark.risk: 窗口参数α和d的选取可能对性能敏感，需要调参。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从理论等价性出发，将离散的Top-K指标转化为连续可微的优化目标，启发其他排序损失函数的设计。

en.tldr: This paper reveals that using beam-search negatives in RL-based LLM recommenders is equivalent to optimizing partial AUC, better aligning with Top-K metrics, and proposes WPAUC and TAWin methods.
en.motivation: Beam-search negatives empirically outperform random negatives in RL training of LLM recommenders, but the mechanism is unclear; standard AUC optimization misaligns with Top-K recommendation.
en.method: Theoretically proves GRPO optimization equals AUC maximization, and beam-search negatives reshape the objective to partial AUC; proposes Windowed Partial AUC (WPAUC) and Threshold-Adjusted Windowed reweighting (TAWin) RL method for direct Top-K optimization.
en.result: Validates the theory on four real-world datasets and achieves consistent state-of-the-art performance.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand the impact of negative sampling on optimization objectives in RL recommenders and provides an efficient method to directly optimize Top-K metrics.

idea_spark.idea: Combine the window concept of partial AUC with policy gradients in RL for more precise ranking optimization.
idea_spark.risk: The choice of window parameters α and d may be sensitive and require tuning.
idea_spark.inspiration: Starting from theoretical equivalence, converting discrete Top-K metrics into continuous differentiable objectives inspires design of other ranking loss functions.

papers.title: Grouped Pattern and Multi-Periodogram Algorithm for Range Estimation in ISAC Systems
papers.summary: This paper proposes a grouped pattern (GP) for sensing signals and a corresponding multi-periodogram algorithm for range estimation in integrated sensing and communications (ISAC) systems. GP partitions subcarriers into groups with an identical intra-group configuration replicated across groups, producing range profiles with periodic peaks and a structured multi-peak signature that improves low-SNR target detection. By identifying targets via cross-pattern peak validation, the proposed approach reduces missed detections and false alarms while requiring fewer dedicated sensing resources. Extensive simulations demonstrate a 16.5% extended detection range and a 61% reduced false alarm rate compared to conventional methods.

ai.tldr: 提出分组模式和多周期图算法，用于ISAC系统中的距离估计，通过跨模式峰值验证减少漏检和虚警。
ai.motivation: 现有ISAC系统在低信噪比下目标检测性能有限，且专用传感资源开销大。
ai.method: 将子载波分组为相同配置的组，产生具有周期性峰值的距离剖面，利用多周期图算法和跨模式峰值验证识别目标。
ai.result: 相比传统方法，检测距离延长16.5%，虚警率降低61%。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解ISAC系统中一种高效的距离估计方法，适用于低信噪比环境。

idea_spark.idea: 将分组模式与深度学习结合，自动学习最优分组配置以进一步提升检测性能。
idea_spark.risk: 分组模式可能引入额外的设计复杂度，且跨模式峰值验证在极端低信噪比下可能失效。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受OFDM子载波分组和周期图法启发，利用结构化多峰特征增强检测鲁棒性。

en.tldr: Proposes a grouped pattern and multi-periodogram algorithm for range estimation in ISAC systems, reducing missed detections and false alarms via cross-pattern peak validation.
en.motivation: Existing ISAC systems suffer from limited low-SNR target detection performance and high dedicated sensing resource overhead.
en.method: Partitions subcarriers into identically configured groups to produce range profiles with periodic peaks, and uses multi-periodogram algorithm with cross-pattern peak validation for target identification.
en.result: Achieves 16.5% extended detection range and 61% reduced false alarm rate compared to conventional methods.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient range estimation method for ISAC systems, especially beneficial under low-SNR conditions.

idea_spark.idea: Combine grouped pattern with deep learning to automatically learn optimal group configurations for further performance improvement.
idea_spark.risk: Grouped pattern may introduce additional design complexity, and cross-pattern peak validation might fail under extremely low SNR.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by OFDM subcarrier grouping and periodogram methods, leveraging structured multi-peak signatures to enhance detection robustness.

papers.title: From Global to Local: Rethinking CLIP Feature Aggregation for Person Re-Identification
papers.summary: CLIP-based person re-identification (ReID) methods aggregate spatial features into a single global \texttt{[CLS]} token optimized for image-text alignment rather than spatial selectivity, making representations fragile under occlusion and cross-camera variation. We propose SAGA-ReID, which reconstructs identity representations by aligning intermediate patch tokens with anchor vectors parameterized in CLIP's text embedding space -- emphasizing spatially stable evidence while suppressing corrupted or absent regions, without requiring textual descriptions of individual images. Controlled experiments isolate the aggregation mechanism under two qualitatively distinct conditions -- synthetic masking, where identity signal is absent, and realistic human distractors, where an overlapping person introduces semantically confusing signal -- with SAGA's advantage over global pooling growing substantially as occlusion increases across both conditions. Benchmark evaluations confirm consistent gains over CLIP-ReID across standard and occluded settings, with the largest improvements where global pooling is most unreliable: up to +10.6 Rank-1 on occluded benchmarks. SAGA's aggregation outperforms dedicated sequential patch aggregation on a stronger backbone, confirming that structured reconstruction addresses a bottleneck that backbone quality and architectural complexity alone cannot resolve. Code available at https://github.com/ipl-uw/Structured-Anchor-Guided-Aggregation-for-ReID.

ai.tldr: 提出SAGA-ReID方法，通过将中间补丁令牌与CLIP文本嵌入空间中的锚向量对齐，重构身份表示，在遮挡和跨相机变化下表现更鲁棒。
ai.motivation: 现有CLIP-based行人重识别方法使用全局[CLS]令牌聚合空间特征，缺乏空间选择性，在遮挡和跨相机变化下表示脆弱。
ai.method: SAGA-ReID通过将中间补丁令牌与CLIP文本嵌入空间中的锚向量对齐，重构身份表示，强调空间稳定证据并抑制损坏或缺失区域，无需单个图像的文本描述。
ai.result: 在标准与遮挡基准上一致优于CLIP-ReID，遮挡基准上Rank-1最高提升+10.6，且优于专用顺序补丁聚合方法。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种无需文本描述即可提升CLIP在遮挡场景下行人重识别鲁棒性的特征聚合策略。

idea_spark.idea: 利用文本嵌入空间中的锚向量引导补丁令牌对齐，实现结构化特征重建，避免全局池化的空间信息丢失。
idea_spark.risk: 锚向量的设计依赖CLIP文本空间，可能对未见过的类别或领域泛化性有限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从全局到局部的特征聚合思路可启发其他多模态任务中空间选择性表示的设计。

idea_spark.risk: 锚向量的设计依赖CLIP文本空间，可能对未见过的类别或领域泛化性有限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从全局到局部的特征聚合思路可启发其他多模态任务中空间选择性表示的设计。

en.tldr: Proposes SAGA-ReID, which reconstructs identity representations by aligning intermediate patch tokens with anchor vectors in CLIP's text embedding space, achieving robustness under occlusion and cross-camera variation.
en.motivation: Existing CLIP-based person ReID methods aggregate spatial features into a single global [CLS] token, lacking spatial selectivity and making representations fragile under occlusion and cross-camera variation.
en.method: SAGA-ReID reconstructs identity representations by aligning intermediate patch tokens with anchor vectors parameterized in CLIP's text embedding space, emphasizing spatially stable evidence while suppressing corrupted or absent regions, without requiring textual descriptions of individual images.
en.result: Consistent gains over CLIP-ReID on standard and occluded benchmarks, with up to +10.6 Rank-1 on occluded benchmarks, and outperforms dedicated sequential patch aggregation on a stronger backbone.
en.help_to_user: Provides a feature aggregation strategy that enhances CLIP's robustness to occlusion in person re-identification without requiring textual descriptions.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage anchor vectors in the text embedding space to guide patch token alignment, enabling structured feature reconstruction that avoids spatial information loss from global pooling.
idea_spark.risk: The design of anchor vectors depends on CLIP's text space, which may limit generalization to unseen categories or domains.
idea_spark.inspiration: The shift from global to local feature aggregation can inspire the design of spatially selective representations in other multimodal tasks.

papers.title: Accelerating Intra-Node GPU-to-GPU Communication Through Multi-Path Transfers with CUDA Graphs
papers.summary: Effective intra-node GPU communication is essential for optimizing performance in MPI-based HPC applications, especially when leveraging multiple communication paths. In this study, we propose a novel approach that integrates CUDA Graphs into the UCX framework to enhance intra-node multi-path point-to-point GPU communication. By concurrently leveraging multiple paths, including NVLink and PCIe through the host, and optimizing communication workflows using CUDA Graph, we achieve significant reductions in communication overhead and improve execution efficiency. To the best of our knowledge, our proposed approach is the first to seamlessly integrate CUDA Graphs into UCX. Through extensive experiments on a four-GPU node, our proposed CUDA Graph-based multi-path communication approach achieves up to a 2.95x bandwidth improvement, compared to the single-path UCX (UCT::CUDA-IPC), in GPU-to-GPU OMB bandwidth test when utilizing the host path and two other GPU paths, at message sizes up to 512MB.

ai.tldr: 提出将CUDA Graphs集成到UCX框架中，通过多路径（NVLink和PCIe）并发传输加速节点内GPU通信，实现最高2.95倍带宽提升。
ai.motivation: MPI高性能应用中节点内GPU通信效率至关重要，现有单路径方案存在瓶颈，需要利用多路径并发来降低开销。
ai.method: 在UCX框架中集成CUDA Graphs，将多路径（NVLink、PCIe通过主机）通信工作流优化为图执行，实现路径并发与调度。
ai.result: 在四GPU节点上，与单路径UCX相比，消息大小达512MB时带宽提升最高2.95倍。
ai.help_to_user: 若您从事GPU集群或MPI通信优化，本方法可指导如何利用CUDA Graphs实现多路径并发，提升节点内通信性能。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将CUDA Graphs用于动态路径选择，根据实时负载和拓扑自动切换最优路径组合。
idea_spark.risk: CUDA Graphs的图构建开销可能在小消息场景下抵消收益；多路径并发可能增加主机内存带宽竞争。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受UCX框架可扩展性启发，考虑将类似图调度思想应用于异构计算中CPU-GPU协同通信。

idea_spark.risk: CUDA Graphs的图构建开销可能在小消息场景下抵消收益；多路径并发可能增加主机内存带宽竞争。

en.tldr: Proposes integrating CUDA Graphs into the UCX framework to accelerate intra-node GPU communication via multi-path transfers (NVLink and PCIe), achieving up to 2.95x bandwidth improvement.
en.motivation: Intra-node GPU communication is critical for MPI-based HPC applications; existing single-path approaches are bottlenecked, necessitating concurrent multi-path utilization.
en.method: Integrates CUDA Graphs into UCX, optimizing multi-path (NVLink, PCIe via host) communication workflows as graph executions for concurrency and scheduling.
en.result: On a four-GPU node, achieves up to 2.95x bandwidth improvement over single-path UCX for message sizes up to 512MB.
en.help_to_user: If you work on GPU clusters or MPI communication optimization, this method guides how to leverage CUDA Graphs for multi-path concurrency to improve intra-node communication performance.

idea_spark.idea: Explore using CUDA Graphs for dynamic path selection, automatically switching optimal path combinations based on real-time load and topology.
idea_spark.risk: Graph construction overhead of CUDA Graphs may negate benefits for small messages; multi-path concurrency could increase host memory bandwidth contention.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by UCX framework extensibility, consider applying similar graph scheduling ideas to CPU-GPU cooperative communication in heterogeneous computing.

papers.title: Learning Reactive Human Motion Generation from Paired Interaction Data Using Transformer-Based Models
papers.summary: Recent advances in deep learning have enabled the generation of videos from textual descriptions as well as the prediction of future sequences from input videos. Similarly, in human motion modeling, motions can be generated from text or predicted from a single person's motion sequence. However, these approaches primarily focus on single-agent motion generation. In contrast, this study addresses the problem of generating the motion of one person based on the motion of another in interaction scenarios, where the two motions are mutually dependent. We construct a dataset of paired action-reaction motion sequences extracted from boxing match videos and investigate the effectiveness of Transformer-based models for this task. Specifically, we implement and compare three models: a simple Transformer, iTransformer, and Crossformer. In addition, we introduce a person ID embedding to explicitly distinguish between individuals, enabling the model to maintain structural consistency and better capture interaction dynamics. Experimental results show that the simple Transformer can generate plausible interaction-aware motions without suffering from posture collapse, while iTransformer and Crossformer accumulate errors over time, leading to unstable motion generation. Furthermore, the proposed person ID embedding contributes to preventing structural collapse and improving motion consistency. These results highlight the importance of explicitly modeling individual identity in interaction-aware motion generation.

ai.tldr: 本研究利用配对交互数据（拳击视频）和Transformer模型，生成基于他人动作的反应性人体运动，并引入人物ID嵌入以保持结构一致性。
ai.motivation: 现有运动生成方法多针对单智能体，缺乏对交互场景中两人运动相互依赖关系的建模。
ai.method: 构建拳击配对动作-反应运动序列数据集，比较简单Transformer、iTransformer和Crossformer三种模型，并引入人物ID嵌入区分个体。
ai.result: 简单Transformer能生成合理的交互感知运动且无姿态崩溃，iTransformer和Crossformer随时间累积误差；人物ID嵌入有助于防止结构崩溃并提升运动一致性。
ai.help_to_user: 为交互式人体运动生成任务提供了有效的基线模型和身份嵌入策略，可用于虚拟角色互动、体育分析等场景。

idea_spark.idea: 结合图神经网络显式建模交互拓扑，或引入对抗训练增强长期运动稳定性。
idea_spark.risk: 简单Transformer在复杂交互（如非对称角色）中可能失效，需验证泛化性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从拳击配对数据出发，可拓展至其他强交互领域（如双人武术、社交手势）。

idea_spark.idea: 结合图神经网络显式建模交互拓扑，或引入对抗训练增强长期运动稳定性。

en.tldr: This study generates reactive human motion based on another person's motion in interaction scenarios using paired boxing match data and Transformer models, with a person ID embedding to maintain structural consistency.
en.motivation: Existing motion generation methods focus on single agents, lacking modeling of mutual dependence in two-person interactions.
en.method: Constructs a paired action-reaction motion dataset from boxing videos, compares simple Transformer, iTransformer, and Crossformer, and introduces person ID embedding to distinguish individuals.
en.result: Simple Transformer generates plausible interaction-aware motions without posture collapse, while iTransformer and Crossformer accumulate errors over time; person ID embedding helps prevent structural collapse and improves motion consistency.
en.help_to_user: Provides a baseline model and identity embedding strategy for interactive human motion generation, applicable to virtual character interaction and sports analysis.

idea_spark.idea: Combine graph neural networks to explicitly model interaction topology, or use adversarial training to enhance long-term motion stability.
idea_spark.risk: Simple Transformer may fail in complex interactions (e.g., asymmetric roles); generalization needs verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Starting from boxing paired data, the approach can extend to other strong interaction domains like martial arts or social gestures.

papers.title: Thinking Without Words: Efficient Latent Reasoning with Abstract Chain-of-Thought
papers.summary: While long, explicit chains-of-thought (CoT) have proven effective on complex reasoning tasks, they are costly to generate during inference. Non-verbal reasoning methods have emerged with shorter generation lengths by leveraging continuous representations, yet their performance lags behind verbalized CoT. We propose $\textbf{Abstract Chain-of-Thought}$, a discrete latent reasoning post-training mechanism in which the language model produces a short sequence of tokens from a reserved vocabulary in lieu of a natural language CoT, before generating a response. To make previously unseen ''abstract'' tokens useful, we introduce a policy iteration-style warm-up loop that alternates between (i.) bottlenecking from a verbal CoT via masking and performing supervised fine-tuning, and (ii.) self-distillation by training the model to generate abstract tokens from the prompt alone via constrained decoding with the codebook. After warm-up, we optimize the generation of abstract sequences with warm-started reinforcement learning under constrained decoding. Abstract-CoT achieves up to $11.6\times$ fewer reasoning tokens while demonstrating comparable performance across mathematical reasoning, instruction-following, and multi-hop reasoning, and generalizes across language model families. We also find an emergent power law distribution over the abstract vocabulary, akin to those seen in natural language, that evolves across the training phases. Our findings highlight the potential for post-training latent reasoning mechanisms that enable efficient inference through a learned abstract reasoning language.

ai.tldr: 提出抽象思维链（Abstract-CoT），用少量离散抽象令牌替代自然语言思维链，在数学推理等任务上推理令牌减少11.6倍，性能相当。
ai.motivation: 显式思维链推理成本高，而连续表示的非语言推理性能不足，需要一种高效且性能不降的离散潜在推理机制。
ai.method: 通过策略迭代预热循环（交替瓶颈化监督微调和自蒸馏）学习抽象令牌，再用强化学习优化抽象序列生成，全程使用约束解码。
ai.result: 推理令牌减少高达11.6倍，在数学推理、指令跟随和多跳推理上性能与显式思维链相当，且跨模型族泛化。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过后训练机制让语言模型学会用少量抽象令牌进行高效推理，降低推理成本。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将抽象令牌与连续潜在表示结合，或用于多模态推理。
idea_spark.risk: 抽象令牌的可解释性差，可能难以调试或对齐人类意图。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受自然语言词频幂律分布启发，发现抽象令牌也呈现类似分布，提示潜在推理可能遵循类似的语言规律。

zh.motivation: 显式思维链推理成本高，而连续表示的非语言推理性能不足，需要一种高效且性能不降的离散潜在推理机制。

zh.result: 推理令牌减少高达11.6倍，在数学推理、指令跟随和多跳推理上性能与显式思维链相当，且跨模型族泛化。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过后训练机制让语言模型学会用少量抽象令牌进行高效推理，降低推理成本。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将抽象令牌与连续潜在表示结合，或用于多模态推理。
idea_spark.risk: 抽象令牌的可解释性差，可能难以调试或对齐人类意图。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受自然语言词频幂律分布启发，发现抽象令牌也呈现类似分布，提示潜在推理可能遵循类似的语言规律。

en.tldr: Proposes Abstract Chain-of-Thought (Abstract-CoT), replacing natural language CoT with a short sequence of discrete abstract tokens, achieving up to 11.6× fewer reasoning tokens with comparable performance on math, instruction-following, and multi-hop reasoning.
en.motivation: Explicit CoT is costly at inference, while continuous non-verbal reasoning underperforms; a discrete latent reasoning mechanism is needed for efficiency without sacrificing accuracy.
en.method: Uses a policy iteration warm-up loop alternating between bottlenecked supervised fine-tuning and self-distillation with constrained decoding, then optimizes abstract sequence generation via warm-started reinforcement learning.
en.result: Reduces reasoning tokens by up to 11.6× while matching verbal CoT performance across multiple reasoning tasks and generalizing across model families.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how post-training can teach language models to reason efficiently using a small set of abstract tokens, reducing inference cost.

idea_spark.idea: Explore combining abstract tokens with continuous latent representations or applying to multimodal reasoning.
idea_spark.risk: Abstract tokens may lack interpretability, making debugging or alignment difficult.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the power-law distribution in natural language, the abstract vocabulary also exhibits a similar distribution, suggesting latent reasoning may follow analogous linguistic patterns.

papers.title: Transformer-Based Rhythm Quantization of Performance MIDI Using Beat Annotations
papers.summary: Rhythm transcription is a key subtask of notation-level Automatic Music Transcription (AMT). While deep learning models have been extensively used for detecting the metrical grid in audio and MIDI performances, beat-based rhythm quantization remains largely unexplored. In this work, we introduce a novel deep learning approach for quantizing MIDI performances using a priori beat information. Our method leverages the transformer architecture to effectively process synchronized score and performance data for training a quantization model. Key components of our approach include dataset preparation, a beat-based pre-quantization method to align performance and score times within a unified framework, and a MIDI tokenizer tailored for this task. We adapt a transformer model based on the T5 architecture to meet the specific requirements of rhythm quantization. The model is evaluated using a set of score-level metrics designed for objective assessment of quantization performance. Through systematic evaluation, we optimize both data representation and model architecture. Additionally, we apply performance and score augmentations, such as transposition, note deletion, and performance-side time jitter, to enhance the model's robustness. Finally, a qualitative analysis compares our model's quantization performance against state-of-the-art probabilistic and deep-learning models on various example pieces. Our model achieves an onset F1-score of 97.3% and a note value accuracy of 83.3% on the ASAP dataset. It generalizes well across time signatures, including those not seen during training, and produces readable score output. Fine-tuning on instrument-specific datasets further improves performance by capturing characteristic rhythmic and melodic patterns. This work contributes a robust and flexible framework for beat-based MIDI quantization using transformer models.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于Transformer的MIDI节奏量化方法，利用节拍注释将演奏MIDI量化为乐谱表示。
ai.motivation: 现有深度学习方法多用于检测节拍网格，但基于节拍的节奏量化研究不足。
ai.method: 采用T5架构的Transformer模型，结合节拍预量化、MIDI分词器、数据增强（移调、删音、时间抖动）进行训练。
ai.result: 在ASAP数据集上达到97.3%的起始F1分数和83.3%的音符值准确率，对未见拍号泛化良好。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户将MIDI演奏自动转换为可读乐谱，提升音乐转录的准确性和鲁棒性。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将节拍预量化与Transformer结合，用于实时音乐转录系统。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖高质量节拍注释，在无注释数据上可能性能下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受T5模型在序列到序列任务中的成功启发，将其适配到音乐量化领域。

zh.tldr: 提出一种基于Transformer的MIDI节奏量化方法，利用节拍注释将演奏MIDI量化为乐谱表示。
zh.motivation: 现有深度学习方法多用于检测节拍网格，但基于节拍的节奏量化研究不足。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将节拍预量化与Transformer结合，用于实时音乐转录系统。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖高质量节拍注释，在无注释数据上可能性能下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受T5模型在序列到序列任务中的成功启发，将其适配到音乐量化领域。

en.tldr: Proposes a transformer-based rhythm quantization method for performance MIDI using beat annotations to produce readable scores.
en.motivation: Existing deep learning models focus on metrical grid detection, but beat-based rhythm quantization is underexplored.
en.method: Adapts a T5 transformer with beat-based pre-quantization, MIDI tokenizer, and augmentations (transposition, note deletion, time jitter).
en.result: Achieves 97.3% onset F1 and 83.3% note value accuracy on ASAP dataset, generalizing to unseen time signatures.
en.help_to_user: Enables automatic conversion of MIDI performances into readable sheet music with high accuracy and robustness.

idea_spark.idea: Explore real-time music transcription by integrating beat pre-quantization with transformer models.
idea_spark.risk: Performance may degrade on data without reliable beat annotations.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by T5's success in sequence-to-sequence tasks, adapted for music quantization.

papers.title: QuantClaw: Precision Where It Matters for OpenClaw
papers.summary: Autonomous agent systems such as OpenClaw introduce significant efficiency challenges due to long-context inputs and multi-turn reasoning. This results in prohibitively high computational and monetary costs in real-world development. While quantization is a standard approach for reducing cost and latency, its impact on agent performance in realistic scenarios remains unclear. In this work, we analyze quantization sensitivity across diverse complex workflows over OpenClaw, and show that precision requirements are highly task-dependent. Based on this observation, we propose QuantClaw, a plug-and-play precision routing plugin that dynamically assigns precision according to task characteristics. QuantClaw routes lightweight tasks to lower-cost configurations while preserving higher precision for demanding workloads, saving cost and accelerating inference without increasing user complexity. Experiments show that our QuantClaw maintains or improves task performance while reducing both latency and computational cost. Across a range of agent tasks, it achieves up to 21.4% cost savings and 15.7% latency reduction on GLM-5 (FP8 baseline). These results highlight the benefit of treating precision as a dynamic resource in agent systems.

ai.tldr: 提出QuantClaw，一种动态精度路由插件，根据任务特性分配不同精度，在保持或提升性能的同时降低成本和延迟。
ai.motivation: OpenClaw等自主代理系统因长上下文和多轮推理导致计算和货币成本过高，而量化对代理性能的影响尚不明确。
ai.method: 分析OpenClaw上多种复杂工作流的量化敏感性，发现精度需求高度依赖任务；据此设计QuantClaw插件，将轻量任务路由至低成本配置，高精度保留给高负载任务。
ai.result: 在GLM-5（FP8基线）上实现最高21.4%成本节省和15.7%延迟降低，同时保持或提升任务性能。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户在不增加复杂度的情况下，通过动态精度分配降低代理系统的推理成本和延迟。

idea_spark.idea: 将精度视为可动态调度的资源，而非固定属性，实现按需分配。
idea_spark.risk: 任务特性分类可能引入额外开销，且极端任务可能误判精度需求。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从量化敏感性分析出发，启发更细粒度的资源管理策略，如混合精度调度与任务预测结合。

zh.tldr: 提出QuantClaw，一种动态精度路由插件，根据任务特性分配不同精度，在保持或提升性能的同时降低成本和延迟。
zh.motivation: OpenClaw等自主代理系统因长上下文和多轮推理导致计算和货币成本过高，而量化对代理性能的影响尚不明确。

zh.result: 在GLM-5（FP8基线）上实现最高21.4%成本节省和15.7%延迟降低，同时保持或提升任务性能。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户在不增加复杂度的情况下，通过动态精度分配降低代理系统的推理成本和延迟。

idea_spark.idea: 将精度视为可动态调度的资源，而非固定属性，实现按需分配。
idea_spark.risk: 任务特性分类可能引入额外开销，且极端任务可能误判精度需求。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从量化敏感性分析出发，启发更细粒度的资源管理策略，如混合精度调度与任务预测结合。

en.tldr: Proposes QuantClaw, a plug-and-play precision routing plugin that dynamically assigns precision based on task characteristics, reducing cost and latency while maintaining or improving performance.
en.motivation: Autonomous agent systems like OpenClaw suffer from high computational and monetary costs due to long-context inputs and multi-turn reasoning, and the impact of quantization on agent performance is unclear.
en.method: Analyzes quantization sensitivity across diverse OpenClaw workflows, revealing task-dependent precision requirements; designs QuantClaw to route lightweight tasks to low-cost configurations and preserve high precision for demanding workloads.
en.result: Achieves up to 21.4% cost savings and 15.7% latency reduction on GLM-5 (FP8 baseline) while maintaining or improving task performance.
en.help_to_user: Helps users reduce inference cost and latency in agent systems without added complexity, via dynamic precision allocation.

idea_spark.idea: Treat precision as a dynamically schedulable resource rather than a fixed attribute, enabling on-demand allocation.
idea_spark.risk: Task classification may introduce overhead, and extreme tasks might be misjudged for precision needs.
idea_spark.inspiration: Starting from quantization sensitivity analysis, inspires finer-grained resource management strategies, such as combining mixed-precision scheduling with task prediction.

papers.title: Knowledge-driven Augmentation and Retrieval for Integrative Temporal Adaptation
papers.summary: Time introduces fundamental challenges in model development and deployment: models are usually trained on historical data while deployed on future data where semantic distributions and domain knowledge may evolve. Unfortunately, existing studies either overlook temporal shifts or hardly capture rich shifting patterns of both semantic and knowledge. We develop Knowledge-driven Augmentation and Retrieval for Integrative Temporal Adaptation (KARITA) to capture diverse temporal shifts (e.g., uncertainty and feature shift), construct and integrate rich knowledge sources (e.g., medical ontology like MeSH), and leverage shifting insights for selecting-retrieval augmented learning. We evaluate KARITA on classification tasks across multiple domains, clinical, legal, and scientific corpora, demonstrating consistent improvements across multiple domains with temporal adaptation. Our results show that knowledge integration can be more critical and effective in temporal augmentation and learning.

ai.tldr: 提出KARITA方法，通过知识驱动的增强与检索，应对时间变化带来的语义和知识分布偏移，在临床、法律、科学文本分类中取得一致提升。
ai.motivation: 现有方法忽略时间偏移或难以捕捉语义与知识的复杂变化模式。
ai.method: 构建并整合丰富知识源（如MeSH医学本体），利用偏移洞察进行选择-检索增强学习。
ai.result: 在多个领域（临床、法律、科学）的分类任务上，时间适应性能一致提升。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何利用领域知识增强模型对时间变化的适应能力。

idea_spark.idea: 将领域本体（如法律、科学）与检索增强结合，动态更新模型知识库。
idea_spark.risk: 知识源构建成本高，且本体更新滞后可能引入噪声。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用时间偏移模式指导检索策略，而非简单固定检索。

zh.tldr: 提出KARITA方法，通过知识驱动的增强与检索，应对时间变化带来的语义和知识分布偏移，在临床、法律、科学文本分类中取得一致提升。
zh.motivation: 现有方法忽略时间偏移或难以捕捉语义与知识的复杂变化模式。

idea_spark.risk: 知识源构建成本高，且本体更新滞后可能引入噪声。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用时间偏移模式指导检索策略，而非简单固定检索。

en.tldr: Proposes KARITA, a knowledge-driven augmentation and retrieval method for temporal adaptation, achieving consistent improvements on clinical, legal, and scientific text classification.
en.motivation: Existing methods overlook temporal shifts or fail to capture complex patterns of semantic and knowledge evolution.
en.method: Constructs and integrates rich knowledge sources (e.g., MeSH ontology) and leverages shift insights for selective retrieval-augmented learning.
en.result: Consistent improvements across multiple domains (clinical, legal, scientific) in classification tasks with temporal adaptation.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to leverage domain knowledge to enhance model adaptation to temporal changes.

idea_spark.idea: Combine domain ontologies (e.g., legal, scientific) with retrieval augmentation to dynamically update the model's knowledge base.
idea_spark.risk: High cost of constructing knowledge sources and potential noise from outdated ontologies.
idea_spark.inspiration: Use temporal shift patterns to guide retrieval strategies rather than fixed retrieval.

papers.title: SAMIDARE: Advanced Tracking-by-Segmentation for Dense Scenarios
papers.summary: Automated sports analysis demands robust multi-object tracking (MOT), yet segmentation-based methods often struggle with mask errors and ID switches in dense scenes. We propose SAMIDARE, a framework that enhances SAM2MOT for crowded scenes through three key components: (1) density-aware mask re-generation and (2) selective memory updates, both for adaptive mask control to preserve target feature integrity, and (3) state-aware association and new track initialization, which improves robustness under mutual occlusions and frequent frame-out events. Evaluated on the SportsMOT dataset, SAMIDARE achieves state-of-the-art performance, outperforming the baseline by 2.5 HOTA and 4.2 IDF1 points on the validation set. These results demonstrate that adaptive feature management using mask control and state-aware association provide a robust and efficient solution for dense sports tracking. Code is available at https://github.com/ZabuZabuZabu/SAMIDARE

ai.tldr: 提出SAMIDARE框架，通过密度感知掩码重生成、选择性记忆更新和状态感知关联，提升密集场景下的分割跟踪性能。
ai.motivation: 现有基于分割的多目标跟踪方法在密集场景中因掩码错误和ID切换而表现不佳。
ai.method: 包含三个关键组件：密度感知掩码重生成、选择性记忆更新、状态感知关联与新轨迹初始化。
ai.result: 在SportsMOT数据集上达到最优，验证集HOTA提升2.5，IDF1提升4.2。

idea_spark.inspiration: Leveraging scene density to guide adaptive adjustments in tracking components; similar ideas applicable to other vision tasks.

papers.title: How Many Visual Levers Drive Urban Perception? Interventional Counterfactuals via Multiple Localised Edits
papers.summary: Street-view perception models predict subjective attributes such as safety at scale, but remain correlational: they do not identify which localized visual changes would plausibly shift human judgement for a specific scene. We propose a lever-based interventional counterfactual framework that recasts scene-level explainability as a bounded search over structured counterfactual edits. Each lever specifies a semantic concept, spatial support, intervention direction, and constrained edit template. Candidate edits are generated through prompt-conditioned image editing and retained only if they satisfy validity checks for same-place preservation, locality, realism, and plausibility. In a pilot across 50 scenes from five cities, the framework reveals preliminary proxy-based directional patterns and a practical failure taxonomy under prompt-only editing, with Mobility Infrastructure and Physical Maintenance showing the largest auxiliary safety shifts. Human pairwise judgements remain the ground-truth endpoint for future validation.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于杠杆的干预性反事实框架，通过结构化编辑识别影响城市感知的局部视觉变化。
ai.motivation: 现有街景感知模型仅能预测主观属性，但无法识别哪些局部视觉变化会改变人类判断。
ai.method: 定义语义概念、空间支持、干预方向和约束编辑模板的杠杆，通过提示条件图像编辑生成候选编辑，并验证其同一地点保持、局部性、真实性和合理性。
ai.result: 在五个城市50个场景的试点中，发现移动基础设施和物理维护对安全感知影响最大，并总结了提示编辑的失败分类。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解城市感知模型中哪些视觉特征驱动人类判断，为城市规划提供可操作的见解。

idea_spark.idea: 结合人类成对判断作为真实标签，验证反事实编辑的有效性。
idea_spark.risk: 提示编辑可能产生不真实或不合理的编辑，影响结果可靠性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用大规模语言模型生成编辑模板，自动化反事实生成过程。

en.tldr: Proposes a lever-based interventional counterfactual framework to identify localized visual changes that drive urban perception via structured edits.
en.motivation: Current street-view perception models predict subjective attributes but fail to identify which localized visual changes would shift human judgment.
en.method: Defines levers with semantic concept, spatial support, intervention direction, and constrained edit template; generates candidate edits via prompt-conditioned image editing and validates for same-place preservation, locality, realism, and plausibility.
en.result: In a pilot across 50 scenes from five cities, Mobility Infrastructure and Physical Maintenance show the largest safety shifts; a failure taxonomy under prompt-only editing is provided.

idea_spark.risk: Prompt-based editing may produce unrealistic or implausible edits, affecting result reliability.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverage large language models to generate edit templates, automating the counterfactual generation process.

papers.title: A general optimization solver based on OP-to-MaxSAT reduction
papers.summary: Optimization problems are fundamental in diverse fields, such as engineering, economics, and scientific computing. However, current algorithms are mostly designed for specific problem types and exhibit limited generality in solving multiple types of optimization problems. To enhance generality, we propose an automated reduction method named OP-to-MaxSAT reduction and a general optimization solver based on OP-to-MaxSAT reduction (GORED). GORED unifies the solving of multiple types of optimization problems by reducing the problems from optimization problems to MaxSAT instances in polynomial time and solving them using the state-of-the-art MaxSAT solver. The generality and solution quality of GORED are validated through experiments on 136 instances across 11 types of optimization problems. Experimental results demonstrate that GORED not only successfully solves a wide range of optimization problems but also yields solutions comparable in quality to those from existing methods, with no statistically significant differences observed. By introducing automated reduction, this work shifts the paradigm of optimization solvers from designing specialized algorithms for each problem type to employing a single algorithm for diverse problems. As a result, advances in this single algorithm can now drive progress in a wide range of optimization problems across various domains.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于OP-to-MaxSAT归约的通用优化求解器GORED，将多种优化问题统一归约为MaxSAT实例求解。
ai.motivation: 现有优化算法针对特定问题类型设计，缺乏解决多种优化问题的通用性。
ai.method: 提出OP-to-MaxSAT自动归约方法，将优化问题多项式时间归约为MaxSAT实例，再用先进MaxSAT求解器求解。
ai.result: 在11类136个实例上验证，GORED成功求解广泛优化问题，解质量与现有方法无显著差异。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种通用优化求解思路，用户无需为每种问题设计专用算法。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将更多优化问题（如多目标优化）归约为MaxSAT，扩展GORED适用范围。
idea_spark.risk: 归约过程可能引入额外计算开销，影响大规模问题求解效率。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴此自动归约思想，设计其他领域的通用求解器，如自动推理或调度问题。

en.tldr: Proposes a general optimization solver GORED based on OP-to-MaxSAT reduction, unifying multiple optimization problems into MaxSAT instances.
en.motivation: Existing optimization algorithms are designed for specific problem types and lack generality across diverse optimization problems.
en.method: Introduces an automated OP-to-MaxSAT reduction method that reduces optimization problems to MaxSAT instances in polynomial time, solved by a state-of-the-art MaxSAT solver.
en.result: Validated on 136 instances across 11 problem types; GORED successfully solves a wide range of problems with solution quality comparable to existing methods.
en.help_to_user: Provides a general optimization approach, eliminating the need for problem-specific algorithm design.

idea_spark.idea: Explore reducing more optimization problems (e.g., multi-objective optimization) to MaxSAT to extend GORED's applicability.
idea_spark.risk: The reduction process may introduce additional computational overhead, affecting efficiency on large-scale problems.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverage this automated reduction idea to design general solvers in other domains, such as automated reasoning or scheduling.

papers.title: Selective Depthwise Separable Convolution for Lightweight Joint Source-Channel Coding in Wireless Image Transmission
papers.summary: Depthwise separable convolutional (DSConv) layers have been successfully applied to deep learning (DL)-based joint source-channel coding (JSCC) schemes to reduce computational complexity. However, a systematic investigation of the layerwise and ratio-wise replacement of standard convolutional (Conv) layers with DSConv layers in JSCC systems for wireless image transmission remains largely unexplored. In this letter, we propose a configurable lightweight JSCC framework that incorporates a selective replacement strategy, enabling flexible substitution of standard Conv layers with DSConv layers at various layer positions and replacement ratios. By adjusting the proportion of layers replaced, we achieve different model compression levels and analyze their impact on reconstruction performance. Furthermore, we investigate how replacements at different encoder and decoder depths influence reconstruction quality under a fixed replacement ratio. Our results show that Conv-to-DSConv replacement at intermediate layers achieves a favorable complexity-performance trade-off, revealing layer-wise redundancy in DL-based JSCC systems. Extensive experiments further demonstrate that the proposed framework achieves substantial parameter reduction with only slight performance degradation, enabling flexible complexity-performance trade-offs for resource-constrained edge devices.

ai.tldr: 提出一种可配置的轻量级联合源信道编码框架，通过选择性替换标准卷积为深度可分离卷积，实现灵活的参数压缩与性能折中。
ai.motivation: 现有基于深度学习的联合源信道编码方案计算复杂度过高，而深度可分离卷积虽能降低复杂度，但缺乏对替换位置和比例的系统研究。
ai.method: 设计选择性替换策略，在编码器/解码器的不同层和不同比例下，用深度可分离卷积替换标准卷积，并分析替换深度对重建质量的影响。
ai.result: 中间层替换可获得最佳的复杂度-性能折中，显著减少参数量且性能损失很小，适用于资源受限的边缘设备。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过选择性替换卷积层来平衡模型压缩与传输质量，为边缘设备上的无线图像传输提供实用指导。

idea_spark.idea: 可进一步研究自适应替换比例，根据信道条件或设备资源动态调整替换层数。
idea_spark.risk: 替换比例过高可能导致重建质量严重下降，需谨慎选择替换位置。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受卷积层冗余性启发，可探索其他结构（如注意力模块）的类似选择性替换。

en.tldr: Proposes a configurable lightweight joint source-channel coding framework that selectively replaces standard convolutions with depthwise separable convolutions for flexible parameter compression and performance trade-offs.
en.motivation: Existing deep learning-based joint source-channel coding schemes suffer from high computational complexity; depthwise separable convolutions reduce complexity but lack systematic study on replacement positions and ratios.
en.method: Designs a selective replacement strategy that substitutes standard convolutions with depthwise separable convolutions at different encoder/decoder depths and ratios, analyzing the impact on reconstruction quality.
en.result: Replacement at intermediate layers achieves the best complexity-performance trade-off, significantly reducing parameters with minimal performance degradation, suitable for resource-constrained edge devices.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to balance model compression and transmission quality via selective convolution replacement, providing practical guidance for wireless image transmission on edge devices.

idea_spark.idea: Further research could explore adaptive replacement ratios that dynamically adjust based on channel conditions or device resources.
idea_spark.risk: Excessive replacement ratios may cause severe degradation in reconstruction quality; careful selection of replacement positions is necessary.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by layer-wise redundancy, similar selective replacement could be explored for other structures such as attention modules.

papers.title: Fine-Grained Analysis of Shared Syntactic Mechanisms in Language Models
papers.summary: While language models demonstrate sophisticated syntactic capabilities, the extent to which their internal mechanisms align with cross-constructional principles studied in linguistics remains poorly understood. This study investigates whether models employ shared neural mechanisms across different syntactic constructions by applying causal interpretability methods at a granular level. Focusing on filler-gap dependencies and negative polarity item (NPI) licensing, we utilize activation patching to identify the functional roles of specific attention heads and MLP blocks. Our results reveal a highly localized and shared mechanism for filler-gap dependencies located in the early to middle layers, whereas NPI processing exhibits no such unified mechanism. Furthermore, we find that these mechanisms identified by activation patching generalize to out-of-distribution, while distributed alignment search, a supervised interpretability method, is susceptible to overfitting on narrow linguistic distributions. Finally, we validate our findings by demonstrating that the manipulation of the identified components improves model performance on acceptability judgment benchmarks.

ai.tldr: 通过因果可解释性方法，发现语言模型在填充词-空位依赖上共享局部神经机制，而否定极性项处理则无统一机制，且激活修补识别的机制可泛化到分布外数据。
ai.motivation: 探究语言模型内部机制是否与语言学中的跨构式原则一致，理解其句法能力的底层共享性。
ai.method: 使用激活修补（activation patching）对特定注意力头和MLP块进行功能角色识别，并对比分布式对齐搜索（distributed alignment search）。
ai.result: 填充词-空位依赖在早期到中层存在高度局部化和共享的机制；否定极性项处理无统一机制；激活修补识别的机制可泛化，而监督方法易过拟合。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解语言模型句法处理的内部机制及其泛化能力，为可解释性和模型改进提供指导。

idea_spark.idea: 利用激活修补识别共享机制，设计更高效的模型架构或训练策略。
idea_spark.risk: 机制可能因模型架构或训练数据不同而失效，需验证跨模型泛化性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从语言学理论出发，用因果方法验证神经网络的认知合理性。

en.tldr: Using causal interpretability, we find that language models share localized neural mechanisms for filler-gap dependencies but not for negative polarity item licensing, and activation-patching-identified mechanisms generalize out-of-distribution.
en.motivation: To investigate whether internal mechanisms of language models align with cross-constructional principles in linguistics.
en.method: Activation patching to identify functional roles of attention heads and MLP blocks, compared with distributed alignment search.
en.result: Filler-gap dependencies exhibit highly localized and shared mechanisms in early-to-middle layers; NPI processing lacks unified mechanism; activation patching generalizes while supervised method overfits.
en.help_to_user: Provides insights into the shared syntactic mechanisms in LLMs and their generalization, aiding interpretability and model improvement.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage identified shared mechanisms to design more efficient architectures or training strategies.
idea_spark.risk: Mechanisms may not generalize across different model architectures or training data.
idea_spark.inspiration: Use linguistic theory to guide causal validation of neural network cognitive plausibility.

papers.title: Unitary Quadratic Quantum Gravity in 4D
papers.summary: In quadratic gravity, with a positive Weyl squared coefficient, the extra spin-2 sector is shown to correspond to a dual inverted harmonic oscillator, instead of a ghost. Using the Wightman spectrum condition, we prove that the associated Källén--Lehmann spectral density vanishes, reflecting the absence of a normalizable ground state and the spacelike nature of the propagator pole. This uniquely fixes the propagator to a principal value form as a theorem, not a prescription. The optical theorem is satisfied, the dual IHO spin-2 is not an asymptotic state, and gives only virtual contributions at all loop orders. As a result, unitarity is preserved consistently with renormalizability.

ai.tldr: 在四维二次引力中，正Weyl平方系数对应的额外自旋-2扇区被证明是双对倒谐振子而非鬼场，通过Wightman谱条件证明其谱密度为零，唯一确定传播子为主值形式，满足光学定理且保持幺正性与可重整化性。
ai.motivation: 解决二次引力中自旋-2鬼场导致的幺正性破坏问题。
ai.method: 利用Wightman谱条件分析谱密度，证明传播子极点类空，强制传播子取主值形式。
ai.result: 自旋-2扇区为虚粒子，不贡献渐近态，所有圈阶仅虚贡献，幺正性成立。

en.tldr: In 4D quadratic gravity with positive Weyl squared coefficient, the extra spin-2 sector is shown to be a dual inverted harmonic oscillator rather than a ghost. The Wightman spectrum condition forces the spectral density to vanish, uniquely fixing the propagator to a principal value form. The optical theorem holds, the spin-2 is virtual at all loops, preserving unitarity and renormalizability.
en.motivation: To resolve the unitarity problem caused by the spin-2 ghost in quadratic gravity.
en.method: Analyze the spectral density using the Wightman spectrum condition; show the propagator pole is spacelike, forcing a principal value prescription.
en.result: The spin-2 sector contributes only virtually, never as an asymptotic state, and unitarity is preserved at all loop orders.
en.help_to_user: Provides a unitary and renormalizable framework for quantum gravity models.

idea_spark.idea: Treat the extra spin-2 as an inverted harmonic oscillator and use spectral conditions to eliminate unphysical states.
idea_spark.risk: The principal value propagator may violate causality; further checks are needed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Reinterpret ghosts via duality with inverted oscillators.

papers.title: When AI Speaks, Whose Values Does It Express? A Cross-Cultural Audit of Individualism-Collectivism Bias in Large Language Models
papers.summary: When you ask an AI assistant for advice about your career, your marriage, or a conflict with your family, does it give you the same answer regardless of where you are from? We tested this systematically by presenting three leading AI systems (Claude Sonnet 4.5, GPT-5.4, and Gemini 2.5 Flash) with ten real-life personal dilemmas, framed for users from 10 countries across 5 continents in 7 languages (n=840 scored responses). We compared AI advice against World Values Survey Wave 7 data measuring what people in each country actually believe.   All three AI systems consistently gave Western-style, individualist advice even to users from societies that prioritize family, community, and authority, significantly more so than local values would predict (mean gap +0.76 on a 1-5 scale; t=15.65, p<0.001). The gap is largest for Nigeria (+1.85) and India (+0.82). Japan is the sole exception: AI systems treated Japanese users as more group-oriented than surveys show, revealing that AI encodes outdated stereotypes. Claude and GPT-5.4 show nearly identical bias magnitude, while Gemini is lower but still significant. The models diverge in mechanism: Claude shifts further collectivist in the user's native language; Gemini shifts more individualist; GPT-5.4 responds only to stated country identity. These findings point to a systemic homogenization of values across frontier AI. Data, code, and scoring pipeline are openly released.

ai.tldr: 对三个前沿AI系统进行跨文化审计，发现它们普遍输出西方个人主义价值观，与当地实际价值观存在显著偏差。
ai.motivation: AI助手在个人建议中可能隐含文化偏见，但缺乏系统性跨文化验证。
ai.method: 用10个真实困境、10个国家、7种语言向Claude、GPT-5.4、Gemini提问，并与世界价值观调查数据对比。
ai.result: 所有模型一致偏向个人主义，尼日利亚和印度偏差最大；日本被刻板地视为更集体主义；模型机制不同。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户意识到AI建议可能不反映其文化背景，避免盲目采纳。

idea_spark.idea: 开发文化自适应提示模板，在输入中显式注入用户文化背景以校准输出。
idea_spark.risk: 过度校正可能引入新偏见，或使模型对文化标签过于敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合世界价值观调查数据，构建文化感知的AI对齐基准。

en.motivation: AI assistants may embed cultural biases in personal advice, but systematic cross-cultural evidence is lacking.
en.method: Presented 10 personal dilemmas to Claude, GPT-5.4, and Gemini across 10 countries and 7 languages, comparing responses to World Values Survey data.

idea_spark.inspiration: Leverage World Values Survey data to build culture-aware alignment benchmarks for LLMs.

papers.title: Source-Modality Monitoring in Vision-Language Models
papers.summary: We define and investigate source-modality monitoring -- the ability of multimodal models to track and communicate the input source from which pieces of information originate. We consider source-modality monitoring as an instance of the more general binding problem, and evaluate the extent to which models exploit syntactic vs. semantic signals in order to bind words like image in a user-provided prompt to specific components of their input and context (i.e., actual images). Across experiments spanning 11 vision-language models (VLMs) performing target-modality information retrieval tasks, we find that both syntactic and semantic signals play an important role, but that the latter tend to outweigh the former in cases when modalities are highly distinct distributionally. We discuss the implications of these findings for model robustness, and in the context of increasingly multimodal agentic systems.

ai.tldr: 定义了源模态监控能力，并研究了视觉语言模型如何利用句法和语义信号绑定信息到输入源。
ai.motivation: 多模态模型需要跟踪信息来自哪个输入源，这对鲁棒性和智能体系统至关重要。
ai.method: 在11个视觉语言模型上设计目标模态信息检索任务，分析句法和语义信号的作用。
ai.result: 句法和语义信号都重要，但模态分布差异大时语义信号占主导。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解多模态模型的信息溯源机制，提升模型设计和评估。

idea_spark.idea: 设计训练策略增强模型对输入源的显式绑定能力。
idea_spark.risk: 过度依赖语义信号可能导致对句法结构的忽视，影响泛化。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴认知科学中的绑定问题，改进多模态模型架构。

en.motivation: Multimodal models need to track which input source information comes from, crucial for robustness and agentic systems.
en.method: Designed target-modality retrieval tasks across 11 VLMs, analyzing the role of syntactic and semantic signals.
en.result: Both signals matter, but semantic signals dominate when modalities are distributionally distinct.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand information source tracking in multimodal models, aiding model design and evaluation.

idea_spark.inspiration: Draw from cognitive science binding problem to improve multimodal architectures.

papers.title: EV-CLIP: Efficient Visual Prompt Adaptation for CLIP in Few-shot Action Recognition under Visual Challenges
papers.summary: CLIP has demonstrated strong generalization in visual domains through natural language supervision, even for video action recognition. However, most existing approaches that adapt CLIP for action recognition have primarily focused on temporal modeling, often overlooking spatial perception. In real-world scenarios, visual challenges such as low-light environments or egocentric viewpoints can severely impair spatial understanding, an essential precursor for effective temporal reasoning. To address this limitation, we propose Efficient Visual Prompting for CLIP (EV-CLIP), an efficient adaptation framework designed for few-shot video action recognition across diverse scenes and viewpoints. EV-CLIP introduces two visual prompts: mask prompts, which guide the model's attention to action-relevant regions by reweighting pixels, and context prompts, which perform lightweight temporal modeling by compressing frame-wise features into a compact representation. For a comprehensive evaluation, we curate five benchmark datasets and analyze domain shifts to quantify the influence of diverse visual and semantic factors on action recognition. Experimental results demonstrate that EV-CLIP outperforms existing parameter-efficient methods in overall performance. Moreover, its efficiency remains independent of the backbone scale, making it well-suited for deployment in real-world, resource-constrained scenarios. The code is available at https://github.com/AI-CV-Lab/EV-CLIP.

ai.tldr: 提出EV-CLIP，通过两种视觉提示（掩码提示和上下文提示）高效适应CLIP，解决少样本动作识别中因低光或自我中心视角等视觉挑战导致的空间感知不足。
ai.motivation: 现有CLIP适应动作识别的方法侧重时序建模，忽略空间感知，在低光、自我中心视角等现实场景中空间理解受限，影响时序推理。
ai.method: 引入掩码提示（重加权像素引导注意力到动作相关区域）和上下文提示（压缩帧特征实现轻量时序建模），在五个基准数据集上评估域偏移。
ai.result: 在少样本动作识别中优于现有参数高效方法，且效率与骨干网络规模无关，适合资源受限场景。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种高效、轻量的CLIP适应方案，适用于视觉挑战下的少样本动作识别任务。

idea_spark.idea: 探索自适应掩码提示生成，根据输入场景动态调整注意力区域。
idea_spark.risk: 掩码提示可能过度依赖预训练CLIP的空间先验，在极端域偏移下失效。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将提示学习与域偏移分析结合，启发更鲁棒的跨域视觉适应方法。

en.tldr: Proposes EV-CLIP, which efficiently adapts CLIP via two visual prompts (mask and context prompts) to address spatial perception deficits in few-shot action recognition under visual challenges like low-light or egocentric views.
en.motivation: Existing CLIP adaptations for action recognition focus on temporal modeling, neglecting spatial perception, which is impaired in real-world scenarios such as low-light environments or egocentric viewpoints, hindering temporal reasoning.
en.method: Introduces mask prompts (reweighting pixels to guide attention to action-relevant regions) and context prompts (compressing frame features for lightweight temporal modeling), evaluated on five benchmark datasets with domain shift analysis.
en.result: Outperforms existing parameter-efficient methods in few-shot action recognition, with efficiency independent of backbone scale, suitable for resource-constrained deployment.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient, lightweight CLIP adaptation framework for few-shot action recognition under visual challenges.

idea_spark.idea: Explore adaptive mask prompt generation that dynamically adjusts attention regions based on input scenes.
idea_spark.risk: Mask prompts may overly rely on CLIP's spatial priors, failing under extreme domain shifts.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining prompt learning with domain shift analysis inspires more robust cross-domain visual adaptation methods.

papers.title: When Cow Urine Cures Constipation on YouTube: Limits of LLMs in Detecting Culture-specific Health Misinformation
papers.summary: Social media platforms have become primary channels for health information in the Global South. Using gomutra (cow urine) discourse on YouTube in India as a case study, we present a post-facto Large Language Model (LLM)-assisted discourse analysis of 30 multilingual transcripts showing that promotional content blends sacred traditional language with pseudo-scientific claims in ways that sophisticated debunking content itself mirrors, creating a rhetorical register that LLMs, trained predominantly on Western corpora, are systematically ill-equipped to analyse. Varying prompt tone across three LLMs (GPT-4o, Gemini 2.5 Pro, DeepSeek-V3.1), we find that culturally embedded health misinformation does not look like ordinary misinformation, and this cultural obfuscation extends to gendered rhetoric and prompt design, compounding analytical unreliability. Our findings argue that cultural competency in LLM-assisted discourse analysis cannot be retrofitted through prompt engineering alone.

ai.tldr: 以印度YouTube上牛尿（gomutra）健康言论为案例，揭示LLM因训练语料西方化而无法识别文化嵌入的健康虚假信息。
ai.motivation: 全球南方社交媒体上文化特异性健康虚假信息泛滥，现有LLM检测方法缺乏文化能力。
ai.method: 对30个多语言YouTube视频进行事后LLM辅助话语分析，比较GPT-4o、Gemini 2.5 Pro、DeepSeek-V3.1在不同提示语气下的表现。
ai.result: 文化嵌入的虚假信息不呈现典型虚假信息特征，且文化混淆延伸至性别修辞和提示设计，提示工程无法弥补文化能力缺失。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解LLM在跨文化健康信息检测中的系统性局限，避免盲目依赖提示工程。

idea_spark.idea: 构建文化知识图谱辅助LLM，或训练多语言文化敏感模型以提升检测鲁棒性。
idea_spark.risk: 文化知识图谱可能引入新的偏见，且多语言模型训练数据仍可能偏向主流文化。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从人类学话语分析中借鉴文化框架，而非仅依赖统计模式识别。
