idea_spark.inspiration: 从人类学话语分析中借鉴文化框架，而非仅依赖统计模式识别。

en.tldr: Using cow urine (gomutra) discourse on YouTube in India, this study shows LLMs fail to detect culturally embedded health misinformation due to Western-centric training. Prompt engineering alone cannot fix cultural incompetency.
en.motivation: Social media health misinformation in the Global South is culturally specific, and current LLM detection methods lack cultural awareness.
en.method: Post-facto LLM-assisted discourse analysis of 30 multilingual YouTube transcripts, testing GPT-4o, Gemini 2.5 Pro, and DeepSeek-V3.1 with varied prompt tones.
en.result: Culturally embedded misinformation does not resemble typical misinformation; cultural obfuscation extends to gendered rhetoric and prompt design, making prompt engineering insufficient.
en.help_to_user: Highlights the systemic limitations of LLMs in cross-cultural health misinformation detection, cautioning against over-reliance on prompt engineering.

idea_spark.idea: Build cultural knowledge graphs to augment LLMs, or train culturally sensitive multilingual models for robust detection.
idea_spark.risk: Knowledge graphs may introduce new biases; multilingual training data may still skew toward dominant cultures.
idea_spark.inspiration: Incorporate anthropological discourse analysis frameworks rather than relying solely on statistical pattern recognition.

papers.title: FlowAnchor: Stabilizing the Editing Signal for Inversion-Free Video Editing
papers.summary: We propose FlowAnchor, a training-free framework for stable and efficient inversion-free, flow-based video editing. Inversion-free editing methods have recently shown impressive efficiency and structure preservation in images by directly steering the sampling trajectory with an editing signal. However, extending this paradigm to videos remains challenging, often failing in multi-object scenes or with increased frame counts. We identify the root cause as the instability of the editing signal in high-dimensional video latent spaces, which arises from imprecise spatial localization and length-induced magnitude attenuation. To overcome this challenge, FlowAnchor explicitly anchors both where to edit and how strongly to edit. It introduces Spatial-aware Attention Refinement, which enforces consistent alignment between textual guidance and spatial regions, and Adaptive Magnitude Modulation, which adaptively preserves sufficient editing strength. Together, these mechanisms stabilize the editing signal and guide the flow-based evolution toward the desired target distribution. Extensive experiments demonstrate that FlowAnchor achieves more faithful, temporally coherent, and computationally efficient video editing across challenging multi-object and fast-motion scenarios. The project page is available at https://cuc-mipg.github.io/FlowAnchor.github.io/.

ai.tldr: 提出FlowAnchor，一种无需训练、基于流的视频编辑框架，通过锚定编辑位置和强度来稳定编辑信号，实现多目标和快速运动场景下的忠实、时序一致且高效的视频编辑。
ai.motivation: 现有无反转视频编辑方法在多目标或长帧场景中编辑信号不稳定，导致编辑失败。
ai.method: 引入空间感知注意力精炼（Spatial-aware Attention Refinement）和自适应幅度调制（Adaptive Magnitude Modulation），分别锚定编辑区域和编辑强度。
ai.result: 在多个挑战性场景下，编辑更忠实、时序更连贯、计算更高效。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户无需训练即可对视频进行稳定、高质量的编辑，尤其适用于复杂多目标和快速运动场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将编辑信号稳定思想扩展到音频或文本序列编辑，利用类似的空间-语义对齐机制。
idea_spark.risk: 在极长视频或高动态场景中，锚定机制可能仍无法完全消除累积误差。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从控制论中的反馈稳定概念获得启发，将编辑过程视为动态系统，通过锚定关键参数保证轨迹收敛。

zh.result: 在多个挑战性场景下，编辑更忠实、时序更连贯、计算更高效。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户无需训练即可对视频进行稳定、高质量的编辑，尤其适用于复杂多目标和快速运动场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将编辑信号稳定思想扩展到音频或文本序列编辑，利用类似的空间-语义对齐机制。
idea_spark.risk: 在极长视频或高动态场景中，锚定机制可能仍无法完全消除累积误差。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从控制论中的反馈稳定概念获得启发，将编辑过程视为动态系统，通过锚定关键参数保证轨迹收敛。

en.tldr: FlowAnchor is a training-free, flow-based video editing framework that stabilizes the editing signal by anchoring where and how strongly to edit, achieving faithful, temporally coherent, and efficient editing in multi-object and fast-motion scenarios.
en.motivation: Existing inversion-free video editing methods suffer from unstable editing signals in multi-object or long-frame settings, leading to editing failures.
en.method: Introduces Spatial-aware Attention Refinement and Adaptive Magnitude Modulation to anchor the editing region and strength respectively.
en.result: More faithful, temporally coherent, and computationally efficient editing across challenging multi-object and fast-motion scenarios.
en.help_to_user: Enables users to perform stable, high-quality video editing without training, especially for complex multi-object and fast-motion scenes.

idea_spark.idea: Extend the editing signal stabilization idea to audio or text sequence editing using similar spatial-semantic alignment mechanisms.
idea_spark.risk: In extremely long videos or highly dynamic scenes, the anchoring mechanism may still fail to fully eliminate cumulative errors.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by feedback stabilization in control theory, treating the editing process as a dynamic system and anchoring key parameters to ensure trajectory convergence.

papers.title: Using Embedding Models to Improve Probabilistic Race Prediction
papers.summary: Estimating racial disparity requires individual-level race data, which are often unavailable due to the sensitivity of collecting such information. To address this problem, many researchers utilize Bayesian Improved Surname Geocoding (BISG), which have critically relied on Census surname data. Unfortunately, these data capture race-surname relationships only for common surnames, omitting approximately 10% of the US population. We show that predictive performance degrades substantially for individuals with such omitted, uncommon surnames because standard BISG implementation relies on a uninformative generic prior in these cases. To address this limitation, we propose embedding-powered BISG (eBISG), which uses pre-trained text embeddings to represent names as dense vectors and trains neural networks on 2020 Census surname and first-name data to estimate race probabilities for names not covered in the Census. We compare five approaches: standard BISG using only surnames, BIFSG incorporating first name probabilities, surname embedding for unlisted names, surname and first name embedding combining both, and a full-name embedding trained on voter file data from Southern states that captures interactions between name components. We show that each successive eBISG approach improves race prediction, with the full-name embedding yielding the largest gains, particularly for Hispanic and Asian voters whose surnames are absent from the Census list.

ai.tldr: 提出嵌入增强的BISG方法，利用预训练文本嵌入和神经网络，显著提升对罕见姓氏个体的种族预测准确性。
ai.motivation: 标准BISG依赖人口普查姓氏数据，对约10%的罕见姓氏个体预测效果差，因为使用了无信息先验。
ai.method: 使用预训练文本嵌入将姓名表示为稠密向量，训练神经网络基于2020年人口普查姓氏和名字数据估计未覆盖姓名的种族概率，并比较五种逐步改进的方法。
ai.result: 全名嵌入方法在南方州选民数据上表现最佳，尤其对姓氏不在普查列表中的西班牙裔和亚裔选民提升最大。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员在缺乏个体种族数据时，更准确地估计种族差异，减少因罕见姓氏导致的预测偏差。

idea_spark.idea: 将嵌入模型与贝叶斯框架结合，利用外部数据（如选民登记）增强对罕见类别的预测。
idea_spark.risk: 嵌入模型可能引入训练数据中的偏见，导致对某些群体的预测不公；依赖外部数据可能涉及隐私问题。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用预训练语言模型捕捉名称的语义信息，弥补传统统计方法在稀疏数据上的不足。

en.tldr: Proposes embedding-powered BISG (eBISG) using pre-trained text embeddings and neural networks to significantly improve race prediction for individuals with uncommon surnames.
en.motivation: Standard BISG relies on Census surname data and performs poorly for about 10% of the US population with rare surnames due to uninformative priors.
en.method: Uses pre-trained text embeddings to represent names as dense vectors, trains neural networks on 2020 Census surname and first-name data to estimate race probabilities for uncovered names, and compares five progressively refined approaches.
en.result: The full-name embedding approach yields the largest gains, especially for Hispanic and Asian voters whose surnames are absent from the Census list, as evaluated on voter file data from Southern states.
en.help_to_user: Helps researchers estimate racial disparities more accurately when individual-level race data are unavailable, reducing bias due to rare surnames.

idea_spark.idea: Combine embedding models with Bayesian frameworks and leverage external data (e.g., voter registration) to enhance predictions for rare categories.
idea_spark.risk: Embedding models may inherit biases from training data, leading to unfair predictions for certain groups; reliance on external data raises privacy concerns.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverage pre-trained language models to capture semantic information in names, overcoming limitations of traditional statistical methods on sparse data.

keywords_raw: neural networks for name-based race estimation

papers.title: Large Language Models Decide Early and Explain Later
papers.summary: Large Language Models often achieve strong performance by generating long intermediate chain-of-thought reasoning. However, it remains unclear when a model's final answer is actually determined during generation. If the answer is already fixed at an intermediate stage, subsequent reasoning tokens may constitute post-decision explanation, increasing inference cost and latency without improving correctness. We study the evolution of predicted answers over reasoning steps using forced answer completion, which elicits the model's intermediate predictions at partial reasoning prefixes. Focusing on Qwen3-4B and averaging results across all datasets considered, we find that predicted answers change in only 32% of queries. Moreover, once the final answer switch occurs, the model generates an average of 760 additional reasoning tokens per query, accounting for a substantial fraction of the total reasoning budget. Motivated by these findings, we investigate early stopping strategies that halt generation once the answer has stabilized. We show that simple heuristics, including probe-based stopping, can reduce reasoning token usage by 500 tokens per query while incurring only a 2% drop in accuracy. Together, our results indicate that a large portion of chain-of-thought generation is redundant and can be reduced with minimal impact on performance.

ai.tldr: 大型语言模型在生成推理链时，最终答案往往在早期就已确定，后续推理标记多为事后解释，造成冗余。
ai.motivation: 探究大型语言模型在链式思维推理中，最终答案何时被确定，以及后续推理标记是否冗余。
ai.method: 使用强制答案补全方法，在部分推理前缀上获取模型中间预测，分析答案演化；并测试基于探针的早停策略。
ai.result: 仅32%的查询中答案发生变化；答案稳定后平均生成760个额外推理标记；早停可减少500个标记，准确率仅下降2%。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解LLM推理中的冗余，指导设计更高效的推理策略，降低计算成本。

idea_spark.idea: 开发自适应早停机制，根据答案稳定性动态截断推理链。
idea_spark.risk: 早停可能在某些复杂推理任务中过早截断，导致错误答案。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受答案早期固化现象启发，可探索更高效的推理路径压缩方法。

idea_spark.risk: 早停可能在某些复杂推理任务中过早截断，导致错误答案。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受答案早期固化现象启发，可探索更高效的推理路径压缩方法。

en.tldr: Large language models often determine their final answer early in chain-of-thought reasoning, with subsequent tokens serving as post-decision explanation, leading to redundancy.
en.motivation: To investigate when the final answer is determined during chain-of-thought generation and whether later reasoning tokens are redundant.
en.method: Forced answer completion elicits intermediate predictions at partial reasoning prefixes; probe-based early stopping is tested.
en.result: Only 32% of queries change answer; after stabilization, 760 extra tokens are generated on average; early stopping reduces 500 tokens with only 2% accuracy drop.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand redundancy in LLM reasoning and guides design of more efficient inference strategies to reduce cost.

idea_spark.idea: Develop adaptive early stopping that dynamically truncates reasoning chains based on answer stability.
idea_spark.risk: Early stopping may truncate prematurely in complex reasoning tasks, leading to incorrect answers.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the early answer fixation phenomenon, explore more efficient reasoning path compression methods.

papers.title: RouteLMT: Learned Sample Routing for Hybrid LLM Translation Deployment
papers.summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) have achieved remarkable performance in Machine Translation (MT), but deploying them at scale remains prohibitively expensive. A widely adopted remedy is the hybrid system paradigm, which balances cost and quality by serving most requests with a small model and selectively routing a fraction to a large model. However, existing routing strategies often rely on heuristics, external predictors, or absolute quality estimation, which fail to capture whether the large model actually provides a worthwhile improvement over the small one. In this paper, we formulate routing as a budget allocation problem and identify marginal gain, i.e., the large model's improvement over the small model, as the optimal signal for budgeted decisions. Building on this, we propose \textbf{RouteLMT} (routing for LLM-based MT), an efficient in-model router that predicts this expected gain by probing the small translators prompt-token representation, without requiring external models or hypothesis decoding. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our RouteLMT outperforms heuristics, quality/difficulty estimation baselines, achieving a superior quality-budget Pareto frontier. Furthermore, we analyze regression risks and show that a simple guarded variant can mitigate severe quality losses.

ai.tldr: 提出RouteLMT，通过预测大模型相对于小模型的边际增益，实现混合LLM翻译部署中的高效样本路由，在预算约束下达到更优的质量-成本帕累托前沿。
ai.motivation: 大规模LLM翻译部署成本高昂，混合系统通过小模型处理多数请求、大模型处理少数请求来平衡成本与质量，但现有路由策略未能准确判断大模型是否带来值得的改进。
ai.method: 将路由形式化为预算分配问题，以边际增益（大模型相对于小模型的改进）作为决策信号；通过探测小模型提示词表示来预测该增益，无需外部模型或假设解码。
ai.result: 实验表明RouteLMT优于启发式、质量/难度估计基线，实现更优的质量-预算帕累托前沿；简单防护变体可缓解严重质量损失。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何在不显著增加成本的前提下，利用LLM提升翻译质量，并提供了可落地的路由策略。

idea_spark.idea: 将路由问题转化为预算分配问题，并利用小模型内部表示直接预测大模型增益，避免了额外推理开销。
idea_spark.risk: 回归风险可能导致路由决策失误，需设计防护机制；对模型架构和训练数据敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从经济学中的边际效用概念出发，启发在资源受限的AI系统中引入更精细的收益评估。

zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何在不显著增加成本的前提下，利用LLM提升翻译质量，并提供了可落地的路由策略。

idea_spark.idea: 将路由问题转化为预算分配问题，并利用小模型内部表示直接预测大模型增益，避免了额外推理开销。
idea_spark.risk: 回归风险可能导致路由决策失误，需设计防护机制；对模型架构和训练数据敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从经济学中的边际效用概念出发，启发在资源受限的AI系统中引入更精细的收益评估。

en.tldr: Proposes RouteLMT, which predicts the marginal gain of a large model over a small one for efficient sample routing in hybrid LLM translation deployment, achieving a better quality-cost Pareto frontier under budget constraints.
en.motivation: Deploying large LLMs for translation at scale is prohibitively expensive; hybrid systems use a small model for most requests and a large model for a fraction, but existing routing strategies fail to capture whether the large model provides worthwhile improvement.
en.method: Formulates routing as a budget allocation problem, using marginal gain (improvement of large model over small model) as the decision signal; predicts this gain by probing the small model's prompt-token representation, without external models or hypothesis decoding.
en.result: Experiments show RouteLMT outperforms heuristics and quality/difficulty baselines, achieving a superior quality-budget Pareto frontier; a simple guarded variant mitigates severe quality losses.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to improve translation quality with LLMs without significantly increasing cost, and provides a deployable routing strategy.

idea_spark.idea: Reformulates routing as a budget allocation problem and directly predicts large model gain from small model internal representations, avoiding extra inference overhead.
idea_spark.risk: Regression risks may lead to suboptimal routing decisions, requiring guard mechanisms; sensitive to model architecture and training data.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draws from the economic concept of marginal utility, inspiring finer-grained benefit evaluation in resource-constrained AI systems.

papers.title: On the Optimum Secrecy Outage Probability and Ergodic Secrecy Rate over Wireless Channels
papers.summary: We study the secrecy of wireless channels in the presence of an eavesdropper, where the channels are random and the transmitter only has knowledge of the channel statistics. We investigate the optimal input distribution with respect to several secrecy metrics: the Secrecy Outage Probability (SOP), defined as the probability that the coding rate $r$ exceeds the instantaneous secrecy rate; the Ergodic Secrecy Rate (ESR), defined as the expected secrecy rate over channel realizations; and the Ergodic Positive Secrecy Rate (EPSR), defined as the expected value of the positive part of the secrecy rate. We introduce two partial orderings for random channels: the uniformly less noisy order and the less noisy on average order. We show that when the main channel is uniformly less noisy than the eavesdropper channel, the optimal input distribution is a non-precoded Gaussian input for both the SOP and the EPSR. Furthermore, we show that the same input distribution is optimal for the ESR when the less noisy on average order holds. In addition, similar optimality results for the SOP and the EPSR are obtained for single-transmit-antenna channels without requiring any channel ordering assumptions. Closed-form expressions of the secrecy metrics are derived for special cases of Rayleigh fading channels.

ai.tldr: 研究了窃听者存在下无线信道的保密性能，针对保密中断概率、遍历保密速率和遍历正保密速率，找到了最优输入分布。
ai.motivation: 无线信道随机且发射端仅知信道统计信息时，如何选择输入分布以优化多种保密指标。
ai.method: 引入两种信道偏序（均匀较不嘈杂序和平均较不嘈杂序），推导最优输入分布为高斯输入，并给出瑞利衰落下的闭式表达式。
ai.result: 当主信道均匀较不嘈杂时，高斯输入对保密中断概率和遍历正保密速率最优；当平均较不嘈杂时，对遍历保密速率最优。单天线场景无需偏序假设。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解在统计信道信息下如何选择输入分布以提升无线通信的保密性。

idea_spark.idea: 利用信道偏序简化最优输入分布问题，避免复杂优化。
idea_spark.risk: 偏序条件在实际信道中可能不成立，导致结论不适用。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将信道比较转化为偏序关系，为物理层安全提供新分析工具。

zh.tldr: 研究了窃听者存在下无线信道的保密性能，针对保密中断概率、遍历保密速率和遍历正保密速率，找到了最优输入分布。
zh.motivation: 无线信道随机且发射端仅知信道统计信息时，如何选择输入分布以优化多种保密指标。
zh.method: 引入两种信道偏序（均匀较不嘈杂序和平均较不嘈杂序），推导最优输入分布为高斯输入，并给出瑞利衰落下的闭式表达式。
zh.result: 当主信道均匀较不嘈杂时，高斯输入对保密中断概率和遍历正保密速率最优；当平均较不嘈杂时，对遍历保密速率最优。单天线场景无需偏序假设。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助理解在统计信道信息下如何选择输入分布以提升无线通信的保密性。

idea_spark.idea: 利用信道偏序简化最优输入分布问题，避免复杂优化。
idea_spark.risk: 偏序条件在实际信道中可能不成立，导致结论不适用。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将信道比较转化为偏序关系，为物理层安全提供新分析工具。

en.tldr: Studies the secrecy performance of wireless channels with an eavesdropper, finding optimal input distributions for secrecy outage probability, ergodic secrecy rate, and ergodic positive secrecy rate.
en.motivation: How to choose the input distribution to optimize multiple secrecy metrics when wireless channels are random and the transmitter only knows channel statistics.
en.method: Introduces two partial orderings (uniformly less noisy and less noisy on average) and shows that Gaussian input is optimal; closed-form expressions are derived for Rayleigh fading.
en.result: When the main channel is uniformly less noisy, Gaussian input is optimal for SOP and EPSR; when less noisy on average, it is optimal for ESR. Single-antenna case requires no ordering assumptions.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand how to select input distributions to enhance wireless secrecy under statistical channel knowledge.

idea_spark.idea: Use channel partial orderings to simplify the optimal input distribution problem, avoiding complex optimization.
idea_spark.risk: The ordering conditions may not hold in practical channels, limiting applicability.
idea_spark.inspiration: Transforming channel comparisons into partial orders provides a new analytical tool for physical layer security.

papers.title: Knowledge Visualization: A Benchmark and Method for Knowledge-Intensive Text-to-Image Generation
papers.summary: Recent text-to-image (T2I) models have demonstrated impressive capabilities in photorealistic synthesis and instruction following. However, their reliability in knowledge-intensive settings remains largely unexplored. Unlike natural image generation, knowledge visualization requires not only semantic alignment but also strict adherence to domain knowledge, structural constraints, and symbolic conventions, exposing a critical gap between visual plausibility and scientific correctness. To systematically study this problem, we introduce KVBench, a curriculum-grounded benchmark for evaluating knowledge-intensive T2I generation. KVBench covers six senior high-school subjects: Biology, Chemistry, Geography, History, Mathematics, and Physics. The benchmark consists of 1,800 expert-curated prompts derived from over 30 authoritative textbooks. Using this benchmark, we evaluate 14 state-of-the-art open- and closed-source models, revealing substantial deficiencies in logical reasoning, symbolic precision, and multilingual robustness, with open-source models consistently underperforming proprietary systems. To address these limitations, we further propose KE-Check, a two-stage framework that improves scientific fidelity via (1) Knowledge Elaboration for structured prompt enrichment, and (2) Checklist-Guided Refinement for explicit constraint enforcement through violation identification and constraint-guided editing. KE-Check effectively mitigates scientific hallucinations, narrowing the performance gap between open-source and leading closed-source models. Data and codes are publicly available at https://github.com/zhaoran66/KVBench.

ai.tldr: 提出KVBench基准和KE-Check框架，系统评估和提升知识密集型文本到图像生成的科学准确性。
ai.motivation: 现有文本到图像模型在知识密集型场景（如科学图表）中缺乏可靠性和正确性，存在视觉合理但科学错误的问题。
ai.method: 构建包含1800个专家提示的KVBench基准，覆盖六门高中科目；提出KE-Check两阶段框架：知识阐述丰富提示，清单引导修正确保约束。
ai.result: 评估14个模型发现开源模型在逻辑推理、符号精度和多语言鲁棒性上显著不足；KE-Check有效缩小了开源与闭源模型的性能差距。

en.tldr: Proposes KVBench benchmark and KE-Check framework to systematically evaluate and improve scientific accuracy in knowledge-intensive text-to-image generation.
en.motivation: Current T2I models lack reliability in knowledge-intensive settings, producing visually plausible but scientifically incorrect outputs.
en.method: Constructs KVBench with 1,800 expert-curated prompts across six high-school subjects; proposes KE-Check two-stage framework: Knowledge Elaboration for prompt enrichment and Checklist-Guided Refinement for constraint enforcement.
en.result: Evaluation of 14 models reveals significant deficiencies in open-source models; KE-Check effectively narrows the performance gap with proprietary systems.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to evaluate and improve T2I models for scientific knowledge visualization tasks.

idea_spark.idea: Integrate knowledge graphs or textbook structures to automatically generate constraint checklists for more general scientific visualization quality assurance.
idea_spark.risk: Over-reliance on predefined constraints may limit model creativity; incomplete domain coverage could introduce new errors.
idea_spark.inspiration: Systematic construction of evaluation tasks from curriculum design inspires quality benchmarks in other domains like law or medicine.

papers.title: Dynamically Acquiring Text Content to Enable the Classification of Lesser-known Entities for Real-world Tasks
papers.summary: Existing Natural Language Processing (NLP) resources often lack the task-specific information required for real-world problems and provide limited coverage of lesser-known or newly introduced entities. For example, business organizations and health care providers may need to be classified into a variety of different taxonomic schemes for specific application tasks. Our goal is to enable domain experts to easily create a task-specific classifier for entities by providing only entity names and gold labels as training data. Our framework then dynamically acquires descriptive text about each entity, which is subsequently used as the basis for producing a text-based classifier. We propose a novel text acquisition method that leverages both web and large language models (LLMs). We evaluate our proposed framework on two classification problems in distinct domains: (i) classifying organizations into Standard Industrial Classification (SIC) Codes, which categorize organizations based on their business activities; and (ii) classifying healthcare providers into healthcare provider taxonomy codes, which represent a provider's medical specialty and area of practice. Our best-performing model achieved macro-averaged F1-scores of 82.3% and 72.9% on the SIC code and healthcare taxonomy code classification tasks, respectively.

ai.tldr: 提出一种动态获取实体描述文本的框架，仅需实体名称和标签即可训练分类器，用于小众实体分类。
ai.motivation: 现有NLP资源缺乏任务特定信息，对冷门或新实体覆盖不足，难以满足实际应用中的分类需求。
ai.method: 结合网络搜索和大语言模型动态获取每个实体的描述文本，基于文本训练分类器。
ai.result: 在SIC代码分类和医疗提供者分类任务上分别达到82.3%和72.9%的宏平均F1分数。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助领域专家快速构建针对特定实体的分类器，无需大量标注数据。

idea_spark.idea: 利用大语言模型生成或补充实体描述，结合网络文本提高冷门实体的分类效果。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖网络文本质量和LLM生成内容的准确性，可能引入噪声或偏见。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将实体分类转化为文本分类问题，通过动态文本获取降低对预定义知识库的依赖。

en.tldr: Proposes a framework that dynamically acquires descriptive text for entities, enabling classification with only entity names and labels, targeting lesser-known entities.
en.motivation: Existing NLP resources lack task-specific information and have limited coverage of obscure or new entities, hindering real-world classification tasks.
en.method: Combines web search and large language models to dynamically fetch entity descriptions, then trains a text-based classifier.
en.result: Achieves macro-averaged F1 scores of 82.3% and 72.9% on SIC code and healthcare taxonomy code classification tasks, respectively.
en.help_to_user: Enables domain experts to quickly build classifiers for specific entities without extensive labeled data.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage LLMs to generate or supplement entity descriptions, combined with web text to improve classification of rare entities.
idea_spark.risk: Dependence on web text quality and LLM output accuracy may introduce noise or bias.
idea_spark.inspiration: Transform entity classification into text classification by dynamic text acquisition, reducing reliance on predefined knowledge bases.

papers.title: ResRank: Unifying Retrieval and Listwise Reranking via End-to-End Joint Training with Residual Passage Compression
papers.summary: Large language model (LLM) based listwise reranking has emerged as the dominant paradigm for achieving state-of-the-art ranking effectiveness in information retrieval. However, its reliance on feeding full passage texts into the LLM introduces two critical bottlenecks: the "lost in the middle" phenomenon degrades ranking quality as input length grows, and the inference latency scales super-linearly with sequence length, rendering it impractical for industrial deployment. In this paper, we present ResRank, a unified retrieval-reranking framework that fundamentally addresses both challenges. Inspired by multimodal LLMs that project visual inputs into compact token representations, ResRank employs an Encoder-LLM to compress each candidate passage into a single embedding, which is then fed alongside the query text into a Reranker-LLM for listwise ranking. To alleviate the misalignment between the compressed representation space and the ranking space, we introduce a residual connection structure that combines encoder embeddings with contextualized hidden states from the reranker. Furthermore, we replace the conventional autoregressive decoding with a one-step cosine-similarity-based scoring mechanism, eliminating the generation bottleneck entirely. ResRank is trained through a carefully designed dual-stage, multi-task, end-to-end joint optimization strategy that simultaneously trains the encoder and reranker, achieving learning objective alignment between retrieval and reranking while substantially reducing training complexity. Extensive experiments on TREC Deep Learning and eight BEIR benchmark datasets demonstrate that ResRank achieves competitive or superior ranking effectiveness compared to existing approaches while requiring zero generated tokens and processing only one token per passage, yielding a fundamentally better balance between effectiveness and efficiency.

ai.tldr: 提出ResRank框架，通过残差压缩将每个候选段落编码为单个嵌入，结合端到端联合训练，实现检索与列表重排的统一，在保持或提升排序效果的同时大幅降低推理延迟。
ai.motivation: 基于LLM的列表重排虽效果好，但存在“中间丢失”和推理延迟随输入长度超线性增长的问题，难以工业部署。
ai.method: 采用Encoder-LLM将每个候选段落压缩为单个嵌入，通过残差连接与重排器上下文隐藏状态融合，并用余弦相似度一步评分替代自回归解码；设计双阶段多任务端到端联合优化策略。
ai.result: 在TREC Deep Learning和八个BEIR数据集上达到与现有方法相当或更优的排序效果，且零生成令牌、每段落仅处理一个令牌。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过段落压缩和联合训练在检索重排中兼顾效果与效率。

idea_spark.idea: 借鉴多模态LLM的视觉令牌压缩思想，将文本段落投影为紧凑表示，并设计残差连接缓解表示空间与排序空间的对齐问题。
idea_spark.risk: 压缩可能丢失细粒度信息，在需要精确匹配的场景下效果可能下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 多模态LLM的输入压缩策略启发我们将其应用于纯文本检索重排，实现效率突破。

zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过段落压缩和联合训练在检索重排中兼顾效果与效率。

idea_spark.idea: 借鉴多模态LLM的视觉令牌压缩思想，将文本段落投影为紧凑表示，并设计残差连接缓解表示空间与排序空间的对齐问题。
idea_spark.risk: 压缩可能丢失细粒度信息，在需要精确匹配的场景下效果可能下降。
idea_spark.inspiration: 多模态LLM的输入压缩策略启发我们将其应用于纯文本检索重排，实现效率突破。

en.tldr: Proposes ResRank, a unified retrieval-reranking framework that compresses each candidate passage into a single embedding via an Encoder-LLM, uses residual connections and cosine-similarity scoring, and is trained end-to-end to achieve competitive effectiveness with zero generated tokens and one token per passage.
en.motivation: LLM-based listwise reranking suffers from 'lost in the middle' and super-linear inference latency, hindering industrial deployment.
en.method: Employs an Encoder-LLM to compress each passage into a single embedding, combines it with reranker hidden states via residual connections, replaces autoregressive decoding with one-step cosine-similarity scoring, and uses a dual-stage multi-task end-to-end joint training strategy.
en.result: Achieves competitive or superior ranking effectiveness on TREC Deep Learning and eight BEIR datasets while requiring zero generated tokens and only one token per passage.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how passage compression and joint training can balance effectiveness and efficiency in retrieval reranking.

idea_spark.idea: Borrows visual token compression from multimodal LLMs to project text passages into compact representations, with residual connections to align representation and ranking spaces.
idea_spark.risk: Compression may lose fine-grained information, potentially degrading performance in exact-match scenarios.
idea_spark.inspiration: Input compression strategies in multimodal LLMs inspire a breakthrough in efficiency for text retrieval reranking.

papers.title: Railway Artificial Intelligence Learning Benchmark (RAIL-BENCH): A Benchmark Suite for Perception in the Railway Domain
papers.summary: Automated train operation on existing railway infrastructure requires robust camera-based perception, yet the railway domain lacks public benchmark suites with standardized evaluation protocols that would enable reproducible comparison of approaches. We present RAIL-BENCH, the first perception benchmark suite for the railway domain. It comprises five challenges - rail track detection, object detection, vegetation segmentation, multi-object tracking, and monocular visual odometry - each tailored to the specific characteristics of railway environments. RAIL-BENCH provides curated training and test datasets drawn from diverse real-world scenarios, evaluation metrics, and public scoreboards (https://www.mrt.kit.edu/railbench). For the rail track detection challenge we introduce LineAP, a novel segment-based average precision metric that evaluates the geometric accuracy of polyline predictions independently of instance-level grouping, addressing key limitations of existing line detection metrics.

ai.tldr: 提出了铁路领域首个感知基准套件RAIL-BENCH，包含五个挑战任务和新的LineAP评估指标。
ai.motivation: 铁路领域缺乏公开的标准化基准套件，阻碍了可重复的感知方法比较。
ai.method: 构建了包含轨道检测、目标检测、植被分割、多目标跟踪和单目视觉里程计五个挑战的基准，并引入基于线段的平均精度指标LineAP。
ai.result: 提供了来自真实场景的训练/测试数据集、评估指标和公开排行榜。
ai.help_to_user: 为铁路感知研究提供了标准化评估平台，便于方法对比和复现。

idea_spark.idea: 将LineAP应用于自动驾驶车道线检测或机器人路径规划中的线状目标评估。
idea_spark.risk: 新指标可能未被社区广泛接受，需验证其与现有指标的一致性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 领域特定基准的构建思路可迁移至其他工业场景（如港口、矿山）的感知评估。

zh.motivation: 铁路领域缺乏公开的标准化基准套件，阻碍了可重复的感知方法比较。

en.tldr: Proposes RAIL-BENCH, the first perception benchmark suite for the railway domain, comprising five challenges and a novel LineAP metric.
en.motivation: The railway domain lacks public benchmark suites with standardized evaluation protocols for reproducible comparison.
en.method: Constructs a benchmark with five challenges (rail track detection, object detection, vegetation segmentation, multi-object tracking, monocular visual odometry) and introduces LineAP, a segment-based average precision metric.
en.result: Provides curated datasets from real-world scenarios, evaluation metrics, and public scoreboards.
en.help_to_user: Offers a standardized evaluation platform for railway perception research, enabling reproducible method comparison.

idea_spark.idea: Apply LineAP to lane detection in autonomous driving or line-shaped object evaluation in robot path planning.
idea_spark.risk: New metric may not be widely adopted; consistency with existing metrics needs validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Domain-specific benchmark design can be transferred to other industrial scenarios (e.g., ports, mines) for perception evaluation.

papers.title: BLAST: Benchmarking LLMs with ASP-based Structured Testing
papers.summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable performance across a broad spectrum of tasks, including natural language understanding, dialogue systems, and code generation. Despite evident progress, less attention has been paid to their effectiveness in handling declarative paradigms such as Answer Set Programming (ASP), to date. In this paper we introduce BLAST: The first dedicated benchmarking methodology and associated dataset for evaluating the accuracy of LLMs in generating ASP code. BLAST provides a structured evaluation framework featuring two novel semantic metrics tailored to ASP code generation. The paper presents the results of an empirical evaluation involving ten well-established graph-related problems from the ASP literature and a diverse set of eight state-of-the-art LLMs.

ai.tldr: 提出首个专门评估大语言模型生成ASP代码准确性的基准测试BLAST，包含结构化评估框架和两种新的语义指标，并在十个图问题与八个LLM上进行了实证。
ai.motivation: 现有研究较少关注LLM在声明式编程范式（如ASP）上的表现，缺乏专门的评估方法。
ai.method: 设计BLAST基准，包含结构化测试框架和两种针对ASP代码的语义度量指标。
ai.result: 在十个经典图问题与八个先进LLM上的实验展示了不同模型在ASP生成任务上的准确度差异。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究者系统评估和比较LLM在ASP代码生成方面的能力。

idea_spark.idea: 利用ASP的严格语义定义，设计更细粒度的错误分类（如语法错误、逻辑错误、效率问题）。
idea_spark.risk: ASP领域规模较小，基准的通用性和长期维护可能受限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将逻辑编程的精确性引入LLM评估，启发更多基于形式语义的测试方法。

zh.tldr: 提出首个专门评估大语言模型生成ASP代码准确性的基准测试BLAST，包含结构化评估框架和两种新的语义指标，并在十个图问题与八个LLM上进行了实证。
zh.motivation: 现有研究较少关注LLM在声明式编程范式（如ASP）上的表现，缺乏专门的评估方法。
zh.method: 设计BLAST基准，包含结构化测试框架和两种针对ASP代码的语义度量指标。
zh.result: 在十个经典图问题与八个先进LLM上的实验展示了不同模型在ASP生成任务上的准确度差异。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助研究者系统评估和比较LLM在ASP代码生成方面的能力。

idea_spark.idea: 利用ASP的严格语义定义，设计更细粒度的错误分类（如语法错误、逻辑错误、效率问题）。
idea_spark.risk: ASP领域规模较小，基准的通用性和长期维护可能受限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将逻辑编程的精确性引入LLM评估，启发更多基于形式语义的测试方法。

en.tldr: Introduces BLAST, the first dedicated benchmark for evaluating LLM accuracy in generating ASP code, with a structured framework and two novel semantic metrics, empirically tested on ten graph problems and eight LLMs.
en.motivation: Little attention has been paid to LLM performance on declarative paradigms like ASP, and no dedicated evaluation methodology existed.
en.method: Designs BLAST benchmark with a structured evaluation framework and two new semantic metrics tailored for ASP code generation.
en.result: Empirical evaluation on ten classic graph problems and eight state-of-the-art LLMs reveals varying accuracy across models.
en.help_to_user: Enables systematic assessment and comparison of LLMs for ASP code generation tasks.

idea_spark.idea: Leverage ASP's strict semantics to design fine-grained error categories (e.g., syntax, logic, efficiency) for deeper analysis.
idea_spark.risk: The ASP domain is relatively small, which may limit the benchmark's generality and long-term maintenance.
idea_spark.inspiration: Introducing formal semantics into LLM evaluation inspires more rigorous testing methods based on logical precision.

papers.title: Controllable Spoken Dialogue Generation: An LLM-Driven Grading System for K-12 Non-Native English Learners
papers.summary: Large language models (LLMs) often fail to meet the pedagogical needs of K-12 English learners in non-native contexts due to a proficiency mismatch. To address this widespread challenge, we introduce a proficiency-aligned framework that adapts LLM outputs to learner abilities, using China's national curriculum (CSE) as a representative case. Our framework enables precise control over lexical complexity through a four-tier grading system, supported by a comprehensive suite of new resources: graded vocabulary lists and a multi-turn dialogue corpus.   Our core technical contribution is the \textbf{DDPO} algorithm,Diversity Driven Policy Optimization, a multi-turn GRPO-based approach designed to preserve dialogue diversity while holistically optimizing dialogue quality. This method significantly outperforms conventional approaches, achieving low out-of-vocabulary rates and high diversity while enhancing conversational naturalness and pedagogical value. While grounded in the CSE, our framework is designed for flexibility and can be readily adapted to other educational standards. Our models, data, and code will all be open-sourced, providing a scalable platform for personalized English speaking practice that effectively addresses the unique challenges faced by K-12 learners in non-immersive environments.

ai.tldr: 提出一个基于大语言模型的四层分级系统，通过DDPO算法控制词汇复杂度，为K-12非母语英语学习者生成适配其能力的口语对话。
ai.motivation: 现有大语言模型输出的语言难度与K-12非母语学习者的实际水平不匹配，无法满足教学需求。
ai.method: 基于中国英语能力等级量表构建四层分级词汇表和对话语料库，采用多轮GRPO变体DDPO算法优化对话质量与多样性。
ai.result: DDPO算法在保持低词汇超纲率和高多样性的同时，提升了对话的自然度和教学价值，优于传统方法。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助教师和学习者获得难度可控、自然且具有教学价值的英语口语练习对话。

idea_spark.idea: 将DDPO算法与自适应学习系统结合，实现实时根据学习者表现动态调整对话难度。

zh.method: 基于中国英语能力等级量表构建四层分级词汇表和对话语料库，采用多轮GRPO变体DDPO算法优化对话质量与多样性。
zh.result: DDPO算法在保持低词汇超纲率和高多样性的同时，提升了对话的自然度和教学价值，优于传统方法。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助教师和学习者获得难度可控、自然且具有教学价值的英语口语练习对话。

en.tldr: Proposes a four-tier grading system driven by LLMs, using the DDPO algorithm to control lexical complexity and generate proficiency-aligned spoken dialogues for K-12 non-native English learners.
en.motivation: Existing LLM outputs often mismatch the proficiency levels of K-12 non-native English learners, failing to meet pedagogical needs.
en.method: Builds graded vocabulary lists and a multi-turn dialogue corpus based on China's CSE standards, and employs DDPO, a multi-turn GRPO variant, to optimize dialogue quality and diversity.
en.result: DDPO achieves low out-of-vocabulary rates and high diversity while enhancing conversational naturalness and pedagogical value, outperforming conventional approaches.
en.help_to_user: Helps teachers and learners obtain natural, pedagogically valuable spoken dialogue practice with controllable difficulty.

idea_spark.idea: Combine DDPO with adaptive learning systems to dynamically adjust dialogue difficulty based on real-time learner performance.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on high-quality graded corpora; insufficient coverage may degrade model generalization.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by China's Standards of English (CSE), integrating standardized assessment with generative AI for precise pedagogical alignment.

papers.title: Evaluation of image simulation open source solutions for simulation of synthetic images in lunar environment
papers.summary: Synthetic image generation is one of the crucial input for planetary missions. It enables researchers and engineers to visualize planned planetary missions, test imaging systems and plan exploration activities in a virtual environment before actual deployment. Image simulation is essential for assessing landing sites, detecting hazards, and validating navigation systems in a missions. This study offers a detailed evaluation of various image simulation approaches for the lunar environment, with particular emphasis on the effects of different camera models and light illumination conditions on the quality of synthetic lunar images. These images are produced using real Digital Elevation Models (DEM) and terrain data derived from instruments such as Chandrayaan-2 Orbiter High Resolution Camera (OHRC) and NASA's Wide Angle Camera (WAC), and Narrow Angle Camera (NAC) instruments. This research aims to improve the reliability of synthetic imagery in supporting autonomous navigation and decision-making systems in lunar exploration. This work contributes to the development of more effective tools for generating important information for future lunar missions and enhances the understanding of the moon's surface environment.

ai.tldr: 评估多种开源图像仿真方案在月球环境下生成合成图像的质量，重点分析相机模型和光照条件的影响。
ai.motivation: 为月球任务提供可靠的合成图像，支持自主导航和决策系统。

papers.title: Towards Temporal Compositional Reasoning in Long-Form Sports Videos
papers.summary: Sports videos are a challenging domain for multimodal understanding because they involve complex and dynamic human activities. Despite rapid progress in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs), long-horizon reasoning in sports videos remains difficult, as answering questions requires both locating temporally sparse evidence and integrating it into reasoning. We attribute this limitation to two closely coupled factors: insufficient supervision over temporally dispersed evidence, and the lack of methods that require models to identify, localize, and justify temporal evidence. To address these gaps, we introduce SportsTime, a large-scale benchmark for long-form sports video understanding, comprising 14K+ open-ended QA pairs and 50K+ step-wise temporal evidence annotations. Building on SportsTime, we propose Chain-of-Time Reasoning (CoTR), which treats reasoning as a process of temporally grounded evidence composition. Specifically, during training, CoTR introduces a temporal-reward GRPO to encourage temporally grounded reasoning. During inference, it employs an anchor-observe-infer evidence-seeking loop to iteratively localize, verify, and compose temporal evidence before producing the final answer. Experiments demonstrate the usefulness of SportsTime as a benchmark and the effectiveness of CoTR, which consistently improves temporal compositional reasoning and step-wise grounding quality over strong MLLM baselines.

ai.tldr: 提出SportsTime基准和Chain-of-Time Reasoning方法，提升长体育视频中的时间组合推理能力。
ai.motivation: 现有MLLM在长体育视频中难以定位和整合稀疏的时间证据进行推理。
ai.method: 构建SportsTime基准（含14K+问答对和50K+时间证据标注），提出CoTR方法，训练时用时间奖励GRPO，推理时用锚定-观察-推断循环。
ai.result: CoTR在时间组合推理和逐步证据定位上持续优于强MLLM基线。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何让AI在长视频中基于时间线索进行复杂推理。

idea_spark.idea: 将时间证据定位与推理过程显式分解为锚定、观察、推断三步。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖细粒度时间标注，可能难以扩展到无标注的真实场景。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类逐步查找和整合时间线索的启发，设计迭代证据搜索循环。

en.tldr: Proposes SportsTime benchmark and Chain-of-Time Reasoning method to improve temporal compositional reasoning in long-form sports videos.
en.motivation: Existing MLLMs struggle to locate and integrate temporally sparse evidence for reasoning in long sports videos.
en.method: Constructs SportsTime benchmark (14K+ QA pairs and 50K+ temporal evidence annotations) and proposes CoTR with temporal-reward GRPO for training and an anchor-observe-infer loop for inference.
en.result: CoTR consistently improves temporal compositional reasoning and step-wise grounding quality over strong MLLM baselines.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to enable AI to perform complex reasoning based on temporal cues in long videos.

idea_spark.idea: Explicitly decompose temporal evidence localization and reasoning into anchor, observe, and infer steps.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on fine-grained temporal annotations, which may be hard to scale to unlabeled real-world scenarios.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by human step-by-step searching and integrating temporal clues, designing an iterative evidence-seeking loop.

papers.title: LeHome: A Simulation Environment for Deformable Object Manipulation in Household Scenarios
papers.summary: Household environments present one of the most common, impactful yet challenging application domains for robotics. Within household scenarios, manipulating deformable objects is particularly difficult, both in simulation and real-world execution, due to varied categories and shapes, complex dynamics, and diverse material properties, as well as the lack of reliable deformable-object support in existing simulations. We introduce LeHome, a comprehensive simulation environment designed for deformable object manipulation in household scenarios. LeHome covers a wide spectrum of deformable objects, such as garments and food items, offering high-fidelity dynamics and realistic interactions that existing simulators struggle to simulate accurately. Moreover, LeHome supports multiple robotic embodiments and emphasizes low-cost robots as a core focus, enabling end-to-end evaluation of household tasks on resource-constrained hardware. By bridging the gap between realistic deformable object simulation and practical robotic platforms, LeHome provides a scalable testbed for advancing household robotics. Webpage: https://lehome-web.github.io/ .

ai.tldr: LeHome是一个面向家庭场景的可变形物体操作仿真环境，支持多种可变形物体（如衣物、食物）的高保真动力学模拟，并兼容低成本机器人平台。
ai.motivation: 家庭场景中可变形物体操作是机器人领域的难点，现有仿真器对可变形物体的支持不足，且缺乏面向低成本硬件的端到端评估平台。
ai.method: 构建了涵盖衣物、食物等多样可变形物体的仿真环境，提供高保真动力学与真实交互，并集成多种机器人本体，重点支持低成本机器人。
ai.result: LeHome弥合了可变形物体仿真与实用机器人平台之间的差距，为家庭机器人研究提供了可扩展的测试平台。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员在仿真中高效测试和评估可变形物体操作算法，尤其适用于资源受限的硬件场景。

idea_spark.idea: 结合低成本机器人硬件，开发基于LeHome的迁移学习框架，将仿真中习得的操作策略直接部署到真实家庭机器人上。
idea_spark.risk: 仿真与真实世界之间的动力学差异可能导致策略迁移失败，需引入域随机化或系统辨识方法。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受LeHome对低成本机器人支持的启发，可探索利用仿真数据训练通用可变形物体操作基础模型，降低对昂贵硬件的依赖。

en.tldr: LeHome is a simulation environment for deformable object manipulation in household scenarios, supporting high-fidelity dynamics for diverse objects like garments and food, and emphasizing low-cost robots.
en.motivation: Manipulating deformable objects in household settings is challenging due to varied shapes, dynamics, and material properties, and existing simulators lack adequate support for such objects and low-cost hardware evaluation.
en.method: LeHome covers a wide range of deformable objects with realistic physics and interactions, integrates multiple robot embodiments, and focuses on low-cost robotic platforms for end-to-end task evaluation.
en.result: LeHome bridges the gap between realistic deformable object simulation and practical robotics, providing a scalable testbed for household robotics research.
en.help_to_user: Enables researchers to efficiently test and evaluate deformable object manipulation algorithms in simulation, especially for resource-constrained hardware.

idea_spark.idea: Develop a transfer learning framework using LeHome with low-cost robots to deploy manipulation policies learned in simulation directly to real household robots.
idea_spark.risk: Sim-to-real dynamics mismatch may cause policy transfer failure; domain randomization or system identification methods are needed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by LeHome's focus on low-cost robots, explore training a general-purpose deformable object manipulation foundation model using simulation data to reduce reliance on expensive hardware.

papers.title: Looking Into the Past: Eye Movements Characterize Elements of Autobiographical Recall in Interviews with Holocaust Survivors
papers.summary: Eye movement and memory retrieval are deeply and bidirectionally intertwined, however existing literature is generally confined to controlled lab settings. We investigate the relationship between eye gaze and memory recall in free-form autobiographical recall, which comprises both autonoetic consciousness -- the ability to mentally place oneself in the past or future -- and various affective states. Using a large video corpus of semi-naturalistic interviews with Holocaust survivors (N = 806), we examine eye movements with respect to episodic, semantic, affective, and temporal dimensions of traumatic and highly emotional autobiographical recall. We observe gaze patterns vary significantly across certain temporal contexts, most prominently in vertical eye movements. We additionally train intra-subject sequence models to predict temporal context of sentences from segments of gaze features, and find that eye movements entirely preceding sentence onset are sufficient for prediction. Our results corroborate prior findings in literature linking eye movements to memory in controlled and semi-structured settings, reinforcing the role of eye gaze in retrieving and constructing memories, especially in highly emotional and remote memory recall.

ai.tldr: 通过分析大屠杀幸存者访谈中的眼动数据，发现眼动模式与自传体记忆的时空维度相关，且句子开始前的眼动足以预测时间语境。
ai.motivation: 现有研究多在受控实验室中进行，缺乏对自然情境下眼动与记忆关系的探索。
ai.method: 使用806名大屠杀幸存者的半自然访谈视频，分析眼动与记忆的语义、情感、时间维度，并训练序列模型预测时间语境。
ai.result: 垂直眼动在时间语境中变化显著，句子开始前的眼动特征足以预测时间语境。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解自然回忆中眼动与记忆的关联，可用于情感计算或创伤记忆研究。

idea_spark.idea: 利用眼动特征实时预测回忆的时间维度，开发辅助心理评估工具。
idea_spark.risk: 模型可能受个体差异或回忆内容影响，需更多验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合眼动与自然语言处理，探索记忆构建的神经机制。

en.tldr: Analyzing eye movements in Holocaust survivor interviews reveals gaze patterns vary with temporal context of autobiographical recall, and pre-sentence gaze predicts temporal context.
en.motivation: Existing studies are mostly in controlled labs; naturalistic eye-memory relationships are underexplored.
en.method: Used a large video corpus of semi-naturalistic interviews (N=806) to examine eye movements across episodic, semantic, affective, and temporal dimensions, and trained intra-subject sequence models.
en.result: Vertical eye movements significantly vary with temporal context; gaze before sentence onset suffices for prediction.
en.help_to_user: Provides insights into naturalistic memory recall and eye movement links, useful for affective computing and trauma research.

idea_spark.idea: Develop real-time tools using eye movements to assess temporal aspects of memory in clinical or interview contexts.
idea_spark.risk: Individual differences and recall content may affect model generalizability.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combine eye tracking with NLP to explore neural mechanisms of memory construction.

papers.title: A Probabilistic Framework for Hierarchical Goal Recognition
papers.summary: Goal recognition aims to infer an agent's goal from observations of its behaviour. In realistic settings, recognition can benefit from exploiting hierarchical task structure and reasoning under uncertainty. Planning-based goal recognition has made substantial progress over the past decade, but to the best of our knowledge no existing approach jointly integrates hierarchical task structure with probabilistic inference. In this paper, we introduce the first planning-based probabilistic framework for hierarchical goal recognition over Hierarchical Task Networks (HTNs). We instantiate the framework by exploiting an HTN planner with a three-stage generative model for likelihood estimation, yielding posterior distributions over goal hypotheses. Empirical results show improved recognition performance over the existing HTN-based recognizer on HTN benchmarks. Overall, the framework lays a foundation for probabilistic goal recognition grounded in hierarchical planning structure, moving goal recognition toward more practical settings.

ai.tldr: 提出首个基于规划的概率框架，用于层次任务网络中的目标识别，通过三阶段生成模型估计似然，得到目标假设的后验分布。
ai.motivation: 现有目标识别方法未能同时整合层次任务结构与概率推理，限制了在现实不确定性环境中的应用。
ai.method: 利用HTN规划器构建三阶段生成模型进行似然估计，计算目标假设的后验概率。
ai.result: 在HTN基准测试上，识别性能优于现有基于HTN的识别器。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何结合层次规划与概率推理来更准确地推断智能体的目标。

idea_spark.idea: 将层次规划与概率图模型结合，实现不确定性下的目标推理。
idea_spark.risk: 生成模型假设可能不适用于所有领域，似然估计的计算成本较高。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发将层次任务网络与贝叶斯推理融合，提升目标识别的鲁棒性。

en.motivation: Existing goal recognition methods do not jointly integrate hierarchical task structure with probabilistic inference, limiting applicability in realistic uncertain settings.
en.method: Exploits an HTN planner with a three-stage generative model for likelihood estimation, computing posterior probabilities over goal hypotheses.
en.result: Improved recognition performance over existing HTN-based recognizer on HTN benchmarks.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to combine hierarchical planning with probabilistic reasoning for more accurate agent goal inference.

idea_spark.idea: Combine hierarchical planning with probabilistic graphical models for goal reasoning under uncertainty.
idea_spark.risk: Generative model assumptions may not hold across all domains; likelihood estimation can be computationally expensive.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires fusion of hierarchical task networks with Bayesian inference to enhance robustness of goal recognition.

papers.title: Inter-Stance: A Dyadic Multimodal Corpus for Conversational Stance Analysis
papers.summary: Social interactions dominate our perceptions of the world and shape our daily behavior by attaching social meaning to acts as simple and spontaneous as gestures, facial expressions, voice, and speech. People mimic and otherwise respond to each other's postures, facial expressions, mannerisms, and other verbal and nonverbal behavior, and form appraisals or evaluations in the process. Yet, no publicly-available dataset includes multimodal recordings and self-report measures of multiple persons in social interaction. Dyadic recordings and annotation are lacking. We present a new data corpus of multimodal dyadic interaction (45 dyads, 90 persons) that includes synchronized multi-modality behavior (2D face video, 3D face geometry, thermal spectrum dynamics, voice and speech behavior, physiology (PPG, EDA, heart-rate, blood pressure, and respiration), and self-reported affect of all participants in a communicative interaction scenario. Two types of dyads are included: persons with shared past history and strangers. Annotations include social signals, agreement, disagreement, and neutral stance. With a potent emotion induction, these multimodal data will enable novel modeling of multimodal interpersonal behavior. We present extensive experiments to evaluate multimodal dyadic communication of dyads with and without interpersonal history, and their affect. This new database will make multimodal modeling of social interaction never possible before. The dataset includes 20TB of multimodal data to share with the research community.

ai.tldr: 提出了一个包含45对双人、90人的多模态对话语料库，同步记录面部视频、3D几何、热谱、语音、生理信号和自我报告情感，用于对话立场分析。
ai.motivation: 现有公开数据集缺乏多模态同步记录和多人社交互动中的自我报告测量，尤其是双人对话的标注。
ai.method: 收集45对双人（包括熟人和陌生人）在情感诱导下的多模态行为数据，标注社交信号、同意、不同意和中性立场。
ai.result: 实验评估了有无人际历史的双人多模态沟通及其情感，数据集包含20TB数据可供研究社区使用。
ai.help_to_user: 提供现成的多模态双人互动数据集，支持立场分析、情感计算和社交信号建模研究。

idea_spark.idea: 利用该数据集训练多模态融合模型，预测对话中的立场转变和情感动态。
idea_spark.risk: 数据量大（20TB），处理和分析需要高性能计算资源；隐私和伦理问题需谨慎处理。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合生理信号和面部热谱，探索非言语行为在立场形成中的隐性作用。

en.tldr: Presents a dyadic multimodal corpus of 45 dyads (90 persons) with synchronized face video, 3D geometry, thermal spectrum, voice, physiology, and self-reported affect for conversational stance analysis.
en.motivation: No publicly available dataset includes multimodal recordings and self-report measures of multiple persons in social interaction; dyadic annotations are lacking.
en.method: Collected multimodal data from 45 dyads (including acquaintances and strangers) under emotion induction, annotated with social signals, agreement, disagreement, and neutral stance.
en.result: Experiments evaluated multimodal dyadic communication with and without interpersonal history and affect; dataset comprises 20TB of data shared with the research community.
en.help_to_user: Provides a ready-to-use multimodal dyadic interaction dataset for stance analysis, affective computing, and social signal modeling.

idea_spark.idea: Train multimodal fusion models on this dataset to predict stance shifts and affective dynamics during conversation.
idea_spark.risk: Large data volume (20TB) requires high-performance computing; privacy and ethical issues need careful handling.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combine physiological signals and facial thermal dynamics to explore the implicit role of nonverbal behavior in stance formation.

papers.title: An Artifact-based Agent Framework for Adaptive and Reproducible Medical Image Processing
papers.summary: Medical imaging research is increasingly shifting from controlled benchmark evaluation toward real-world clinical deployment. In such settings, applying analytical methods extends beyond model design to require dataset-aware workflow configuration and provenance tracking. Two requirements therefore become central: \textbf{adaptability}, the ability to configure workflows according to dataset-specific conditions and evolving analytical goals; and \textbf{reproducibility}, the guarantee that all transformations and decisions are explicitly recorded and re-executable. Here, we present an artifact-based agent framework that introduces a semantic layer to augment medical image processing. The framework formalizes intermediate and final outputs through an artifact contract, enabling structured interrogation of workflow state and goal-conditioned assembly of configurations from a modular rule library. Execution is delegated to a workflow executor to preserve deterministic computational graph construction and provenance tracking, while the agent operates locally to comply with most privacy constraints. We evaluate the framework on real-world clinical CT and MRI cohorts, demonstrating adaptive configuration synthesis, deterministic reproducibility across repeated executions, and artifact-grounded semantic querying. These results show that adaptive workflow configuration can be achieved without compromising reproducibility in heterogeneous clinical environments.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于工件的智能体框架，在医学图像处理中同时实现自适应工作流配置和可重复性。
ai.motivation: 医学影像研究从基准评估转向临床部署时，需要适应数据集特定条件并保证可重复性。
ai.method: 通过工件契约形式化中间和最终输出，利用模块化规则库进行目标条件组装，由工作流执行器保持确定性计算图。
ai.result: 在真实CT和MRI数据上验证了自适应配置合成、确定性可重复性和基于工件的语义查询。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何在异构临床环境中平衡自适应性与可重复性。

idea_spark.inspiration: 将软件工程中的契约式设计引入医学图像处理工作流管理。

en.tldr: An artifact-based agent framework that achieves both adaptive workflow configuration and reproducibility in medical image processing.
en.motivation: Medical imaging research shifting to clinical deployment requires adaptability to dataset-specific conditions and reproducibility guarantees.
en.method: Formalizes intermediate and final outputs via artifact contracts, assembles configurations from a modular rule library, and delegates execution to a workflow executor for deterministic computation.
en.result: Validated on real CT and MRI cohorts, demonstrating adaptive configuration synthesis, deterministic reproducibility, and artifact-grounded semantic querying.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to balance adaptability and reproducibility in heterogeneous clinical environments.

idea_spark.idea: Use artifact contracts to enable semantic querying of workflow state, supporting dynamic configuration adjustments.
idea_spark.risk: Semantic definitions of artifact contracts may be incomplete, leading to inaccurate query results.
idea_spark.inspiration: Introduce design-by-contract from software engineering into medical image processing workflow management.

papers.title: Preference Heads in Large Language Models: A Mechanistic Framework for Interpretable Personalization
papers.summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) exhibit strong implicit personalization ability, yet most existing approaches treat this behavior as a black box, relying on prompt engineering or fine tuning on user data. In this work, we adopt a mechanistic interpretability perspective and hypothesize the existence of a sparse set of Preference Heads, attention heads that encode user specific stylistic and topical preferences and exert a causal influence on generation. We introduce Differential Preference Steering (DPS), a training free framework that (1) identifies Preference Heads through causal masking analysis and (2) leverages them for controllable and interpretable personalization at inference time. DPS computes a Preference Contribution Score (PCS) for each attention head, directly measuring its causal impact on user aligned outputs. During decoding, we contrast model predictions with and without Preference Heads, amplifying the difference between personalized and generic logits to selectively strengthen preference aligned continuations. Experiments on widely used personalization benchmarks across multiple LLMs demonstrate consistent gains in personalization fidelity while preserving content coherence and low computational overhead. Beyond empirical improvements, DPS provides a mechanistic explanation of where and how personalization emerges within transformer architectures. Our implementation is publicly available.

ai.tldr: 提出偏好头假设和差分偏好引导框架，实现大语言模型可解释的个性化生成。
ai.motivation: 现有大语言模型个性化方法多为黑盒，缺乏可解释性和可控性。
ai.method: 通过因果掩码分析识别偏好头，在解码时对比有无偏好头的预测，放大个性化与通用logits的差异。
ai.result: 在多个模型和基准上提升个性化保真度，同时保持内容连贯性和低计算开销。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解模型如何实现个性化，并提供一种无需训练即可控制个性化程度的方法。

idea_spark.idea: 将因果分析引入注意力头功能定位，实现细粒度的模型行为调控。
idea_spark.risk: 偏好头稀疏性假设可能不适用于所有任务或模型规模。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从神经科学中功能定位的思路启发，将模型内部组件与特定行为因果关联。

en.tldr: Proposes Preference Heads hypothesis and Differential Preference Steering framework for interpretable personalization in LLMs.
en.motivation: Existing personalization methods treat LLMs as black boxes, lacking interpretability and controllability.
en.method: Identifies Preference Heads via causal masking analysis and contrasts model predictions with/without them during decoding to amplify preference-aligned logits.
en.result: Achieves consistent gains in personalization fidelity across multiple models and benchmarks while maintaining coherence and low overhead.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how personalization emerges in LLMs and provides a training-free method to control it.

idea_spark.idea: Introduces causal analysis to pinpoint functional roles of attention heads for fine-grained model behavior modulation.
idea_spark.risk: Sparsity assumption of Preference Heads may not hold across all tasks or model scales.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by functional localization in neuroscience, causally linking internal components to specific behaviors.

papers.title: Depth-Aware Rover: A Study of Edge AI and Monocular Vision for Real-World Implementation
papers.summary: This study analyses simulated and real-world implementations of depth-aware rover navigation, highlighting the transition from stereo vision to monocular depth estimation using edge AI. A Unity-based lunar terrain simulator with stereo cameras and OpenCV's StereoSGBM was used to generate disparity maps. A physical rover built on Raspberry Pi 4 employed UniDepthV2 for monocular metric depth estimation and YOLO12n for real-time object detection. While stereo vision yielded higher accuracy in simulation, the monocular approach proved more robust and cost-effective in real-world deployment, achieving 0.1 FPS for depth and 10 FPS for detection.

ai.tldr: 本研究分析了深度感知漫游车在模拟和真实环境中的导航，从立体视觉转向基于边缘AI的单目深度估计，模拟中立体视觉精度更高，但真实部署中单目方法更鲁棒且成本更低。
ai.motivation: 在资源受限的漫游车平台上实现低成本、鲁棒的深度感知导航，替代昂贵的立体视觉方案。
ai.method: 使用Unity模拟器生成立体视差图，在树莓派4上部署UniDepthV2进行单目深度估计，YOLO12n进行实时目标检测。
ai.result: 立体视觉在模拟中精度更高，单目方法在真实部署中更鲁棒，深度估计0.1 FPS，检测10 FPS。
ai.help_to_user: 展示了如何在边缘设备上结合单目深度估计和目标检测实现漫游车导航，适合低成本机器人项目参考。

idea_spark.idea: 在真实环境中融合单目深度估计与立体视觉的混合方案，以平衡精度与鲁棒性。
idea_spark.risk: 单目深度估计在复杂光照或纹理缺失场景下可能失效，需额外传感器辅助。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用边缘AI在低功耗设备上实现实时深度感知，启发低成本自主导航系统的设计。

en.tldr: This study analyzes depth-aware rover navigation in simulation and real-world, transitioning from stereo vision to monocular depth estimation with edge AI; stereo vision is more accurate in simulation, while monocular is more robust and cost-effective in real deployment.
en.motivation: To achieve low-cost, robust depth-aware navigation on resource-constrained rover platforms, replacing expensive stereo vision.
en.method: Used Unity simulator for stereo disparity maps, deployed UniDepthV2 for monocular depth estimation and YOLO12n for real-time object detection on Raspberry Pi 4.
en.result: Stereo vision achieved higher accuracy in simulation; monocular approach was more robust in real deployment, with 0.1 FPS for depth and 10 FPS for detection.
en.help_to_user: Demonstrates how to combine monocular depth estimation and object detection on edge devices for rover navigation, suitable for low-cost robotics projects.

idea_spark.idea: A hybrid scheme fusing monocular depth estimation with stereo vision in real environments to balance accuracy and robustness.
idea_spark.risk: Monocular depth estimation may fail in complex lighting or textureless scenes, requiring additional sensor support.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leveraging edge AI for real-time depth perception on low-power devices inspires the design of low-cost autonomous navigation systems.

papers.title: AgentSearchBench: A Benchmark for AI Agent Search in the Wild
papers.summary: The rapid growth of AI agent ecosystems is transforming how complex tasks are delegated and executed, creating a new challenge of identifying suitable agents for a given task. Unlike traditional tools, agent capabilities are often compositional and execution-dependent, making them difficult to assess from textual descriptions alone. However, existing research and benchmarks typically assume well-specified functionalities, controlled candidate pools, or only executable task queries, leaving realistic agent search scenarios insufficiently studied. We introduce AgentSearchBench, a large-scale benchmark for agent search in the wild, built from nearly 10,000 real-world agents across multiple providers. The benchmark formalizes agent search as retrieval and reranking problems under both executable task queries and high-level task descriptions, and evaluates relevance using execution-grounded performance signals. Experiments reveal a consistent gap between semantic similarity and actual agent performance, exposing the limitations of description-based retrieval and reranking methods. We further show that lightweight behavioral signals, including execution-aware probing, can substantially improve ranking quality, highlighting the importance of incorporating execution signals into agent discovery. Our code is available at https://github.com/Bingo-W/AgentSearchBench.

ai.tldr: 提出AgentSearchBench，一个基于近万个真实智能体的大规模基准，用于评估在可执行任务查询和高层描述下的智能体搜索，发现语义相似度与实际性能存在差距，并证明轻量级执行信号可显著提升排序质量。
ai.motivation: 现有基准假设功能明确、候选池可控或仅支持可执行查询，未充分研究真实场景中智能体能力组合与执行依赖带来的搜索挑战。
ai.method: 从多个提供商收集近万个真实智能体，将搜索形式化为检索与重排序问题，使用执行驱动的性能信号评估相关性。
ai.result: 语义相似度与实际智能体性能之间存在一致差距，基于描述的检索与重排序方法受限；引入执行感知探测等轻量级行为信号可大幅改善排序质量。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解当前智能体搜索方法的局限性，并提供利用执行信号提升搜索效果的思路。

idea_spark.idea: 设计一个在线学习框架，根据用户对智能体执行结果的反馈持续优化搜索排序模型。
idea_spark.risk: 执行信号采集可能带来额外开销，且不同智能体执行环境差异大，信号标准化困难。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴推荐系统中的协同过滤思想，利用用户-智能体交互历史来预测任务适配度。

zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解当前智能体搜索方法的局限性，并提供利用执行信号提升搜索效果的思路。

idea_spark.risk: 执行信号采集可能带来额外开销，且不同智能体执行环境差异大，信号标准化困难。

en.tldr: Introduces AgentSearchBench, a large-scale benchmark built from nearly 10,000 real-world agents, to evaluate agent search under executable task queries and high-level descriptions, revealing a gap between semantic similarity and actual performance, and showing that lightweight behavioral signals significantly improve ranking quality.
en.motivation: Existing benchmarks assume well-specified functionalities, controlled candidate pools, or only executable queries, leaving realistic agent search scenarios with compositional and execution-dependent capabilities insufficiently studied.
en.method: Collects nearly 10,000 real-world agents from multiple providers, formalizes search as retrieval and reranking problems, and evaluates relevance using execution-grounded performance signals.
en.result: A consistent gap exists between semantic similarity and actual agent performance; description-based retrieval and reranking are limited, while lightweight behavioral signals like execution-aware probing substantially improve ranking quality.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the limitations of current agent search methods and provides insights on leveraging execution signals to improve search effectiveness.

idea_spark.idea: Design an online learning framework that continuously optimizes the search ranking model based on user feedback on agent execution outcomes.
idea_spark.risk: Collecting execution signals may introduce additional overhead, and the diversity of agent execution environments makes signal standardization challenging.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draw inspiration from collaborative filtering in recommendation systems, using user-agent interaction history to predict task suitability.

papers.title: Recognition Without Authorization: LLMs and the Moral Order of Online Advice
papers.summary: Large language models are increasingly used to mediate everyday interpersonal dilemmas, yet how their advisory defaults interact with the concentrated moral orders of specific communities remains poorly understood. This article compares four assistant-style LLMs with community-endorsed advice on 11,565 posts from r/relationship_advice, using the subreddit as a concentrated, vote-ratified moral formation whose prescriptive clarity makes divergence measurable. Across models, LLMs identify many of the same dynamics as human commenters, but are markedly less likely to convert that recognition into directive authorization for action. The gap is sharpest where community consensus is strongest: on high-consensus posts involving abuse or safety threats, models recommend exit at roughly half the human rate while maintaining elevated levels of hedging, validation, and therapeutic framing.   The article describes this pattern as recognition without authorization: the capacity to register harm while withholding socially ratified permission for consequential action. This divergence is not incidental but structural: a portable advisory style that remains validating, risk-averse, and weakly directive across contexts. Safety alignment is one plausible contributor to this pattern, alongside training-data averaging and broader assistant design. The article argues that model divergence can be reframed from a technical error to a way of seeing what standardized assistant norms flatten when they encounter situated moral worlds.

ai.tldr: 比较了四个助手型LLM与Reddit社区在11,565条关系建议上的差异，发现LLM能识别问题但较少授权行动，尤其在安全威胁上建议退出率仅为人类一半。
ai.motivation: LLM越来越多地用于调解人际困境，但其建议默认值与特定社区的集中道德秩序如何相互作用尚不清楚。
ai.method: 将四个助手型LLM与r/relationship_advice社区认可的建议在11,565条帖子中进行比较，利用该子版块作为投票批准的集中道德形态。
ai.result: LLM识别出与人类评论者相同的动态，但将识别转化为行动授权的可能性显著降低；在高共识帖子（涉及虐待或安全威胁）上，模型建议退出的比率约为人类的一半，同时保持更多的对冲、验证和治疗性框架。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解LLM在提供建议时的结构性偏差，即倾向于验证和风险规避，而非授权果断行动。

idea_spark.idea: 设计一个“道德情境感知”的LLM，根据社区共识强度动态调整建议的授权程度，在安全威胁时更果断。
idea_spark.risk: 过度授权可能导致LLM给出不当的激进建议，增加责任风险；而保持现状则可能削弱LLM在紧急情况下的实用性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从人类社区中学习“何时授权”的隐含规则，通过强化学习或上下文学习使LLM模仿不同情境下的授权模式。

en.tldr: Comparing four assistant LLMs with community-endorsed advice on 11,565 relationship posts, LLMs recognize problems but authorize action less, especially on safety threats where exit recommendation rate is half that of humans.
en.motivation: LLMs are increasingly used to mediate everyday interpersonal dilemmas, yet how their advisory defaults interact with the concentrated moral orders of specific communities remains poorly understood.
en.method: Comparing four assistant-style LLMs with community-endorsed advice on 11,565 posts from r/relationship_advice, using the subreddit as a concentrated, vote-ratified moral formation.
en.result: LLMs identify many of the same dynamics as human commenters but are markedly less likely to convert recognition into directive authorization; on high-consensus posts involving abuse or safety threats, models recommend exit at roughly half the human rate while maintaining elevated hedging, validation, and therapeutic framing.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the structural bias of LLMs in advice-giving, favoring validation and risk aversion over decisive authorization.

idea_spark.idea: Design a 'moral-context-aware' LLM that dynamically adjusts authorization level based on community consensus strength, being more decisive in safety threats.
idea_spark.risk: Over-authorization may lead to inappropriate aggressive advice, increasing liability; maintaining status quo may reduce LLM utility in emergencies.
idea_spark.inspiration: Learn implicit 'when to authorize' rules from human communities via reinforcement learning or in-context learning to mimic authorization patterns across contexts.

papers.title: Measuring and Mitigating Persona Distortions from AI Writing Assistance
papers.summary: Hundreds of millions of people use artificial intelligence (AI) for writing assistance. Here, we evaluated how AI writing assistance distorts writer personas - their perceived beliefs, personality, and identity. In three large-scale experiments, writers (N=2,939) wrote political opinion paragraphs with and without AI assistance. Separate groups of readers (N=11,091) blindly evaluated these paragraphs across 29 socially salient dimensions of reader perception, spanning political opinion, writing quality, writer personality, emotions, and demographics. AI writing assistance produced persona distortions across all dimensions: with AI, writers seemed more opinionated, competent, and positive, and their perceived demographic profile shifted towards more privileged groups. Writers objected to many of the observed distortions, yet continued to prefer AI-assisted text even when made aware of them. We successfully mitigated objectionable persona distortions at the model level by training reward models on our experimental data (10,008 paragraphs, 2,903,596 ratings) to steer AI outputs towards faithful representation of writer stance. However, this came at a cost to user acceptance, suggesting an entanglement between desirable and undesirable properties of AI writing assistance that may be difficult to resolve. Together, our findings demonstrate that persona distortions from AI writing assistance are pervasive and persistent even under realistic conditions of human oversight, which carries implications for public discourse, trust, and democratic deliberation that scale with AI adoption.

ai.tldr: 研究AI写作辅助如何扭曲作者的人格感知，并尝试通过奖励模型进行缓解。
ai.motivation: 数百万人使用AI辅助写作，但AI可能改变作者在读者眼中的形象，影响公共讨论和信任。
ai.method: 通过三个大规模实验（作者N=2,939，读者N=11,091），评估AI辅助写作对29个社会维度的感知影响，并训练奖励模型（10,008段落，2,903,596评分）来引导AI输出忠实于作者立场。
ai.result: AI辅助写作在所有维度上产生人格扭曲，作者反对但偏好AI文本；奖励模型可缓解扭曲但降低用户接受度。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解AI写作辅助可能带来的感知偏差，并提供一种缓解方法。

idea_spark.idea: 开发自适应AI写作助手，根据用户反馈动态调整输出以平衡忠实度和接受度。
idea_spark.risk: 缓解扭曲可能降低用户满意度，需权衡忠实度与用户体验。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从社会心理学角度设计AI系统，考虑用户身份和感知的复杂性。

en.tldr: AI writing assistance distorts writer personas across multiple dimensions, and reward models can mitigate these distortions at a cost to user acceptance.
en.motivation: Hundreds of millions use AI for writing, but it may alter how writers are perceived, affecting public discourse and trust.
en.method: Three large-scale experiments (N=2,939 writers, N=11,091 readers) evaluated AI's impact on 29 social perception dimensions; reward models were trained on 10,008 paragraphs and 2,903,596 ratings to steer AI outputs toward faithful representation.
en.result: AI assistance caused persona distortions across all dimensions; writers objected but preferred AI text; reward models mitigated distortions but reduced user acceptance.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand and mitigate persona distortions from AI writing assistance.

idea_spark.idea: Develop adaptive AI writing assistants that balance faithfulness and user acceptance through real-time feedback.
idea_spark.risk: Mitigating distortions may lower user satisfaction, requiring careful trade-offs.
idea_spark.inspiration: Design AI systems considering social psychology and the complexity of user identity and perception.

papers.title: Verbal Confidence Saturation in 3-9B Open-Weight Instruction-Tuned LLMs: A Pre-Registered Psychometric Validity Screen
papers.summary: Verbal confidence elicitation is widely used to extract uncertainty estimates from LLMs. We tested whether seven instruction-tuned open-weight models (3-9B parameters, four families) produce verbalised confidence that meets minimal validity criteria for item-level Type-2 discrimination under minimal numeric elicitation with greedy decoding. In a pre-registered study (OSF: osf.io/azbvx), 524 TriviaQA items were administered under numeric (0-100) and categorical (10-class) elicitation to eight models at Q5_K_M quantisation on consumer hardware, yielding 8,384 deterministic trials. A psychometric validity screen was applied to each model-format cell. All seven instruct models were classified Invalid on numeric confidence (H2 confirmed, 7/7 vs. predicted >=4/7), with a mean ceiling rate of 91.7% (H1 confirmed). Categorical elicitation did not rescue validity. Instead, it disrupted task performance in six of seven models, producing accuracy below 5% (H4 not confirmed). Token-level logprobability did not usefully predict verbalised confidence under the observed variance regime (H5 confirmed, mean cross-validated R^2 < 0.01). Within the reasoning-distilled model, reasoning-trace length showed a strong negative partial correlation with confidence (rho = -0.36, p < .001), consistent with the Reasoning Contamination Effect. These results do not imply that internal uncertainty representations are absent. They show that minimal verbal elicitation fails to preserve internal signals at the output interface in this model-size regime. Psychometric screening should precede any downstream use of such signals.

ai.tldr: 对3-9B参数的开源指令微调LLM进行预注册心理测量学筛查，发现其口头置信度在最小数值提示下不满足项目级Type-2区分效度标准。
ai.motivation: 验证口头置信度提取方法在小规模开源指令微调模型中的有效性，确保其满足心理测量学最低标准。
ai.method: 在8个模型（7个指令微调+1个基础模型）上，使用数值（0-100）和分类（10类）两种提示方式，对524个TriviaQA项目进行确定性贪婪解码测试，共8,384次试验，并应用心理测量学效度筛查。
ai.result: 所有7个指令微调模型在数值置信度上均被判定为无效（平均天花板率91.7%），分类提示未能改善效度反而降低任务性能，token级对数概率无法有效预测口头置信度，推理蒸馏模型中推理链长度与置信度呈强负相关。
ai.help_to_user: 提示用户在使用口头置信度信号前必须进行心理测量学筛查，避免直接依赖不可靠的置信度输出。

idea_spark.idea: 探索非口头置信度提取方法（如基于logit的置信度）或更复杂的提示设计（如思维链校准）以改善小模型置信度质量。
idea_spark.risk: 口头置信度可能完全无法反映内部不确定性，过度依赖可能导致错误决策。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受推理污染效应启发，可研究推理链长度与置信度偏差的关系，设计自适应提示策略。

zh.motivation: 验证口头置信度提取方法在小规模开源指令微调模型中的有效性，确保其满足心理测量学最低标准。
