en.tldr: A pre-registered psychometric validity screen of 3-9B open-weight instruction-tuned LLMs shows that verbal confidence fails to meet minimal Type-2 discrimination criteria under minimal numeric elicitation with greedy decoding.
en.motivation: To test whether verbal confidence from small instruction-tuned LLMs meets psychometric validity standards for item-level uncertainty estimation.
en.method: Eight models (seven instruct-tuned, one base) were tested on 524 TriviaQA items using numeric (0-100) and categorical (10-class) elicitation with greedy decoding, yielding 8,384 deterministic trials, followed by a psychometric validity screen.
en.result: All seven instruct models were invalid on numeric confidence (mean ceiling rate 91.7%), categorical elicitation disrupted accuracy in six models, token-level logprobability poorly predicted confidence (R^2 < 0.01), and reasoning-trace length showed a strong negative correlation with confidence in the reasoning-distilled model.
en.help_to_user: Highlights the necessity of psychometric screening before using verbal confidence signals from small LLMs in downstream tasks.

idea_spark.idea: Explore non-verbal confidence methods (e.g., logit-based) or more sophisticated prompting (e.g., chain-of-thought calibration) to improve confidence quality in small models.
idea_spark.risk: Verbal confidence may completely fail to reflect internal uncertainty, leading to erroneous decisions if relied upon.
idea_spark.inspiration: The reasoning contamination effect inspires investigation into how reasoning chain length biases confidence, potentially leading to adaptive prompting strategies.

papers.title: How Large Language Models Balance Internal Knowledge with User and Document Assertions
papers.summary: Large language models (LLMs) often need to balance their internal parametric knowledge with external information, such as user beliefs and content from retrieved documents, in real-world scenarios like RAG or chat-based systems. A model's ability to reliably process these sources is key to system safety. Previous studies on knowledge conflict and sycophancy are limited to a binary conflict paradigm, primarily exploring conflicts between parametric knowledge and either a document or a user, but ignoring the interactive environment where all three sources exist simultaneously. To fill this gap, we propose a three-source interaction framework and systematically evaluate 27 LLMs from 3 families on 2 datasets. Our findings reveal general patterns: most models rely more on document assertions than user assertions, and this preference is reinforced by post-training. Furthermore, our behavioral analysis shows that most models are impressionable, unable to effectively discriminate between helpful and harmful external information. To address this, we demonstrate that fine-tuning on diverse source interaction data can significantly increase a model's discrimination abilities. In short, our work paves the way for developing trustworthy LLMs that can effectively and reliably integrate multiple sources of information. Code is available at https://github.com/shuowl/llm-source-balancing.

ai.tldr: 提出三源交互框架，系统评估27个LLM在内部知识、用户断言和文档断言间的平衡能力，发现模型更依赖文档断言且偏好被后训练强化，并通过微调提升区分能力。
ai.motivation: 现有知识冲突和谄媚研究局限于二元冲突范式，忽略了内部知识、用户断言和文档断言三者同时存在的交互环境，无法全面评估LLM在真实场景中的信息整合能力。
ai.method: 构建三源交互框架，在2个数据集上对3个家族共27个LLM进行系统评估，包括行为分析和微调实验。
ai.result: 大多数模型更依赖文档断言而非用户断言，后训练强化了这一偏好；模型难以区分有益和有害的外部信息；微调可显著提升区分能力。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解LLM在处理多源信息时的行为偏好和潜在风险，指导更安全地使用RAG和对话系统。

idea_spark.idea: 设计自适应权重机制，让模型根据来源可信度动态调整内部知识、用户断言和文档断言的融合比例。
idea_spark.risk: 微调可能引入新的偏见或过度拟合特定交互模式，需在多样化场景中验证泛化性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类认知中来源监控理论启发，可探索LLM是否具备类似的人类来源记忆和元认知能力。

zh.tldr: 提出三源交互框架，系统评估27个LLM在内部知识、用户断言和文档断言间的平衡能力，发现模型更依赖文档断言且偏好被后训练强化，并通过微调提升区分能力。

idea_spark.idea: 设计自适应权重机制，让模型根据来源可信度动态调整内部知识、用户断言和文档断言的融合比例。
idea_spark.risk: 微调可能引入新的偏见或过度拟合特定交互模式，需在多样化场景中验证泛化性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类认知中来源监控理论启发，可探索LLM是否具备类似的人类来源记忆和元认知能力。

en.tldr: Proposes a three-source interaction framework to systematically evaluate 27 LLMs on balancing internal knowledge, user assertions, and document assertions, revealing a preference for document assertions reinforced by post-training, and improving discrimination via fine-tuning.
en.motivation: Previous studies on knowledge conflict and sycophancy are limited to binary conflict paradigms, ignoring the interactive environment where internal knowledge, user assertions, and document assertions coexist, which is crucial for real-world LLM safety.
en.method: A three-source interaction framework is introduced, evaluating 27 LLMs from 3 families on 2 datasets through behavioral analysis and fine-tuning experiments.
en.result: Most models rely more on document assertions than user assertions, a preference reinforced by post-training; models struggle to discriminate helpful from harmful external information; fine-tuning significantly improves discrimination.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand LLM behavioral preferences and risks when integrating multiple information sources, guiding safer use of RAG and chat systems.

idea_spark.idea: Design an adaptive weighting mechanism that dynamically adjusts the fusion ratio of internal knowledge, user assertions, and document assertions based on source credibility.
idea_spark.risk: Fine-tuning may introduce new biases or overfit specific interaction patterns, requiring validation across diverse scenarios.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by source monitoring theory in human cognition, explore whether LLMs possess analogous source memory and metacognitive abilities.

papers.title: Optimal Question Selection from a Large Question Bank for Clinical Field Recovery in Conversational Psychiatric Intake
papers.summary: Psychiatric intake is a sequential, high-stakes information-gathering process in which clinicians must decide what to ask, in what order, and how to interpret incomplete or ambiguous responses under limited time. Despite growing interest in conversational AI for healthcare, there is still limited infrastructure for conversational AI in this application. Accordingly, we formulate this task as a question-selection problem with clinically grounded questions, known target information, and controllable patient difficulty. We also introduce a task-specific question-selection benchmark based on a bank of 655 clinician-authored intake questions and corresponding synthetic patient vignettes with 5 different behavioral conditions. In our evaluation, we compare random questioning, a clinical psychiatric intake form baseline, and an LLM-guided adaptive policy across 300 interview sessions spanning four patients and five behavioral conditions. Across the benchmark, the clinically ordered fixed form substantially outperforms random questioning, and the LLM-guided policy achieves the strongest overall recovery. The advantage of adaptation grows sharply under patient behavior that is less amenable to field recovery, especially under guarded-concise conditions. These findings suggest that performance in conversational clinical systems depends not only on language understanding after information is disclosed, but also on whether the system reaches the right topics within a limited interaction budget. More broadly, the benchmark provides a controlled framework for studying how clinical structure and adaptive follow-up contribute to information recovery in interactive clinical machine learning.

ai.tldr: 提出一个基于临床问题库的对话式精神科初诊问题选择基准，比较随机提问、临床固定表单和LLM自适应策略，发现自适应策略在信息恢复上表现最佳，尤其在患者不配合时优势明显。
ai.motivation: 精神科初诊是高风险的信息收集过程，现有对话AI基础设施不足，需要系统研究如何高效选择问题以在有限交互中恢复临床信息。
ai.method: 构建包含655个临床问题的问题库和5种行为条件的合成患者案例，设计随机、固定表单和LLM引导自适应三种策略，在300次模拟访谈中评估信息恢复效果。
ai.result: 临床固定表单显著优于随机提问，LLM自适应策略整体恢复最强；在患者回答简洁或防御性时，自适应策略的优势更加突出。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解在临床对话系统中，问题选择策略比语言理解更关键，为设计高效的精神科AI初诊工具提供基准和方法。

idea_spark.idea: 结合患者历史记录和实时情感分析，动态调整问题难度和顺序，进一步提升信息恢复效率。
idea_spark.risk: 合成患者数据可能无法完全模拟真实临床复杂性，导致策略在真实场景中泛化不足。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受临床医生在有限时间内优先问关键问题的启发，可探索强化学习优化问题序列，并引入不确定性量化来指导提问时机。

zh.tldr: 提出一个基于临床问题库的对话式精神科初诊问题选择基准，比较随机提问、临床固定表单和LLM自适应策略，发现自适应策略在信息恢复上表现最佳，尤其在患者不配合时优势明显。
zh.motivation: 精神科初诊是高风险的信息收集过程，现有对话AI基础设施不足，需要系统研究如何高效选择问题以在有限交互中恢复临床信息。
zh.method: 构建包含655个临床问题的问题库和5种行为条件的合成患者案例，设计随机、固定表单和LLM引导自适应三种策略，在300次模拟访谈中评估信息恢复效果。
zh.result: 临床固定表单显著优于随机提问，LLM自适应策略整体恢复最强；在患者回答简洁或防御性时，自适应策略的优势更加突出。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助理解在临床对话系统中，问题选择策略比语言理解更关键，为设计高效的精神科AI初诊工具提供基准和方法。

idea_spark.idea: 结合患者历史记录和实时情感分析，动态调整问题难度和顺序，进一步提升信息恢复效率。
idea_spark.risk: 合成患者数据可能无法完全模拟真实临床复杂性，导致策略在真实场景中泛化不足。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受临床医生在有限时间内优先问关键问题的启发，可探索强化学习优化问题序列，并引入不确定性量化来指导提问时机。

en.tldr: Proposes a benchmark for question selection in conversational psychiatric intake using a bank of 655 clinician-authored questions, comparing random, fixed-form, and LLM-adaptive policies, finding adaptive policy achieves best clinical field recovery, especially under guarded patient behavior.
en.motivation: Psychiatric intake is a high-stakes information-gathering process with limited conversational AI infrastructure; systematic study of question selection is needed to maximize information recovery under time constraints.
en.method: Constructed a question bank of 655 clinician-authored questions and synthetic patient vignettes with 5 behavioral conditions; evaluated random questioning, a clinical fixed form, and an LLM-guided adaptive policy across 300 simulated interviews.
en.result: The clinically ordered fixed form outperformed random questioning; the LLM-adaptive policy achieved the strongest overall recovery, with greater advantage under guarded-concise patient behavior.
en.help_to_user: Provides a controlled benchmark and insights for designing conversational clinical systems, highlighting that question selection strategy is more critical than language understanding for information recovery.

idea_spark.idea: Incorporate patient history and real-time sentiment analysis to dynamically adjust question difficulty and order, further improving information recovery efficiency.
idea_spark.risk: Synthetic patient data may not fully capture real clinical complexity, potentially limiting generalization of strategies to real-world settings.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by clinicians prioritizing key questions under time pressure; explore reinforcement learning to optimize question sequences and use uncertainty quantification to guide when to ask follow-ups.

papers.title: Automation-Exploit: A Multi-Agent LLM Framework for Adaptive Offensive Security with Digital Twin-Based Risk-Mitigated Exploitation
papers.summary: The offensive security landscape is highly fragmented: enterprise platforms avoid memory-corruption vulnerabilities due to Denial of Service (DoS) risks, Automatic Exploit Generation (AEG) systems suffer from semantic blindness, and Large Language Model (LLM) agents face safety alignment filters and "Live Fire" execution hazards. We introduce Automation-Exploit, a fully autonomous Multi-Agent System (MAS) framework designed for adaptive offensive security in complex black-box scenarios. It bridges the abstraction gap between reconnaissance and exploitation by autonomously exfiltrating executables and contextual intelligence across multiple protocols, using this data to fuel both logical and binary attack chains. The framework introduces an adaptive safety architecture to mitigate DoS risks. While it natively resolves logical and web-based vulnerabilities, it employs a conditional isomorphic validation for high-risk memory-corruption flaws: if the target binary is successfully exfiltrated, it dynamically instantiates a cross-platform digital twin. By enforcing strict state synchronization, including libc alignment and runtime file descriptor hooking, potentially destructive payloads are iteratively debugged in an isolated replica. This enables a highly risk-mitigated "one-shot" execution on the physical target. Empirical evaluations across eight scenarios, including undocumented zero-day environments to rule out LLM data contamination, validate the framework's architectural resilience, demonstrating its ability to prevent "live fire" crashes and execute risk-mitigated compromises on actual targets.

ai.tldr: 提出一个名为Automation-Exploit的多智能体LLM框架，用于自适应黑盒攻击安全，通过数字孪生技术降低内存破坏漏洞的利用风险。
ai.motivation: 现有攻击安全平台因拒绝服务风险回避内存破坏漏洞，自动利用生成系统存在语义盲区，LLM代理面临安全对齐过滤和实弹执行危险。
ai.method: 框架采用多智能体系统，自主提取可执行文件和上下文情报，驱动逻辑与二进制攻击链；引入自适应安全架构缓解拒绝服务风险；对高风险内存破坏漏洞使用条件同构验证，通过数字孪生进行迭代调试。
ai.result: 在八个场景（包括未公开零日环境）中验证了框架的架构韧性，能防止实弹崩溃并在实际目标上执行风险缓解的利用。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助安全研究人员在复杂黑盒场景中自动化、低风险地执行攻击测试，尤其适用于内存破坏漏洞的利用。

idea_spark.idea: 结合数字孪生与LLM代理，实现高风险漏洞利用的离线调试与一次执行。
idea_spark.risk: 数字孪生的状态同步精度可能不足，导致实际执行时仍存在崩溃风险。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从企业平台对拒绝服务风险的回避出发，设计自适应安全机制来平衡攻击能力与系统稳定性。

idea_spark.risk: 数字孪生的状态同步精度可能不足，导致实际执行时仍存在崩溃风险。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从企业平台对拒绝服务风险的回避出发，设计自适应安全机制来平衡攻击能力与系统稳定性。

en.tldr: Proposes Automation-Exploit, a multi-agent LLM framework for adaptive black-box offensive security, using digital twins to mitigate risks of memory-corruption exploitation.
en.motivation: Enterprise platforms avoid memory-corruption vulnerabilities due to DoS risks, AEG systems suffer semantic blindness, and LLM agents face safety filters and live-fire hazards.
en.method: The framework uses a multi-agent system to autonomously exfiltrate executables and contextual intelligence, driving logical and binary attack chains; introduces adaptive safety architecture to mitigate DoS risks; employs conditional isomorphic validation for high-risk memory-corruption flaws via digital twin debugging.
en.result: Validated across eight scenarios including undocumented zero-day environments, demonstrating ability to prevent live-fire crashes and execute risk-mitigated compromises on actual targets.
en.help_to_user: Helps security researchers automate offensive testing in complex black-box scenarios with reduced risk, especially for memory-corruption exploitation.

idea_spark.idea: Combine digital twins with LLM agents to enable offline debugging and one-shot execution of high-risk vulnerability exploits.
idea_spark.risk: State synchronization accuracy of digital twins may be insufficient, leading to crash risks during actual execution.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by enterprise platforms' avoidance of DoS risks, designing adaptive safety mechanisms to balance attack capability and system stability.

papers.title: Voice Under Revision: Large Language Models and the Normalization of Personal Narrative
papers.summary: This study examines how large language model rewriting alters the style and narrative texture of personal narratives. It analyzes 300 personal narratives rewritten by three frontier LLMs under three prompt conditions: generic improvement, rewrite-only, and voice-preserving revision. Change is measured across 13 linguistic markers drawn from computational stylistics, including function words, vocabulary diversity, word length, punctuation, contractions, first-person pronouns, and emotion words.   Across models and prompt conditions, LLM rewriting produces a consistent pattern of stylistic normalization. Function words, contractions, and first-person pronouns decrease, while vocabulary diversity, word length, and punctuation elaboration increase. These shifts occur whether the prompt asks the model to "improve" the text or simply to "rewrite" it. Voice-preserving prompts reduce the magnitude of the changes but do not eliminate their direction. Stylometric analysis shows that rewritten texts converge in feature space and become harder to match back to their source texts. Additional narrative markers indicate a shift from embedded to distanced narration, and from explicit causal reasoning to compressed abstraction.   The findings suggest that contemporary LLMs exert a directional pull toward a more polished, less situated register. This has consequences for digital humanities and computational text analysis, where features such as function words, pronouns, contractions, and punctuation often serve as evidence for style, voice, authorship, and corpus integrity. LLM revision should therefore be understood not merely as surface-level editing, but as a consequential form of textual mediation.

ai.tldr: 研究显示，大型语言模型改写个人叙事时，会系统性地导致风格归一化，即使使用保留声音的提示也无法完全避免。
ai.motivation: 探究LLM改写对个人叙事风格和叙事纹理的影响，特别是其是否导致风格归一化。
ai.method: 分析300篇由三个前沿LLM在三种提示条件下改写的个人叙事，测量13个计算文体学标记的变化。
ai.result: LLM改写导致功能词、缩略词和第一人称代词减少，词汇多样性、词长和标点符号增加；改写文本在特征空间中趋同，难以匹配回源文本；叙事从嵌入转向疏离，从显性因果推理转向压缩抽象。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解LLM改写对文本风格和作者声音的潜在影响，提醒在数字人文和计算文本分析中注意这种中介作用。

idea_spark.idea: 开发一种LLM改写检测工具，基于文体特征变化识别文本是否经过LLM改写。
idea_spark.risk: 过度依赖文体特征可能导致误判，尤其是当改写提示精心设计时。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发设计更精细的提示策略以保留作者声音，或探索对抗性改写方法以规避检测。

en.tldr: The study shows that large language model rewriting of personal narratives systematically leads to stylistic normalization, even with voice-preserving prompts.
en.motivation: To investigate how LLM rewriting alters the style and narrative texture of personal narratives, particularly whether it causes stylistic normalization.
en.method: Analyzed 300 personal narratives rewritten by three frontier LLMs under three prompt conditions, measuring changes across 13 computational stylistics markers.
en.result: LLM rewriting decreases function words, contractions, and first-person pronouns, while increasing vocabulary diversity, word length, and punctuation; rewritten texts converge in feature space and become harder to match to source texts; narrative shifts from embedded to distanced, and from explicit causal reasoning to compressed abstraction.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand the potential impact of LLM rewriting on text style and authorial voice, alerting to this mediation in digital humanities and computational text analysis.

idea_spark.idea: Develop a tool to detect LLM-rewritten text based on changes in stylistic features.
idea_spark.risk: Over-reliance on stylistic features may lead to misclassification, especially when rewriting prompts are carefully designed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires designing more sophisticated prompting strategies to preserve authorial voice, or exploring adversarial rewriting methods to evade detection.
